title: Copilot Free, BYOK model no longer can access tools?
body: I use Ollama with Copilot and previously had no issues using BYOK models. However, I recently decided to cancel my GitHub Copilot subscription, and it transitioned to the Free tier today. Since this change, my Ollama model can no longer use any tools, including the cant even edit files. Why is this happening? Or it NOT possible use use even BYOK without subscription, is that it? - VS Code Version: 1.123.0 - OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04 - Selected model: Ollama (any model) Steps to Reproduce: 1. use Free version of Copilot 2. Ask Haiku to use #askQuestions tool and compare with Ollama (or probably other BYOK model) 3. Haiku can use all tools but my Ollama model no longer can able use any tool Before anyone asks, let me remind you: I had no issues before, and nothing changedŌĆösame modelŌĆöbut after the free transition, I can no longer use the tool. Haiku: Ollama Kimi-k2.6:cloud (or any other models)

title: Unsaved image preview tab can't be closed (and is buggy)
body:
Type: Bug 1. Create a new unsaved file 2. Paste any text, or a valid SVG, into the file (I used godotengine/godot/tree/master/editor/icons/Enum.svg) 3. Right-click the tab and select "Reopen Editor With..." 4. Select "Image Preview" 5. Ensure the file isn't saved; the preview should say an error occurred 6. Try to close the tab (and select one of the saving options) Note: It didn't matter which button I clicked. All three of "Save", "Don't save", and "Cancel" had the same result. After creating this file, and clicking "Open file using VS Code's standard text/binary editor?", the tab's icon was changed to the default language icon, and the tab's name was changed to Untitled-1. I created a new text tab, which was also named Untitled-1, and it appeared that the bugged image preview tab copied the title and icon of the newly created text tab, but only if the tab wasn't currently visible (i.e. with split screen, the title doesn't update until I focus a different tab on that screen). Close All Editors doesn't work either. Closing and reopening the window causes the unsaved image previews to be shown as saved (despite still being Untitled) and in this state they can be closed normally. <!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version:  1.123.0
- OS Version:  Kubuntu 26.04 LTS

After updating VS Code, the integrated terminal freezes when pasting text copied from LibreOffice.

The paste target in LibreOffice was plain text, but the issue still occurs.
If the text contains line breaks, the freeze is 100% reproducible.
If I paste the same content into Kate and copy it again, VS Code no longer freezes.
Some single-line text copied from LibreOffice can also trigger the issue.

This only happens in the integrated terminal, not necessarily in the editor.

title: [Bug] TypeError: h is not a function opening remote files after upgrading VS Code to 1.123, works fine on 1.122 with same SSH FS v1.26.1
body:

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.123
- OS Version:  windows11

Steps to Reproduce:

1. SSH FS v1.26.1 works perfectly on VS Code 1.122, no errors when browsing or opening remote SFTP files.
After upgrading VS Code to 1.123, I can browse remote directory list normally, but opening any remote file throws runtime error TypeError: h is not a function.
Full error stack:
TypeError: h is not a function
	at D (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\14f8.extension.js:1:145959)
	at SFTP.open (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\14f8.extension.js:1:167395)
	at se.open (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\14f8.extension.js:1:161216)
	at new se (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\14f8.extension.js:1:160219)
	at SFTP.createReadStream (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\14f8.extension.js:1:166937)
	at c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\f1d0.extension.js:1:2898
	at new Promise (<anonymous>)
	at g.readFile (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\f1d0.extension.js:1:2859)
	at c.readFile (c:\Users\wjjoo\.vscode\extensions\kelvin.vscode-sshfs-1.26.1\dist\extension.js:1:53039)
No changes to SSH FS version (still fixed v1.26.1), no changes to remote Ubuntu(Raspberry Pi CM5) server or SFTP connection config.
Steps to reproduce
Keep SSH FS fixed at v1.26.1
VS Code 1.122: connect SFTP to remote Ubuntu, open any remote file ŌåÆ success
Upgrade VS Code to 1.123, keep extension unchanged
Reuse identical existing SFTP connection: view folder tree OK, double-click any file ŌåÆ crash with above TypeError
Expected behavior
Remote file opens normally on VS Code 1.123 just like on 1.122.
Environment
OS: Windows
VS Code: 1.123 (broken) / 1.122 (working)
Extension: SSH FS v1.26.1
Remote OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (Raspberry Pi CM5)
Additional info
Downgrade VS Code back to 1.122 resolves the issue instantly; problem is purely compatibility break between this extension and VS Code 1.123 new Electron/Node runtime.

title: Allow hiding upgrade-only Copilot models from the model picker
body: ## Feature request The Copilot model picker shows upgrade-only models directly in the model list, for example: - Claude Opus 4.8 - Upgrade - GPT-5.5 - Upgrade This looks like a promotional section inside the picker and adds noise for users who only want to see models they can actually use. ## Expected behavior Please provide a way to hide upgrade-only or otherwise unavailable Copilot models from the model picker. Ideally this could be controlled from "Manage Language Models" using the existing visibility controls, or via a setting. ## Actual behavior Upgrade-only models are shown in the picker with an "Upgrade" action, even though they are not usable on the current plan. ## Why this matters The model picker should be focused on choosing an available model, not showing upgrade prompts. Related: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/272991 ** **

title: Notebook Generate ignores extension-contributed chatInstructions
body:
Type: Bug ## Description The notebook cell "Generate" action does not include applicable extension-contributed `chatInstructions` in the prompt sent to the selected language model. This creates inconsistent behavior between regular Chat and notebook Generate. It particularly affects extensions that provide custom notebook types or languages for which the model requires language-specific guidance. ## Steps to reproduce 1. Create a VS Code extension that contributes: - a custom language - a custom notebook type whose code cells use that language - a `chatInstructions` resource applicable when `notebookType` or `editorLangId` matches 2. Open a notebook using that notebook type. 3. Add or select a code cell. 4. Invoke the notebook cell "Generate" action. 5. Inspect the generated model request using "Show Chat Debug View" or a local model endpoint. ## Expected behavior The generated request includes the applicable contributed instructions, just as other contextual Chat requests do. ## Actual behavior The contributed instruction file is absent. Notebook Generate includes generic notebook context and may include agent instructions such as `AGENTS.md`, but it does not resolve applicable `chatInstructions`. ## Impact Custom language and notebook extensions cannot reliably supply the syntax, semantics, and conventions needed to generate valid cells. The available workaround is to add an entry to: `github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.instructions` That setting is deprecated, requires modifying user or workspace settings, and does not provide a suitable extension integration mechanism. ## Suggested resolution Either: 1. Resolve and include applicable contextual `chatInstructions` when building notebook Generate prompts, or 2. Expose a supported extension API/contribution point for notebook generation instructions. ## Environment VS Code version: Code 1.123.0 (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8, 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.114.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2

title: Copilot Chat gets stuck indefinitely after a few file creation/editing requests
body: ## Copilot Chat gets stuck indefinitely after a few file creation/editing requests After updating VS Code to version **1.123.0**, I started experiencing an issue where Copilot Chat gets stuck indefinitely during file creation/editing workflows. The issue does not always happen immediately. If I start a new chat session, Copilot may be able to create or edit files correctly for a short time. However, after a couple of requests in the same chat, it eventually gets stuck in an ŌĆ£in progress / running / thinkingŌĆØ state and never completes the action. In the latest case, Copilot checked whether a file existed in the workspace before deciding whether it should create or update it. After determining that the file did not exist, it remained stuck indefinitely and never created the file. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open VS Code version `1.123.0`. 2. Open a project/workspace. 3. Open Copilot Chat. 4. Start a new chat session. 5. Ask Copilot to create or edit a file. 6. Confirm that the first request may complete successfully. 7. Continue making a couple of file-related requests in the same chat. 8. Ask Copilot to create a file that does not exist yet. 9. Copilot checks whether the file exists. 10. After confirming that the file does not exist, Copilot remains stuck indefinitely in an ŌĆ£in progress / running / thinkingŌĆØ state. ### Actual Behavior - Starting a new chat temporarily allows Copilot to create or edit files again. - After a few requests in the same chat, Copilot gets stuck indefinitely. - The issue appears to happen during file creation/editing workflows. - In the latest case, Copilot checked whether a file existed before deciding whether to create or update it. - After determining that the file did not exist, it remained ŌĆ£in progressŌĆØ indefinitely and never created the file. - The only temporary workaround I found is starting a new chat, but the issue eventually happens again. ### Additional Context I also noticed that the previous Copilot Chat modes/options such as **Ask** and **Plan** are no longer visible in the UI after the update. I am not sure whether this is related to the file creation/editing issue or whether it is an intentional UI/product change, but I am mentioning it because both changes appeared after updating VS Code. The main issue I am reporting is that Copilot Chat gets stuck indefinitely after a few requests in the same chat session, especially when checking whether a file exists before deciding whether to create or edit it. ### Environment - VS Code version: `1.123.0 (user setup)` - Commit: `6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8` - Date: `2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00` - Electron: `42.2.0` - ElectronBuildId: `14159160` - Chromium: `148.0.7778.97` - Node.js: `24.15.0` - V8: `14.8.178.14-electron.0` - OS: `Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200` - Workspace type: - GitHub Copilot extension version: - GitHub Copilot Chat extension version: ### Notes This does not seem to fail immediately in every new chat session. A new chat can temporarily restore file-writing behavior, but the problem returns after a few interactions. I have not been able to confirm whether this is caused by the VS Code update, the Copilot extension, the chat session state, or the recent chat/agent UI changes.

title: Panel-specific zoom controls for chat and other sidebar views
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
## Description
Currently, Ctrl+/Ctrl- applies zoom to the entire VS Code window, affecting all panels including the editor, explorer, and chat panel simultaneously. This makes it difficult to focus on specific panels without affecting the visibility of code or other content.

## Desired Behavior
Add the ability to zoom in/out on individual panels (such as the Copilot Chat panel, Explorer, or other sidebar views) independently without affecting other parts of the interface.

## Use Case
When working with the AI agent chat, I often need to zoom in to better read responses without affecting the code editor zoom level or other panels. Currently, as a workaround, I have to:
- Resize the entire window, which changes all panel zoom levels.
- Manually adjust individual font size settings in VS Code settings.

## Proposed Solution
Allow right-click context menu or keyboard shortcuts (e.g., Alt+Ctrl+/Ctrl-) to zoom specific panels independently, or add a zoom control button on each panel header.

title: Built-in agents silently burn copilot credits ŌĆö user-level .agent.md overrides are ignored
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
### Summary

Built-in Copilot Chat agents (like `Explore`) ship with a hardcoded `model` field in their `.agent.md` file under the extension's `globalStorage`. Editing that file to use a BYOK or non-Copilot model is silently undone on every VS Code restart or after a set timeframe (difficult to confirm). There is no supported way to override the model on a built-in agent.

This is a **cost issue for BYOK users** under usage-based billing ŌĆö every subagent invocation burns Copilot credits even when the user has their own provider configured and is using a BYOK model as their primarily selected model.

### Proposed Solutions

**Option A ŌĆö VS Code setting**: Introduce a user setting (e.g. `github.copilot.chat.agentModelOverrides`) that lets users map built-in agent names to a specific model. This gives explicit control without relying on file-system precedence rules.

**Option B ŌĆö Drop hardcoded `model` from built-in agent files (preferred)**: Remove the `model:` frontmatter from built-in `.agent.md` files like `Explore.agent.md`. Without it, the agent falls back to the user's currently selected default model in the picker. Users who want a specific model can still pin one by creating a user-level `.agent.md` override in `%APPDATA%\Code\User\prompts\`. This is simpler, respects user defaults, and doesn't require a new setting.

<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->

## Summary
VS Code running on Windows using Remote-SSH to connect to a Linux code workspace with `.mcp.json`, causes MCP servers to error on start with `Connection state: Error spawn /path/to/mcp-server ENOENT`. Opening the same code workspace in VS Code running on Linux loads the same `.mcp.json` and MCP servers without issues.

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (verified at commit `c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de`)
- OS Version: Windows 11 client ŌåÆ Linux remote (Remote-SSH)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. From a **Windows** VS Code client, connect via Remote-SSH to a Linux host.
2. Create `.mcp.json` at the workspace root with any stdio server using an absolute remote command path:

   ```json
   {
     "mcpServers": {
       "echo": { "type": "stdio", "command": "/bin/echo", "args": ["hello"] }
     }
   }
   ```
3. Trust the server. Run `MCP: List Servers` ŌåÆ `echo` ŌåÆ `Show Output`.

**Expected:** Server spawns successfully. `/bin/echo` is an absolute, valid Linux path.

**Actual:** Server fails with `Connection state: Error spawn /bin/echo ENOENT`.

The same definition placed in `.vscode/mcp.json` (top-level key `"servers"` instead of `"mcpServers"`) starts cleanly. Opening the same code workspace directly in Linux also works.

## Failing log

```
2026-06-05 12:46:22.270 [info] Starting server my-mcp-server
2026-06-05 12:46:22.270 [info] Connection state: Starting
2026-06-05 12:46:22.270 [info] Starting server from Remote extension host
2026-06-05 12:46:22.365 [info] Connection state: Starting
2026-06-05 12:46:22.365 [info] Connection state: Error spawn /path/to/mcp-server ENOENT
```

The path to `/path/to/mcp-server` is valid and can be found in the integrated terminal. The `Starting server from Remote extension host` line confirms the spawn happens on the Linux remote.

## Identified root cause

The `.mcp.json` discovery adapter ([`claudeConfigToServerDefinition`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de/src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/discovery/nativeMcpDiscoveryAdapters.ts#L51)) computes `cwd: cwd?.fsPath` on the **workbench** side. On a Windows client, [`URI.fsPath`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de/src/vs/base/common/uri.ts#L644-L651) flips forward slashes to backslashes based on the **client** OS, producing a string like `\opt\example\workspace`. That value is shipped to the Linux ext host and used as `cwd` in [`spawn()`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de/src/vs/workbench/api/node/extHostMcpNode.ts#L87-L100). The directory doesn't exist on Linux, the child's `chdir()` fails before `execve`, and Node reports the error as `ENOENT` against the *command*.

`.vscode/mcp.json` doesn't hit this because [`installedMcpServersDiscovery.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de/src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/discovery/installedMcpServersDiscovery.ts#L88-L116) propagates the workspace folder as a `URI` via `variableReplacement.folder` - `fsPath` is then evaluated on the Linux ext host where slashes stay forward.

## Workaround

Move the contents of `.mcp.json` into `.vscode/mcp.json` and rename the top-level key from `"mcpServers"` to `"servers"`.

## Notes

- `.cursor/mcp.json` ([`CursorWorkspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/c83b87ffb27e2fe0cf7a7f358d48b2edb3e797de/src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/common/discovery/workspaceMcpDiscoveryAdapter.ts#L71)) uses the same adapter and is likely also affected.

title: Agents: Improve how we render many editor tabs
body: When there are many editor tabs a ton of weird looking empty spaces show up due to the allocated close icon space we give to tabs. We need a design pass on how we treat these scenarios. Potential solutions: 1. Add horizontal scrolling to tab container 2. Update tab rendering so that instead of having the close icon render next to tab labels we render the icon on top of the label (still anchored to the right) when hover styling is applied.

title: agents -- cut off menu entry border on workspace picker
Show token cost metadata for BYOK models
body: IŌĆÖd like VS Code to support displaying cost information and possibly other additional metadata for Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) models in the model management UI. (chatModelsWidget and beyond) For teams using multiple BYOK models, it would be helpful for developers to see model costs directly in the editor when choosing a model, instead of checking external pricing docs. Requested fields: - `inputCost` - `outputCost` - `cacheCost` For example: GPT-5.5 Global via Azure's OpenAI service is priced (USD) at inputCost of 5, outputCost of 30, and cacheCost of 0.5 (per 1M tokens) for non-priority processing. These could be shown when provided, similar to how other model metadata such as context size and capabilities are displayed today. This would help teams make more informed model choices and better understand the cost impact of different BYOK models from within VS Code.

title: Tools without `inputSchema` send requests to OpenAI-compatible endpoints with `parameters` field omitted
body:
**Copilot Chat Extension version**: v0.51.0
**VS Code version**: 1.123.0 (commit: 6a44c352bd)
**OS**: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
**Feature**: Agent mode / BYOK (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
**Model**: LM Studio (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)

---

### Background

When using LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible endpoint, Agent mode failed with the following error for many tools:

```
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: e10ac910-5cb9-46ad-942b-e759cf9ba637

Reason: Request Failed: 400 { "error": { "message": "Invalid input", "type": "invalid_request_error", "param": "tools.51", "code": "custom" } }
```

The `tools.51` parameter index indicates the 51st tool definition is malformed. Investigating the source revealed that many tool definitions are being sent without a `parameters` field.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Start an OpenAI-compatible endpoint in LM Studio
2. Configure Copilot BYOK to use the endpoint
3. Start a chat in Agent mode
4. 400 error occurs

### Problem

Many tools including `terminal_selection` and `terminal_last_command` are sent to OpenAI-compatible endpoints with the `parameters` field omitted entirely.

Per the [[OpenAI Function Calling specification](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling)](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling), `parameters` is a required field. Even when a function takes no parameters, the following form is required:

```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {},
  "required": [],
  "additionalProperties": false
}
```

This was also observed in #283518, where the error log showed tool definitions without `parameters`:

```json
{"type":"function","function":{"name":"activate_python_environment_management","description":"..."}}
```

### Root Cause

`IToolData.inputSchema` is optional (`readonly inputSchema?: IJSONSchema`), but the conversion layer does not fall back correctly.

**`toolCallingLoop.ts`** ŌĆö passes `inputSchema` as-is, leaving it `undefined`:
```typescript
parameters: toolInfo.inputSchema,
```

**`inlineChatIntent.ts` and others** ŌĆö falls back to `undefined` (omitted) instead of `{ type: 'object', properties: {} }`:
```typescript
parameters: tool.inputSchema && Object.keys(tool.inputSchema).length
    ? tool.inputSchema
    : undefined  // ŌåÉ omitted from JSON
```

### Affected Files

Running `tsc` with `inputSchema` made required reveals the following production code missing the field (under `src/`):

| File |
|---|
| `getTerminalSelectionTool.ts` |
| `getTerminalLastCommandTool.ts` |
| `editFileTool.ts` |
| `chatModel.ts` |
| `stateToProgressAdapter.ts` |

Including tests and fixtures, approximately 115 locations in total.

### Suggested Fix

**Short-term**: Use `inputSchema ?? { type: 'object', properties: {} }` at each conversion site.

**Long-term**: Make `IToolData.inputSchema` required to catch missing definitions at compile time. However, since extensions contributed via `package.json` also use `IToolData`, a naive required change would be a breaking change for existing extensions. A separate type for internal tools or a fallback in the contribution-to-`IToolData` conversion layer would be needed.

I verified the scope of the change via `tsc`, but given the backward-compatibility implications for external extensions, I'd like to leave the design decision to the maintainers.

title: Show credit optimization tips for users
body:
We want to provide actionable and helpful tips to users to help them get the most of their allocated AICs. We currently notify users when they hit certain usage checkpoints (50/75/90) and prompt them towards their continuation options. We should surface actions they could take to make the most of their remaining usage or use the tips infra to surface actionable suggestions based on their usage / configuration. 

- Reduce reasoning effort (if high or x-high and it is not the default)
- Switch to Auto (10% discount) 
- Remove unused tools

Similar ideas to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/315079 and https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319750

title: Handlebars syntax highlighter does not recognize end of comment when using whitespace control (~) before closing braces
body:
### Bug Description
In Handlebars templates, VSCode's syntax highlighting fails to properly recognize the end of a comment block when a whitespace control character (`~`) is used just before the closing delimiter. As a result, the line following the comment is incorrectly highlighted as if it were still part of the comment.

The following snippet demonstrates the bug:

```handlebars
    {{!-- This is a comment --~}}
    But this is not
```

The second line, `But this is not`, is rendered as a comment, but it should not be.

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Paste the following Handlebars code into a .hbs file in VSCode:
   ```handlebars
   {{!-- This is a comment --~}}
   But this is not
   ```
2. Observe the syntax highlighting of the second line.

**Expected:**
- Only the first line should be highlighted as a comment.
- The second line should be highlighted as normal Handlebars content.

**Actual:**
- The second line is improperly highlighted as a comment.

**Notes:**
- This issue occurs with the built-in Handlebars syntax highlighting in the latest VSCode version.
- The bug seems to be specific to comments that use the Handlebars whitespace control character (`~`) before the closing braces.
- Extensions disabled: Yes (still reproduces)
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: MacOS 26.5

title: "Session will stop" notification is not correct for agent host
body:
VS Code shows the "session will stop" notification for the agent host when closing a window, but this isn't quite right for the agent host lifecycle.

- Quitting vscode entirely- local AH sessions will stop, remote ones will not
- Closing a vscode window- local AH sessions will not stop

We might also think through whether it's confusing that a local AH session can keep running even when no window is open with that session. But the session may be open on a remote.

title: Agents web app connect to machine button needs improved visual feedback
body: On vscode.dev/agents, we need to always show some kind of visual feedback when clicking the connect to a machine icon button in the very bottom left corner. It very inconsistently renders a notification that it's attempting to connect to a machine ŌĆōŌĆō most of the time just looks/feels like a broken button where you click and nothing happens.

title: AHP + Agents: Move radio tower icon out of session chat panel
body: We should move the radio tower icon to a more "global" position outside of any individual chat session. Currently having the radio tower icon be shown on every chat session is confusing. It implies that each chat session you want to be synced across devices needs to be individually toggled "on" to be seen/synced.

title: Copilot Chat fails to process images with third-party OpenAI-compatible models (DeepSeek V4): unknown variant image_url, expected text
body:
Type: Bug When using a third-party OpenAI-compatible model (DeepSeek V4) in Copilot Chat, pasting an image causes a 400 error. The same image works fine with built-in models like GPT-4o. Steps to reproduce: Add DeepSeek V4 as a third-party model in Copilot Chat Select it as the active model Paste an image into the chat and send Expected: Model responds based on image content (DeepSeek V4 supports vision via OpenAI standard format) Actual: Error - "unknown variant image_url, expected text" Root cause: Copilot sends images in its internal proprietary format instead of converting to the standard OpenAI-compatible {type: "image_url"} format that third-party models expect. Workaround: Use built-in models for images, or manually switch models. Please add a format conversion layer for third-party models to translate Copilot's internal image format to OpenAI standard. title: Issue reporter wizard drops preset extension "data" when opened with extensionId
body:
## Issue

When the issue reporter is opened programmatically via the `workbench.action.openIssueReporter` command with both an `extensionId` and `data` (the optional extension-data string), the `data` is silently dropped in the new wizard-based issue reporter (`IssueReporterOverlay`).

The old reporter (`baseIssueReporterService.updateExtensionStatus`) propagated `data.data` into the model's `extensionData` automatically when the reporter was opened with a preset extension; the new wizard does not, so attached context never makes it into the final issue body.

### Repro

Run from any extension:

```ts
vscode.commands.executeCommand('workbench.action.openIssueReporter', {
    extensionId: 'GitHub.copilot-chat',
    issueTitle: '',
    issueBody: '# Description\n...',
    data: '<details><summary>STest</summary>...attached context...</details>',
});
```

Expected: the `data` payload appears as extension data in the rendered issue body.
Actual: `data` is absent from the issue body. Only `issueTitle` / `issueBody` survive.

### Concrete impact

Copilot's NES "Report feedback" command (`InlineEditDebugComponent` in vscode-copilot) attaches the STest, recording, and UI repro via this `data` field. With the new reporter, all of that context is lost from the issue body.

### Root cause

In `src/vs/workbench/contrib/issue/browser/issueReporterOverlay.ts`, `createWizard()` calls:

```ts
if (this.data.extensionId) {
    void this.updateSelectedExtension(this.data.extensionId, /* loadExtensionData */ false);
}
```

With `loadExtensionData=false`, `applyExtensionIssueData` is never invoked, so the preset `data.data` / `data.uri` / `data.privateUri` are never merged into the selected extension or into the model (`extensionData` + `includeExtensionData: true`).

cc @Giuspepe

title: [accessibility] screen reader cannot announce results while navigating between results in search box
body:
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (system setup)
- OS Version: windows 11 pRO

Steps to Reproduce:

1. NVDA is opened.
2. A project is opened with VSCode.
3. Search box is opened with CTRL + Shift + F and a text known to be in the project is entered into the search box.
4. To reach results, tab is pressed until the treeview showing results is reached.
5. When we focus on the treeview, NVDA tells us, for example: 48 results in 4 files - Search: foot
6. {regression} User navigates between results with arrow keys but NVDA cannot announce the search results, moreover even in object navigation (NVDA + num4/num6 etc.) these cannot be captured by NVDA.
  NOTE: navigation works correctly but NVDA seems unable to access the objects here.

findings:

This regression probably occurred in version 123.0. There was no such problem in the previous version.

title: scm.autoReveal does not reveal/select staged (index) files in the Source Control view
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.120.0 (arm64, macOS)
- `scm.autoReveal`: `true` (default)

### Description

`scm.autoReveal` is documented as *"Controls whether the Source Control view should automatically reveal and select files when opening them."* It works as expected for **working-tree (unstaged)** changes, but **silently does nothing for staged (index) changes** ŌĆö making a staged file's diff the active editor does not reveal or select its row in the Source Control view.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. In a repo, modify a file and **stage** it (so it appears under *Staged Changes*).
2. Modify and stage a second file too.
3. Open the first staged file's diff, then switch the active editor to the second staged file's diff *without clicking the row in the SCM view* (e.g. via a command/keybinding such as `workbench.action.nextEditor`).

**Expected:** The Source Control view reveals and selects the row of the now-active staged file (same as it does for working-tree files).

**Actual:** Nothing is revealed/selected for staged files; the selection stays on whatever was last clicked. Working-tree (unstaged) files reveal correctly under the same conditions.

### Root cause (from source)

In `scmViewPane.ts`, `onDidActiveEditorChange()` resolves the active editor with `EditorResourceAccessor.getOriginalUri(activeEditor, { supportSideBySide: SideBySideEditor.PRIMARY })` and matches Source Control resources by `sourceUri`:

```ts
const resource = this.viewMode === ViewMode.Tree
    ? groupItem.resourceTree.getNode(uri)?.element
    : groupItem.resources.find(r => this.uriIdentityService.extUri.isEqual(r.sourceUri, uri));
```

For a **staged** diff, the modified (primary) side is a `git:`-scheme URI (`toGitUri(uri, '')` per `getRightResource` in `extensions/git/src/repository.ts`), so `getOriginalUri` returns a `git:` URI. But every SCM resource's `sourceUri` is the on-disk `file:` URI, so `isEqual` never matches and the reveal is skipped.

For a **working-tree** diff the modified side is the plain `file:` URI, which matches `sourceUri` ŌĆö hence auto-reveal works there but not for staged files.

### Suggested fix

In the auto-reveal match, normalize a `git:`-scheme active-editor URI back to its underlying `file:` path (the path is carried in the git URI's query) before comparing against `sourceUri` ŌĆö or match on the canonical path rather than the raw URI.

### Related

- #170819 (*SCM - Reveal Active File in Git View*) is a related feature request for a *manual* reveal command; this issue is specifically that the **existing** `scm.autoReveal` is inconsistent between staged and unstaged files.

title: Terminal: TUI input box renders corrupted when mixing ASCII and CJK characters since 1.123.0 (local macOS + Remote SSH ŌåÆ Ubuntu)
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (darwin arm64)
- OS Version: macOS (Apple Silicon); also reproduced via Remote SSH ŌåÆ Ubuntu

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the VS Code integrated terminal (local **or** via Remote SSH to an Ubuntu host)
2. Run a TUI app with a readline-based input box (e.g. `gh copilot suggest`, `claude`, or any readline TUI)
3. In the input box, type a mix of ASCII characters / half-width punctuation and Chinese (CJK) characters
4. Observe rendering corruption

## Expected behavior

Input renders correctly; every character appears at the right column position. Copying selected text gives correct UTF-8 output.

## Actual behavior

After mixing English letters, half-width punctuation, or a line wrap with Chinese characters, **one character appears duplicated or offset** in the input box. One character is misplaced/overwritten each time the buffer redraws.

Example: Typed `likeĶ┐ÖµĀĘŃĆé.Ķ┐ÖµĀĘŃĆé` but the terminal renders it as `like µĀĘµĀĘŃĆé.µĀĘŃĆé` ŌĆö one character is dropped/overwritten.

Copying the selected text yields garbled mojibake output such as:
```
├ó copilot cli ├©┬Š├ź┬ź├”┬Ī├”┬Ė┬▓├”├ż┬╝┬╝├ż┬╣├”├®┬«├®┬ó
```
(UTF-8 bytes interpreted as Latin-1)

## Environment details

- **Regression**: works correctly in 1.122, broken in 1.123.0
- Reproducible in **two** scenarios:
  1. Local macOS integrated terminal
  2. macOS VS Code ŌåÆ **Remote SSH ŌåÆ Ubuntu** (same corruption, so it is not macOS-specific)
- Does **not** reproduce in iTerm2 or Terminal.app on the same machine with the same TUI app
- Setting `"terminal.integrated.unicodeVersion": "11" or "6"` does **not** resolve the issue

## Root cause hypothesis

The Electron 42 / Chromium 148 upgrade in 1.123.0 changed xterm.js's East Asian Width (wcwidth) table or Unicode version default. This causes a column-width mismatch between xterm.js and the TUI app's own wcwidth library:
- The TUI writes at column N, but xterm.js places the cursor at column N┬▒1
- Subsequent redraws overwrite the wrong cell, causing one CJK character to be dropped or duplicated

The clipboard corruption (copy ŌåÆ mojibake) is a related but separate regression tracked in #319821 (OSC 52 `atob()` vs `TextDecoder` regression in xterm.js: xtermjs/xterm.js#6000).

title: Agent Feedback Feature for AHP for Agents Window
body:
### Overview
We propose to implement a commenting feature in the agents window that integrates with the agent host protocol. This feature will enhance collaboration and communication between users and agents by allowing users to comment on selected code snippets.

### Requirements
- **User Interaction**: Users should be able to select code, write comments about it, and submit these comments to the agent for further action.
- **Review Agent Functionality**: A designated review agent should have the ability to create comments that users can view, review, delete, and respond to. This will facilitate a discussion around the code.
- **Agent Collaboration**: The system should allow one agent to create comments while another agent tackles them, promoting an orchestrated workflow.
- **Server-Side State Management**: The state of comments should be maintained server-side to ensure functionality in remote AHP setups, where no client is connected. This will allow users to see comments across different clients and devices.

### Golden Scenario

A typical end-to-end scenario would look like this:

1. A user implements a feature in an initial chat (Chat 1).
2. The user starts a review agent in a separate chat, but within the same session.
3. The review agent analyses the code and adds comments independently, potentially continuing its work even while the client is disconnected from the server.
4. When the user returns, they can:
  - Browse all generated comments
  - Discard comments they donŌĆÖt find relevant
  - Instruct an agent to address selected comments
5. Another agent (or the same one) can then tackle the feedback, creating an iterative workflow across chats.

This scenario enables asynchronous, multi-agent collaboration while keeping the user in control of what feedback is applied.

title: "Open New External Terminal" does not correctly work with GNOME's default terminal emulator `ptyxis` . Fails to open window  that is navigated to the directory
body: ### Summary I am using Ubuntu 26.04 and it seems Ubuntu is now using ptyxis as its default terminal emulator. In Visual Studio Code it is possible to set the setting ``` "terminal.explorerKind": "external", // use external terminal "terminal.external.linuxExec": "ptyxis", // determines which, default is system terminal emulator ``` and this allows users to right-click on a directory in VSC's file exlplorer and open it in their terminal emulator. By that I mean that a **new** terminal emulator window appears and is **navigated to the clicked directory**. This works with gnome-terminal and a variety of other terminal emulators but not with ptyxis. ptyxis sends a system notification stating its ready but it neither creates a new window or tab nor navigates to the clicked directory. Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes ``` Version: 1.123.0 Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8 Date: 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00 Electron: 42.2.0 ElectronBuildId: 14159160 Chromium: 148.0.7778.97 Node.js: 24.15.0 V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0 OS: Linux x64 7.0.0-15-generic ``` ### Steps to Reproduce: 1. Install VS Code on a distro using GNOME, with `ptyxis` as the default terminal emulator 2. Set the following VS Code settings: ``` "terminal.explorerKind": "external", // use external terminal "terminal.external.linuxExec": "ptyxis", // determines which, default is system terminal emulator ``` this allows users to right-click on a directory in VSC's file exlplorer and open it in their terminal emulator. By that I mean that a new terminal emulator window appears and is navigated to the clicked directory 3.Right-click on a directory in the file explorer and click on "Open in External Terminal" ### Expected Behavior A `ptyxis` terminal window (or tab) with the shell having been navigated into that directory. E. g. if I open VS Code in a `/home/kg/code` and that directory contains a directory called `subdir1`, then clicking "Open In External Terminal" should create a `ptyxis` window (or at least tab) with `pwd` being `/home/kg/code/subdir1` ### Actual Behavior If there's no `ptyxis` window open, it starts `ptyxis` in the profile's default directory (`$HOME` in my case) If there is already one, then I get a GNOME notification saying: "Terminal" is ready. But nothing else happens. ### Troubleshooting attempts I tried passing -d in the "terminal.external.linuxExec" but that just results in errors in VS Code: ``` log.ts:460 ERR spawn 'ptyxis --new-window -d' ENOENT: Error: spawn 'ptyxis --new-window -d' ENOENT at ChildProcess._handle.onexit (node:internal/child_process:285:19) at onErrorNT (node:internal/child_process:483:16) at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:89:21) ``` According to the `ptyxis` [maintainer](https://gitlab.gnome.org/chergert/ptyxis/-/work_items/578), this is a configuration issue but I've yet to figure out what the required configuration would be and why it's not the default for GNOME systems. ### Actual Behavior If there's no `ptyxis` window open, it starts `ptyxis` in the profile's default directory (`$HOME` in my case) If there is already one, then I get a GNOME notification saying: "Terminal" is ready. But nothing else happens.

title: PerŌĆæSession AI Credit Budget for Copilot Agent Sessions
body:
## Motivation

With usageŌĆæbased billing, autonomous Copilot agent sessions can consume a large amount of AI credits without additional user intent.  
Today, there is no IDEŌĆælevel mechanism that bounds the **economic impact of a single chat or agent session**.

The goal of this proposal is **not** to limit autonomy, but to provide a **hard safety belt** that prevents catastrophic cost explosions (e.g. fourŌĆædigit invoices) caused by runaway or orbiting agents.

---

## Current Limitations

### 1. `Chat ŌĆ║ Agent: Max Requests` does not address cost safety

VS Code already exposes:

> **Chat ŌĆ║ Agent: Max Requests**  
> *The maximum number of requests to allow per turn when using an agent.*

This setting is **not sufficient** as a safety mechanism:

- It is **modelŌĆæagnostic**
  - 100 requests can differ by orders of magnitude in cost depending on the model used.
- It limits **requests per turn**, not **total session consumption**.
- Reducing it directly conflicts with the purpose of agent mode:
  - Agents are meant to run autonomously across many steps.
- It requires users to guess a ŌĆ£safeŌĆØ number without any cost signal.

This setting throttles **iteration shape**, not **economic impact**.

---

### 2. Monthly dollar caps do not prevent singleŌĆæsession disasters

UserŌĆælevel or organizationŌĆælevel spending caps (e.g. `$10`, `$50`, `$100`) are **accounting controls**, not execution controls:

- A single agent session can still:
  - Consume the entire remaining budget
  - Do so without intermediate user intent
- A `$10` cap does not solve the actual problem:
  > *Preventing a single agent session from accidentally generating a $1000 invoice.*
- These caps stop **future usage**, not the runaway session itself.

Monthly budgets act **after the damage is done**.

---

## Missing Capability

What is missing today is a **sessionŌĆæscoped execution contract** that says:

> ŌĆ£A specific Copilot agent session may spend at most **X** AI credits.
> When the limit is reached, execution must stop and require explicit user intent to continue.ŌĆØ

No existing setting or policy provides this guarantee.

---

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a **PerŌĆæSession AI Credit Budget** as a firstŌĆæclass IDE capability.

### Core Properties

| Property | Description |
|---|---|
| SessionŌĆæscoped | Applies to one chat or agent session |
| Hard stop | Execution pauses when the budget is exhausted |
| Explicit continuation | User must explicitly approve any continuation, then another X AI credits can be consumed |
| NonŌĆæbypassable | Agents cannot increase or reset the budget |

title: TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version rewrites js/ts.tsdk.path in .code-workspace incorrectly
body: Type: Bug In a multi-root VS Code workspace, I set: ```json "js/ts.tsdk.path": "..\\common\\node_modules\\typescript\\lib", "js/ts.tsdk.promptToUseWorkspaceVersion": true ``` When the workspace is reopened, VS Code prompts me to switch to the workspace TypeScript version and accepting that prompt works as expected. However, if I run `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version` manually and choose the workspace version, VS Code temporarily switches to it, but also rewrites the workspace setting to: ```json "js/ts.tsdk.path": "common\\node_modules\\typescript\\lib" ``` That removes the leading `..\\`, which breaks the path the next time the workspace is opened. **Steps to Reproduce** 1. Open a `.code-workspace` file. 2. Set `js/ts.tsdk.path` to a relative path that goes up one level, for example: `..\\common\\node_modules\\typescript\\lib` 3. Set `"js/ts.tsdk.promptToUseWorkspaceVersion": true` 4. Restart VS Code or reopen the workspace. 5. Accept the prompt to use the workspace TypeScript version. 6. Observe that the TypeScript version switches correctly and the `js/ts.tsdk.path` remains unmodified. 7. Run `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version`. 8. Choose `Use VS Code's Version` (which for me is 6.0.3). 9. Observe that the TypeScript version switches correctly to 6.0.3 and the `js/ts.tsdk.path` remains unmodified. 10. Run `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version`. 11. Choose the workspace version. 12. Observe that VS Code rewrites the setting and removes the leading `..\\`. **Expected Behavior** Selecting the workspace TypeScript version should not rewrite the configured js/ts.tsdk.path, or it should preserve the original relative path exactly. **Actual Behavior** The command rewrites the workspace setting and changes a valid relative path into an incorrect one. VS Code version: Code 1.123.0 (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8, 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Copilot Student Credits Exhaust Too Quickly After June 1 Billing Change
body: I am a student on the GitHub Education Plan. Before June 1, when using Copilot (chat or agent mode), my credits never got exhausted ŌĆö I could use it freely for learning and building projects. However, after the billing plan change effective June 1, my student credits are being consumed extremely fast. Even with very minimal usage (just 20ŌĆō30 requests in a day), the 200 credits limit gets exhausted immediately. This new system is harmful to students who rely on Copilot for education. The previous plan allowed us to learn without worrying about credits, but now the restriction makes Copilot nearly unusable for genuine study purposes. **Expected Behavior:** Student credits should be sufficient for daily learning and experimentation. The old system allowed sustainable usage without exhaustion. **Actual Behavior:** Credits are capped at 200 and get consumed almost instantly, making Copilot inaccessible for students. **Impact:** This change negatively affects students who depend on Copilot for learning, coding practice, and project development. **Request:** Please reconsider the new billing structure for students. Either restore the previous unlimited usage or increase the credit limit significantly so that students can continue to use Copilot as an educational tool.

}
Additional Information
The issue occurs only with a GPT-5-mini model deployed through Azure Foundry.
The issue does not occur with other models.
I did not explicitly configure temperature=0.1.

The request payload included:

{
  "thinking": true
}
Steps to Reproduce

Enable optimized tool selection.
Send a request in Agent mode.
The request fails with:
"Unsupported value: 'temperature' does not support 0.1 with this model"

title: Remote session/task persistence: Detach, preserve, and reattach all active tasks, terminals, and extension processes in VS Code Remote
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Feature Request: Universal Remote Session Persistence & Reattachment

When using VS Code Remote connections (SSH, WSL, Containers, etc.), I would like the ability to completely detach/disconnect my local VS Code client while preserving the entire remote sessionŌĆö**not only a Copilot agent or a single task, but all running content in the workspace**. This would include:

- All running integrated terminal processes
- All ongoing tasks (e.g., build, watch, test, custom tasks)
- Background/interactive processes started by extensions
- Debugger sessions and other live connections

After disconnecting (e.g., closing VS Code or losing network for a long period), when I reconnect to the same remote workspace, I would like VS Code to automatically restore all these elements. This means:

- ALL previously running terminals and tasks should remain alive in the background and be reattached, preserving their history and state
- Output/logs from before the disconnect should remain available and reviewable
- Extension-contributed terminal panels/processes/background work (if possible)
- Sessions like debugging, ports, tunnels, etc. should resume if possible
- UI/interaction state (open editors, layout, etc.) restored as far as possible

**In essence:**
VS Code should offer a ŌĆ£remote session persistence & resumeŌĆØ comparable to what tmux/screen provide for terminals, but extended to all VS Code capabilities.

### Why?
- Long-running builds, scripts, training jobs, remote debugging, etc., are often interrupted if the local client disconnects or crashes. Retaining state reduces rework and risk of losing context/logs.
- Some remote operations take hours, and forcibly tying them to the local VS Code client or network is limiting.
- Other tools (tmux, screen) provide process-level session persistence, but deeper editor/task/log/context state is unique to VS Code.

### Examples of actions I want to support:
- Run several tasks, tests, terminals, and disconnect my laptopŌĆöwhen I reconnect, everything is as I left it.
- Start a remote debug session, get interrupted (e.g., lose VPN), reconnect and resume as if nothing happened.
- Review logs or output for a job that completed/disconnected while I was away.

### Related
- [#318997](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/318997): For agent session persistence (Copilot) only, not arbitrary tasks or terminals
- Please link if there are other relevant issues

Thank you for considering! This would be a major productivity and reliability improvement for all remote VS Code users.

title: Git extension falsely detects repository in empty/new window after updating to latest version
body:
VS Code Version
Version: 1.123.0 (Universal)
Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8
Date: 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00 (2 days ago)
Electron: 42.2.0
ElectronBuildId: 14159160
Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
Node.js: 24.15.0
V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 24.6.0 (macOS Sequoia)

**Bug Description**
Immediately after upgrading to the latest VS Code release (v1.123.0), opening a completely new, blank window (via Cmd + N or File > New Window) causes the built-in Git extension to incorrectly initialize a Git repository
.
The Source Control panel activates and shows thousands of pending changes, scanning upward into a parent directory. Crucially, the File Explorer behaves correctly and remains completely empty (showing only the "You have not yet opened a folder" state), while the Source Control panel is independently stuck tracking a rogue root repository.

This decoupling of the Source Control state from the Explorer state did not occur in previous versions.

**Steps to Reproduce**
Update VS Code to version 1.123.0 and allow it to restart.
Open a brand new instance/window with no folder or workspace loaded (File > New Window).
Observe the Sidebar. The File Explorer correctly indicates no folder is open, but the Source Control panel activates anyway and begins tracking thousands of changes from a parent or home directory.

**Expected Behavior**
A newly opened, empty window with no active folder or workspace should not trigger Git repository detection. If the Explorer has no open workspace, the Source Control panel should remain idle.

**Actual Behavior**
The Git extension auto-detects a repository (likely from a legacy or root .git folder in a parent path) and initializes version control tracking in the Source Control panel exclusively, while the Explorer view remains completely un-focused and empty.

title: Regression: Docker Compose containers fail to stop on window close (shutdownAction ignored) in v1.123.0
body:
Type: Bug ### **Environment** * **VS Code Version:** 1.123.0 * **Dev Containers Extension Version:** 0.459.0 * **OS Version:** macOS (Darwin arm64) * **Docker Desktop Version:** 4.76.0 --- ### **Steps to Reproduce** 1. Open a project using a Dev Container configured with `docker-compose.yaml`. 2. Ensure `devcontainer.json` uses the `dockerComposeFile` property. 3. (Optional) Include an explicit `name:` field in the `docker-compose.yaml`. 4. Rebuild and enter the Dev Container. 5. Verify all services in the compose stack are running (e.g., via `docker ps` in an external terminal). 6. Close the VS Code window or Quit the application. 7. Check the container status again in the external terminal. --- ### **Expected Behavior** The containers should stop (or follow the defined `shutdownAction`). In previous versions (v1.122.0 and earlier), closing the window would stop the compose stack, allowing Docker Desktop's "Resource Saver" to pause the VM. ### **Actual Behavior** All containers in the compose stack remain in the `Up` state indefinitely after VS Code is closed. ### **Additional Context** * Explicitly setting `"shutdownAction": "stopCompose"` in `devcontainer.json` and rebuilding **does not** resolve the issue in v1.123.0. * **Downgrading VS Code to v1.122.0** immediately restores the expected behavior (containers stop on window close) using the exact same project configuration even without explicit `"shutdownAction": "stopCompose"` in `devcontainer.json`. * This issue prevents Docker Desktop for Mac from idling/pausing the VM, leading to high background resource usage. title: Credit Request - High spend with low usable output
body:
Type: Performance Issue I request a credit/refund. The poor system performance and malfunctioning model drift can't be stopped. The bias to code js with exposed business logic including sensitive data has been the challenge. I spent all day tuning the semantic clarity of the prompts and coding requirements. Models accepted the tasks, comprehended them, repeat them back and express their understanding - and they still drift off ignoring the intent, contract and responsibility. They even declared the assignment done following the acceptance criteria. This is not fair, ethical or honorable. Anybody offering a service should have the decency to tighten up their protocols to reduce the losses and exposed known problems with these models. Date: June 4, 2026 Issue: ~50% monthly allocation consumed, but output was non-usable and required repeated rework. Refactoring a simple modal form to follow architectural guides - light UI with minimal JS. Remove the risk of exposing data sources. Expectation: architecture-aligned GoldRibbon gate refactor. title: `gh` CLI (and other Go TLS) fail in agent sandbox
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: N/A

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
## Version Metadata
Version: 1.123.0 (Universal)
Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8
Date: 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00
Electron: 42.2.0
ElectronBuildId: 14159160
Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
Node.js: 24.15.0
V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.5.0

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Turn on Agent Sandbox `"chat.agent.sandbox.enabled": "on"`
2. Allow github.com access via `chat.agent.allowedNetworkDomains`
3. Ask an agent to run `gh` CLI to access Github

## Current Behaviour

```
$ gh api rate_limit
Get "https://api.github.com/rate_limit": tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: OSStatus -26276
```

## Root Cause

Go on macOS uses the Security framework directly (SecTrustSettingsCopyTrustSettings) to verify certificate trust, ignoring SSL_CERT_FILE. Without access to trustd.agent, Go programs cannot verify CA certificates. 

VSCode's Agent Sandbox inherits from https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime which have documented the issue [here](https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime/issues/118), and implemented a `enableWeakerNetworkIsolation` option in https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime/pull/120, disabled by default. 

VSCode does not expose or set `enableWeakerNetworkIsolation` for its sandbox. 

## Workaround

Set in the VSCode user settings:
```
"chat.agent.sandbox.advanced.runtime": {
    "enableWeakerNetworkIsolation": true
  },
```

## Expected Behaviour

`gh` CLI is a first-party product - it should work out of the box with VSCode on macOS. `enableWeakerNetworkIsolation` should either just be `true` by default (the rationale of data exfil via IPC to trustd seems exceedingly low), or there should be a workaround for sandboxed environments.

Note this also affects most Go-based tooling - so fixing only for `gh` CLI is suboptimal:
* gcloud
* terraform
* kubectl

## References

anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime:

title: More visibility into boundary between "short context cost" and "long context cost" in meter
body:
### Objective

GitHub Copilot chat needs visibility into the fullness level of the context window _in relation to the boundary between cost levels_, **for example a tick on a meter or multiple meters**.

### Explanation

In GitHub Copilot today many of the models now allow selection of context size. For example Claude Sonnet now allows two levels: **200K** and **1M**:

> ### Claude Sonnet 4.6
> Balanced Claude model offering strong performance for everyday coding and chat tasks at faster speeds.
> 
> #### Cost (per 1M tokens)
> Medium cost  
> Input: 300 credits  
> Cached input: 30 credits  
> Output: 1500 credits  
> 
> #### Long context cost (per 1M tokens)
> Input: 600 credits  
> Cached input: 60 credits  
> Output: 2250 credits
> 
> 
> #### Max context
> 1M

The specifics of how costs are allocated was explained in #319976. If I select "long context cost", my cost nevertheless remains at the "short context cost" until the utilization rises above the 200K boundary (for the Claude Sonnet example above).

Thus if I choose the long context cost, **I need to see at a glance where my utilization level is in relation to the boundary between cost levels**.

### Design Approaches

There are several ways providing visibility could be accomplished. Here are two of them. Right now we don't have _any_ visibility on this, so any approach would be an improvement for the first iteration.

#### Mark on Meter

The meter shown could have a "tick" or some other mark indicating the cost boundary. This is similar to the air speed dial of small aircraft: pilots typically draw different-colored lines indicating such things as the rotation speed, the stall speed, etc. from the technical manual so that they don't have to remember the differences between a Cessna 150 and Cessna 172 for example. (This analogy is actually very accurate.)

#### Multiple Meters

You might also decide to provide two meters: one for the relative "short context cost" level, and the other for the relative "long context cost" level.

Frankly (at least initially) it's not so important how you do it. What's important is that you surface this information, now that you have multiple billing levels. We can see what works best, and you can iterate. Right now we're at a disadvantage because it's difficult to _know_ what cost bracket we're in without interrupting work.

### Downsides from Lacking This Feature

* For now, if I set all my models to use the max context window, I have to manually hover over the meter with the mouse to open its flyover showing the token breakdown and literal utilization.
* Different models may have have different thresholds, so in addition to interrupting my work flow to look at the meter flyover with the mouse, I have to remember which model has which cost boundary.

title: NES: include current file in global token budget cascade
body:
## Problem

The xtab (NES) prompt builder has a **global token budget cascade** (`runGlobalBudgetCascade` in `extensions/copilot/src/extension/xtab/common/promptCrafting.ts`) that allocates a shared pool across:

- `recentlyViewedDocuments`
- `languageContext`
- `neighborFiles`
- `diffHistory`

Each part takes its share, reports `tokensConsumed`, and unused budget cascades to subsequent parts.

**`currentFile` is deliberately excluded from this cascade.** It is sized **outside** and **before** the cascade via `constructTaggedFile` (called at `xtabProvider.ts:312`), using its own static `currentFile.maxTokens` cap. The clipped lines are passed into `getUserPrompt` via `PromptPieces.taggedCurrentDocLines`, and the cascade never sees nor influences current-file sizing. See `extensions/copilot/src/extension/xtab/common/globalBudgetCascade.md` ("Composition with `currentFile`" section).

### Why this matters

1. **Wasted budget when other parts are disabled or small.** When `diffHistory` / `neighborFiles` / `languageContext` are configured small or disabled, their unused share cascades to `recentlyViewedDocuments` but cannot benefit the current file ŌĆö even when expanding the current-file window would be the most valuable thing to do (it's the file being edited).
2. **Asymmetric trimming.** Heuristic recently-viewed snippets compete in a shared pool, but the most context-rich source ŌĆö the file under the cursor ŌĆö is capped independently.
3. **Concrete failure mode.** We observed cases (NES log triage, PR #319998 context) where the current file was trimmed in a way that hid the very symbol the next edit needed to update. Letting the cascade donate spare budget to the current file would directly help.

PR #319998 (merged) added an opt-in flag to donate leftover above-cursor budget to below-cursor expansion **within** current-file paged clipping. This issue is about the orthogonal, larger change: letting the **cross-part** cascade also feed the current file.

## Plan

Behind a new team-internal experiment flag (`InlineEditsXtabGlobalBudgetIncludeCurrentFile`, default `false`), make `currentFile` a first-class participant in `runGlobalBudgetCascade`. Default behavior unchanged on rollout.

### Required changes

1. **`xtabPromptOptions.ts`** (~10 LOC)
   - Add `'currentFile'` to `GlobalBudgetPart` union, validator, default share. No new ordering constraint required (unlike `recentlyViewedDocuments` ŌåÆ `neighborFiles`).
2. **`promptCrafting.ts`** (~50 LOC)
   - Extract `clipCurrentFile(..., maxTokens)` helper that returns `{ clippedDoc, tokensConsumed }` (compute via `countTokensForLines` on the kept lines).
   - Add a `'currentFile'` case in `runGlobalBudgetCascade` that calls the helper with the dynamic budget.
   - Extend `CascadeResult` to carry the clipped current-file doc.
3. **`getUserPrompt`** (~10 LOC)
   - When the cascade owns `currentFile`, prefer its clipped lines over `promptPieces.taggedCurrentDocLines`.
4. **`xtabProvider.ts`** (~30 LOC)
   - Plumb raw inputs (`currentFileContentLines`, `areaAroundCodeToEditForCurrentFile`, `areaAroundEditWindowLinesRange`, `pageSize`, `currentFile` opts) through `PromptPieces` so the cascade can re-clip.
   - Preserve the early `PromptTooLarge('currentFile')` fail-fast (lines 326ŌĆō328) by running a pre-flight clip with `currentFile.maxTokens` (existing behavior) before language-context / neighbor fetches. The cascade then re-clips with the dynamic budget. The clipper is cheap; the double-call cost is negligible.
   - Move / duplicate `setNLinesOfCurrentFileInPrompt` telemetry so the reported value reflects the final post-cascade clip.
5. **Settings** (~15 LOC)
   - Add `InlineEditsXtabGlobalBudgetIncludeCurrentFile` (boolean, exp-based, default `false`).
   - Optionally add `InlineEditsXtabGlobalBudgetOrder` / `InlineEditsXtabGlobalBudgetShares` if we want to tune placement (first vs last) and shares via experiment without code change.
6. **Tests**
   - Existing `constructTaggedFile` specs and `recentFilesForPrompt.snapshots.spec.ts` cascade snapshots remain unchanged (legacy path untouched).
   - Add new cascade-snapshot tests covering: current-file first in order (today's semantics + donation), current-file last in order (cascade donates surplus to current file).
7. **Docs**
   - Update `globalBudgetCascade.md`'s "Composition with `currentFile`" section.

### Design choices to settle

- **Position in `DEFAULT_ORDER`.**
  - *First* ŌåÆ current file claims its share first, leftover donated downward. Closest to today's semantics.
  - *Last* ŌåÆ all other parts claim first, current file gets share + all surplus. Directly addresses the "current file too trimmed when other parts are small" failure mode.
  - Either is supported by the cascade; can be A/B-tested via experiment.
- **`shares` rebalancing.** Shares must sum to 1. Decision needed on the default split when `currentFile` is in the order (e.g., re-balance current `{2/6, 2/6, 1/6, 1/6}` to include current file).
- **Minimum guarantee.** Consider adding a `minTokens` floor per cascade part. If current file's effective budget falls below its minimum, fall back to `PromptTooLarge('currentFile')`. Avoids pathological cases where a huge diff history starves the file under the cursor.

### Risk

**Low**, provided:

1. The new part is opt-in via `order` / `shares`. When `'currentFile'` is not in `order`, behavior is byte-identical to today.
2. `DEFAULT_ORDER` does **not** include `'currentFile'` on initial rollout. Enable separately via experiment after the plumbing lands.

### Effort

| Task | LOC | Time |
|---|---|---|
| Types + validators + defaults | ~10 | 5 min |
| Extract `clipCurrentFile(..., maxTokens)` + expose `tokensConsumed` | ~30 | 20 min |
| `runGlobalBudgetCascade` new case + result plumbing | ~40 | 20 min |
| `xtabProvider.ts` pre-flight + raw-inputs plumbing | ~30 | 30 min |
| Move / duplicate telemetry | ~10 | 10 min |
| Tests (unit + cascade snapshots) | ~150 | 1ŌĆō2 h |
| Settings | ~15 | 15 min |
| `globalBudgetCascade.md` update | ŌĆö | 10 min |

**Total: ~3ŌĆō5 focused hours, ~250ŌĆō400 LOC.**

### Related

- PR #319998 ŌĆö opt-in donation of leftover above-cursor budget to below-cursor expansion **within** current-file paged clipping (intra-part). This issue tracks the cross-part cascade work (inter-part).

title: Copilot OTel file exporter writes spans as empty objects and drops captureContent attributes
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.51.0
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: macOS
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent mode / Copilot Chat OpenTelemetry file exporter
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): GPT-5.2-Codex
- Logs:

The Copilot Chat runtime log confirms that OpenTelemetry is enabled with file export and content capture:

```text
[OTel] Instrumentation enabled ŌĆö exporter=file ... captureContent=true
```

My VS Code settings are:

```json
"github.copilot.chat.otel.enabled": true,
"github.copilot.chat.otel.exporterType": "file",
"github.copilot.chat.otel.outfile": "/tmp/copilot-otel.jsonl",
"github.copilot.chat.otel.captureContent": true
```

The generated `/tmp/copilot-otel.jsonl` contains log and metric records such as:

```text
gen_ai.usage.input_tokens
gen_ai.usage.output_tokens
gen_ai.request.model
gen_ai.response.model
copilot_chat.tool.call
gen_ai.tool.name
traceId
spanId
```

However, the content-bearing span attributes do not appear:

```bash
grep -n "gen_ai.input.messages\|gen_ai.output.messages\|gen_ai.tool.call.arguments\|gen_ai.tool.call.result" /tmp/copilot-otel.jsonl
```

This returns no matches.

The file also contains empty JSON objects:

```json
{}
```

Based on local inspection, these empty objects appear to correspond to exported spans. Logs and metrics serialize correctly, but spans appear to be written as empty objects.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Enable Copilot Chat OpenTelemetry file export with content capture:

```json
"github.copilot.chat.otel.enabled": true,
"github.copilot.chat.otel.exporterType": "file",
"github.copilot.chat.otel.outfile": "/tmp/copilot-otel.jsonl",
"github.copilot.chat.otel.captureContent": true
```

2. Reload VS Code.

3. Run a Copilot Chat / Agent mode interaction that performs at least one tool call or file edit.

4. Inspect `/tmp/copilot-otel.jsonl`.

5. Observe that logs and metrics are written correctly, but span records appear as `{}` and content-bearing span attributes are missing.

Expected Behavior:

With `github.copilot.chat.otel.captureContent` set to `true`, I expected the file exporter to preserve span attributes such as:

```text
gen_ai.input.messages
gen_ai.output.messages
gen_ai.tool.call.arguments
gen_ai.tool.call.result
```

These fields are important because they should contain prompt messages, response messages, tool-call arguments, and tool-call results.

Actual Behavior:

The JSONL file contains metadata logs and metrics, including model names, token counts, tool names, durations, trace IDs, and span IDs.

However, the content-bearing fields are missing, and spans appear to be serialized as `{}`.

Additional Investigation:

I inspected the bundled Copilot Chat implementation locally. The implementation appears to include the requested content attribute names and conditionally attach them when content capture is enabled.

However, the file exporter appears to serialize OpenTelemetry span objects directly. Since OpenTelemetry `ReadableSpan` properties are not directly JSON-enumerable, this appears to produce `{}` for span records.

The SQLite span exporter appears to use a proper span-conversion path, so this may be specific to the file exporter.

Question:

Is this expected behavior for the file exporter, or should it serialize spans in the same way as the SQLite/OTLP exporters?

If this is a bug, I would be willing to help test a fix or submit a PR if maintainers confirm the expected serialization path.

title: [Linux] Workbench system-ui / sans-serif (and explicitly-named non-Nerd fonts) render as an installed Nerd Font; terminal glyph fallback also broken ŌĆö Electron 39
body:
**Type:** Bug

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled? (`code --disable-extensions`):** Yes ŌĆö confirmed; it still reproduces with all extensions disabled. This is renderer-level font resolution, independent of extensions.

### Environment
- VS Code: **1.122.0**, commit `6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724`, x64
- Electron **39.8.8** / Chromium **142.0.7444.265**
- OS: **Bluefin** (Fedora atomic / Silverblue) `44.20260528.1`, image `bluefin-dx-nvidia-open`
- **GNOME Shell 50.1**, **Wayland**
- Fonts: the full [ryanoasis/nerd-fonts](https://github.com/ryanoasis/nerd-fonts) collection installed in `~/.local/share/fonts` (~24 patched families / ~290 files)

### Summary
On Linux, with many Nerd Fonts installed, VS Code's Chromium renderer resolves the **workbench UI font** to an installed broad-coverage **Nerd Font** instead of a sans-serif. The default workbench stack is:

```
system-ui, Ubuntu, "Droid Sans", sans-serif
```

On this machine, every entry in that stack ŌĆö **and even explicitly-named non-Nerd fonts** ŌĆö resolves to a Nerd Font (e.g. `CaskaydiaMono NF`, `FiraCode Nerd Font`). `fc-match` resolves those same families to normal sans-serifs (Noto Sans / Adwaita Sans), so **Chromium's resolution disagrees with fontconfig**.

The same substitution breaks the **integrated terminal's per-glyph fallback**: a character absent from the configured terminal font falls back to a Nerd Font that *also* lacks it (instead of an installed symbol/emoji font), so symbols such as **ŌÅĖ (U+23F8)** render as missing-glyph (`.notdef`) boxes.

### Steps to reproduce
1. On Linux VS Code (Electron 39), install the full Nerd Fonts collection into `~/.local/share/fonts`; `fc-cache -f`.
2. The **workbench chrome** (sidebar, tabs, menus, command palette) renders in a monospace Nerd Font rather than a sans-serif.
3. Confirm via **Help ŌåÆ Toggle Developer Tools**: inspect a sidebar label ŌåÆ **Computed ŌåÆ Rendered Fonts** shows e.g. `CaskaydiaMono NF`, while `getComputedStyle(el).fontFamily` is the declared `system-ui, Ubuntu, "Droid Sans", sans-serif`.
4. In the DevTools Console, a `<div>` with `font-family: "Droid Sans"` ŌĆö or `"Adwaita Sans"`, or a **bogus** family name ŌĆö *also* renders as the Nerd Font. So non-Nerd named fonts **and** the generic last-resort all resolve to a Nerd Font.
5. `fc-match system-ui` and `fc-match sans-serif` ŌåÆ `Noto Sans` / `Adwaita Sans` (Chromium does not agree).
6. **Integrated terminal** (`terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration: off`, DOM renderer): a glyph not in the configured terminal font ŌĆö e.g. **ŌÅĖ (U+23F8)** ŌĆö renders as a `.notdef` box, even though installed fonts (`Adwaita Mono`, `Noto Sans Symbols 2`, `Noto Color Emoji`, `Symbola`) contain it. `fc-match ':charset=23F8'` ŌåÆ **`Adwaita Mono`**, which is literally the terminal's own configured fallback (`terminal.integrated.fontFamily: "'JetBrainsMono Nerd Font Mono', 'Adwaita Mono'"`) ŌĆö yet it isn't used.

### Expected
- The workbench stack resolves to a **sans-serif** (as `fc-match` does, and as happens on Ubuntu/Pop!_OS where the `Ubuntu` family in the stack is installed and matches).
- Explicitly-named installed fonts render as **themselves**.
- The terminal falls back to an installed font that **contains** the requested glyph.

### Actual
Broad-coverage **Nerd Fonts hijack all non-Nerd font resolution** in the renderer ŌĆö the workbench chrome, explicitly-named non-Nerd fonts, and the terminal's missing-glyph fallback.

### Diagnosis / notes
- Chromium's fallback/last-resort appears to pick the **broadest-coverage** font available; with the full Nerd Fonts collection installed, that is always a (monospace, icon-laden) Nerd Font.
- Removing one Nerd Font makes the fallback **hop to the next** Nerd Font (whack-a-mole across ~24 families).
- A user fontconfig `alias`/`prefer` pinning `system-ui`/`sans-serif` to a real sans is **ignored by VS Code** (consistent with #206202), although `fc-match` honors it.
- `org.gnome.desktop.interface font-name` is **not honored** by the workbench, and there is no `workbench.*fontFamily` setting to override the chrome font.
- The **editor** renders correctly only because `editor.fontFamily` names a Nerd Font *explicitly*; it is non-Nerd requests that break.

### Regression timeline
Did **not** occur on Pop!_OS (Ubuntu-based, older Electron, and the `Ubuntu` font present). Appeared on this **Bluefin / Fedora-atomic + Electron 39** setup. Suggests an Electron-39 font-resolution change and/or reliance on the `Ubuntu` font being installed for the stack to match.

### Likely upstream
This looks like Electron/Chromium font fallback behavior on Linux rather than VS Code logic, but it's filed here for visibility and an actionable repro.

### Related issues
- #206202 ŌĆö *Fontconfig charset edit does not apply in vscode* (open). Same root: VS Code/Electron ignoring fontconfig on Linux.
- #292172 ŌĆö *VS Code UI has incorrect text spacing/rendering on Linux with Electron 39* (closed; different symptom ŌĆö spacing).
- electron/electron#18193, electron/electron#18197, electron/electron#15481 ŌĆö historical "sans-serif falls back to the wrong font" / default-font-matching issues (closed).

### Workaround
Remove the full patched Nerd Fonts (keep only the symbols-only **Symbols Nerd Font** + unpatched base fonts, per Nerd Fonts' own [Font Substitution & Fallback](https://github.com/ryanoasis/nerd-fonts/wiki/Font-Substitution-&-Fallback) guidance), and point `editor`/`terminal` fonts at the unpatched base font + Symbols Nerd Font. Trade-off: loses by-name access to the patched fonts.

---

_Happy to attach screenshots / DevTools "Rendered Fonts" captures on request._

title: Option to mark a block when pasting
body:
### Type of Feature Request
Enhancement to editor behavior / paste functionality.

### Description
It would be helpful to have a feature that allows users to automatically "mark" or highlight a specific block of text or code immediately upon pasting it. 

When a user pastes code, the entire newly inserted section would be treated as a unified block. This would allow the editor to visually distinguish the pasted text, maintain specific formatting, or apply unique styles automatically.

### Technical Details / Use Case
* **Improved Context:** When pasting large blocks of code, it can be difficult to see where the pasted code starts and ends. Visually marking the block makes it instantly recognizable.
* **Formatting Control:** It would allow for easier block-level operations (like re-indenting, folding, or moving) immediately after a paste action.
