title: Clarify model "long context cost" determination
body:
In GitHub Copilot today many of the models now allow selection of context size, which is of course welcome. For example Claude Sonnet now says:

> ### Claude Sonnet 4.6
> Balanced Claude model offering strong performance for everyday coding and chat tasks at faster speeds.
> 
> #### Cost (per 1M tokens)
> Medium cost  
> Input: 300 credits  
> Cached input: 30 credits  
> Output: 1500 credits  
> 
> #### Long context cost (per 1M tokens)
> Input: 600 credits  
> Cached input: 60 credits  
> Output: 2250 credits
> 
> 
> #### Max context
> 1M

The two context window sizes to choose from are 200K and 1M. It's not clear how the determination is made for the costs. Does merely setting the context size switch to a different mode for different costs, applying to all submissions regardless of the actual size of content? In other words, is the cost based upon "mode selection" or actual context windows size _used_?

**Question 1:** Is the "long context cost" charged based upon choosing the "Long context cost" mode (in which case even a context of 12K for example would be charged at the "long context cost"), or based upon whether the the content in context windows goes over the standard context window (i.e. 12K in the "long context cost" would still be charged at the "short context context cost)?

**Question 2:** If the latter (i.e. short context windows get charged the same for both modes), for the content that goes over into the "long context window" mode, does the charge switch to applying to the entire context window, or only for the overage (similar to tax brackets in the USA)?

title: Copilot Idea: "Smart Staging"
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

I frequently find myself with a set of changes that I really need to break up into separate PRs. It can be very time consuming to do this manually.

I think that Copilot could do a pretty decent job at this. It can ask whether the user wants to split into stacked PRs (where one depends on another), or independent PRs. Bonus points if it can make build/test succeed in each of them before submitting the PR, and deal with the inevitable merge conflicts.

I was able to get a decent result from just a prompt, but it was slow and not particularly pretty in terms of UX.

/cc @isidorn

title: Feature request: Optional model discovery for customendpoint with OpenRouter or OpenAI-compatible providers
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->

Currently, when using `vendor: "customendpoint"` in `chatLanguageModels.json` (such as with OpenRouter or any OpenAI-compatible provider), models must be listed manually in the `models` array. This is tedious and error-prone when the provider offers many available models or updates its catalog frequently.

**Request:**
- Support optional, automatic model discovery for `customendpoint` providers that expose a compatible model-list API (e.g., OpenAI's `/v1/models`).
- If `url` points to a standard OpenAI-compatible endpoint and discovery is successful, VS Code should fetch and display available models automatically in the picker and config.
- Allow manual `models` entries as an override or fallback, so users can customize/limit their view or supply capability details (tool calling, etc.) for specific models.
- If discovery fails (auth, network, unsupported schema), fall back gracefully to manual or prompt for user action.

### Why this matters
- Providers like OpenRouter expose dozens (sometimes hundreds) of models via a models endpoint, but manual config is not scalable as models change.
- Auto-discovery reduces repetitive config, keeps lists in sync, and matches user expectations based on other provider experiences.
- This would align `customendpoint` with capabilities offered by extensions and some built-in providers for model management.

**Example:**
- User sets up OpenRouter with `vendor: "customendpoint"` and only configures the main endpoint and API key. VS Code should show all available and permitted models in the picker, just as the web portal does.

### References
- Related issues: #318705, #316916
- Docs: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/customization/language-models

Thanks for considering!

title: [Bug] GitHub Copilot Chat takes 100-140s to activate in VS Code with WSL remote due to Electron fetcher fallback timeout
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (6a44c352)
- OS Version: Windows 10 (10.0.26200), WSL2 Ubuntu, VS Code Server over WSL

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Install VS Code on Windows with the WSL extension and GitHub Copilot Chat
2. Open a folder via WSL remote (Remote-WSL: Open Folder in WSL)
3. Open a workspace containing `package.json`, `*.php` files, and `.github/workflows/`
4. Restart VS Code and open `Developer: Startup Performance` from the Command Palette
5. Observe `Finish Activate` time for `GitHub.copilot-chat` (~100-140 seconds)
6. Open the **GitHub Copilot Chat** output channel and observe the delay between `Waiting for copilot token` and `Got Copilot token`

**What happens:**

Copilot Chat takes 100ŌĆō140 seconds to activate in a VS Code Server over WSL environment. The first thing logged is `Can't use the Electron fetcher in this environment` followed by silence for ~100-140 seconds before the token is finally obtained. During this time, unrelated extensions that depend on `languageModelAccess` are also blocked (`vitest.explorer`, `github.vscode-github-actions`), which return to normal activation times (~500ms) when Copilot Chat is disabled.

**Copilot Chat output log (103s case):**

```log
10:29:09.613 [info] Can't use the Electron fetcher in this environment.
10:29:09.613 [info] Using the Node fetch fetcher.
10:29:09.730 [info] ConversationFeature: Waiting for copilot token or BYOK model to activate conversation feature
              ŌåÉ ~103 seconds of silence ŌåÆ
10:30:56.518 [info] FetcherService: node-fetch failed with error: fetch failed
10:30:56.548 [info] FetcherService: node-fetch failed with error: fetch failed
10:30:56.576 [info] [CopilotCLIChatSessionContentProvider] listSessions took 100993.945443ms
10:30:58.181 [info] Got Copilot token for 
10:30:58.228 [info] activationBlocker from 'languageModelAccess' took for 103034ms
10:30:58.814 [info] activationBlocker from 'conversationFeature' took for 103625ms
10:30:59.829 [error] Failed to create database. Falling back to in-memory db: Error: unable to open database file
```

**Startup Performance ŌĆö Copilot Chat enabled vs disabled:**

| Extension | With Copilot Chat | Without Copilot Chat |
|---|---|---|
| `GitHub.copilot-chat` | ~110,000ms | ŌĆö |
| `vitest.explorer` | ~107,000ms | 478ms |
| `github.vscode-github-actions` | ~109,000ms | 1,623ms |

**What was ruled out:**

- Network: `curl https://api.github.com/zen` responds in ~237ms from WSL
- Token corruption: GitHub Sign Out + Sign In did not resolve the issue
- Proxy: No proxy configured in WSL or VS Code settings
- dbus: `DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS` is set and active
- File permissions: Full read/write access on Copilot's `globalStorage` directory
- `github.copilot.chat.claudeAgent.enabled: false`: Did not resolve the issue

**Additional notes:**

- The delay is variable (103ŌĆō140s across restarts), suggesting a retry loop rather than a fixed timeout
- `Developer: GitHub Copilot Chat Diagnostics` command is not available (possibly restricted to paid plans)
- Trace-level logging could not be captured at startup because log levels set via `Developer: Set Log Level...` do not persist across reloads in this environment
- Copilot plan: `free_limited_copilot`

title: Renaming a CLI-originated session in chat history causes it to disappear
body:
## Description

Renaming a Copilot CLI terminal session from the VS Code chat history panel causes it to disappear from the UI. Sessions started directly in VS Code chat can be renamed without issue.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Start a Copilot session in the integrated terminal (Copilot CLI)
2. Have a conversation so the session appears in the chat history panel
3. In the chat history panel, rename that CLI-originated session
4. The session disappears from the history panel

## Expected Behavior

The session should remain visible in the history panel under its new name, regardless of where it originated.

## Actual Behavior

The session vanishes from the history panel. The debug log folder for the session gets cleaned up, making recovery impossible through normal means. The session data persists in the session store database (queryable via SQL) but is not surfaced in the UI.

## Additional Context

- Sessions started in VS Code chat can be renamed, pinned, and renamed without issue
- Only CLI-originated sessions exhibit this behavior
- The `github.copilot.chat.localIndex.enabled` setting is enabled
- Reloading the window does not restore the sessions
- The session store SQL database still contains the session records with the renamed titles

## Environment

- VS Code (macOS)
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension (latest as of June 2026)
- Copilot CLI sessions appearing in chat history

title: synced sessions not appearing on my other machine
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.51.0
- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: Kubuntu 24.04

Details:
I synced my chats from my work-machine (Kubunt 24.04) to github.
Sessions appear on GitHub session page, but do not appear in the local Copilot session history list in VS Code of my home-machine (also (Kubunt 24.04) , even after window reload and after successful reindex.

Logs:
- Chronicle reindex result:
  Local reindex complete
  Sessions: 3 -> 3 (delta 0)
  Turns: 3 -> 3 (delta 0)
  Files: 0 -> 0
  Refs: 0 -> 0
  Processed: 0, Skipped: 3
- Session lookup confirms target session exists in indexed data

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open VS Code 1.123.0 on Linux with GitHub Copilot Chat enabled and signed in.
2. Run /chronicle reindex in Copilot Chat.
3. Observe successful reindex output showing local sessions present (for example Sessions 3 -> 3, Skipped 3).
4. Open Copilot session history in VS Code.
5. Expected: recently indexed sessions are visible.
6. Actual: session is not visible in local history UI, although it is present in indexed/session data and visible on GitHub session page.

title: Adding the first root folder to an empty-window workspace restarts the extension host, killing in-progress Copilot Chat sessions
body:
### Description

Type: Bug / UX papercut

VS Code version: 
Version: 1.123.0 (user setup)
Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8
Date: 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00
Electron: 42.2.0
ElectronBuildId: 14159160
Chromium: 148.0.7778.97
Node.js: 24.15.0
V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
OS: Windows

Describe the bug
When a window has no folder open (empty workspace) and you add a folder via Add Folder to Workspace (workbench.action.addRootFolder), VS Code transitions the workspace and restarts the extension host. This triggers a "Please confirm restart of extensions ŌĆö A session is in progress" dialog and, if confirmed, terminates the active GitHub Copilot Chat session.

Steps to reproduce

Launch VS Code with no folder open (empty window).
Start a Copilot Chat session and do some work.
Add Folder to Workspace ŌåÆ pick any folder.
Observe the "Please confirm restart of extensions" dialog; confirming restarts the extension host and loses the chat session.
Expected behavior
Adding a folder to an existing window should not force an extension-host restart that destroys in-progress extension state (like a Copilot Chat conversation). Going from 0ŌåÆ1 folders should behave like NŌåÆN+1 folders, which does not restart the host.

Actual behavior
The 0ŌåÆ1 folder transition (empty window ŌåÆ single-root/multi-root workspace) restarts the extension host, killing the chat session. There's no path to mount the first folder without losing session state.

Impact
Users who start a chat in an empty window ŌĆö e.g., during design/planning before a repo exists ŌĆö must choose between keeping their conversation or working with the folder mounted in the Explorer. Long planning sessions are lost on folder add.

Possible improvements

Preserve extension-host state across the emptyŌåÆsingle-folder transition (avoid the restart), or
Allow extensions to persist/restore session state across the transition, or
Make the restart non-destructive for chat (serialize + rehydrate the conversation).

### Issue Details

Item|Value
---|---
Issue Category|Feature Request
Target|Visual Studio Code
VS Code Version|Code 1.123.0 (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8, 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00)

title: Markdown `editor.colorDecorators: false` not honored on startup; likely race condition
body:
As per #233211, the following VS Code setting turns off inappropriately showing strings like `#xxxxxx` as a CSS color indicator in Markdown (particularly ironic and irritating when the Markdown is a list of VS Code bug titles):

```json
  "[markdown]": {
    "editor.colorDecorators": false
  },
```

To reproduce (nondeterministic, suggesting a race condition):

1. Set `"editor.colorDecorators": false` in `settings.json` as above.
2. Create a Markdown page full of `#xxxxxx` references, such as these:
    ```markdown
    * [GitHub Copilot Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toString') when resetting. #319626](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319626)
    * [GitHub Copilot Markdown paste discards HTML tag from inline code #319733](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319733)
    * [[major] v1.123 breaks model-selection ability when resubmitting prompt #319743](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319743)
    ```
3. Restart VS Code.

**Expected:** Strings such as ` #319626` do _not_ show a color decorator because `editor.colorDecorators` is set to `false`.

**Actual:** Strings such as ` #319626` often _do_ show a color decorator.

**Workaround:** When this occurs, switch to another tab and then switch back. Upon switching back to the original tab, the color decorators disappear.

**Discussion:** This does not always occur. In my case I'm usually loading large workspaces with many files open when it happens. This, along with the fact that it corrects itself after switching tabs, leads me to believe it's some sort of race condition checking the settings upon first load. Nevertheless, it is irritating to have to manually switch tabs just to get the colors to go away. (They shouldn't have been there in the first place, as discussed in #233211; this [should have been made an opt-in](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/233211#issuecomment-2460269705) for languages other than CSS.)

```

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a new window
2. Open the chat session sidebar
3. Set filters to show all sessions
4. Observe session list does not include sessions from chats in workspace

In the [docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/agents/sessions/chat-sessions#_sessions-list) for the Session List it says:
> The list of sessions is scoped to your workspace. If you don't have a workspace open, the list shows all sessions across your workspaces. The sessions are grouped by time periods, such as Today or Last Week.

Issue: The chat session list does not show all sessions across all workspaces when  a workspace is not open.  I can not search across all sessions in all workspaces and workspaces in my workflow are rather ephemeral so I can't find old chats that I need to refer to.

Expectation:  If you don't have a workspace open, the list shows all sessions across your workspaces.

title: Windows-style absolute paths still flagged as outside of workspace in GitHub Copilot
body:
Using VS Code on Windows with Git Bash for Windows:

1. Create a workspace at `C:\projects\app\`.
2. Create a file `C:\projects\app\foo\bar.txt`.
3. Ask a model (in my case Claude Sonnet 4.6) to edit `C:\projects\foo\bar.txt`. Specifically tell it to **use the form `C:\projects\app\foo\bar.txt`**, not the relative, Git Bash form `foo/bar.txt`.

**Expected:** GitHub Copilot recognizes that `C:\projects\app\foo\bar.txt` resolves to a file inside the workspace, and the edit occurs.

**Actual:** GitHub Copilot suspends actions and blocks, asking the user:

> The model wants to edit files outside of your workspace (`C:\projects\app\foo\bar.txt`). Do you want to allow this?
> 
> <contents are identical>

(I have no idea why it says "<contents are identical>". That's probably another bug.)

This is in the same line with bug #293204, which was probably incorrectly closed as a duplicate of #292424, as I [pointed out](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/292424#issuecomment-3881613660).

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: MacOs 26.5

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Follow [this documentation](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/administer-copilot/manage-for-enterprise/manage-agents/configure-enterprise-plugin-standards)
2. Create the `.github-private` repo in your organisation
3. Define
```
{
  "extraKnownMarketplaces": {
    "defaultPlugin": {
      "source": {
        "source": "github",
        "repo": "MyOrg/MyMarketplaceRepo"
      }
    }
  },
  "enabledPlugins": {
    "defaultPlugin@plugin1": true
  }
}
```

> Behavior

- The Marketplace should be installed automatically => Ō£ģ 
- `plugin1` should be installed by default => Ō£ģ
- other plugins from the `defaultPlugin`, not marked explicitly as `false` should be installable by the user => ŌØī
- plugins from other marketplaces should be installable by the user => ŌØī

I've traced the issue to this code:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blame/aa4c2ff7c3e3d7a9bac8576930efca39cd02f2f5/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/agentPluginServiceImpl.ts#L183-L189

It is in my opinion not in accordance with the documentation
I am not setting `strictMarketplaces`, so i do not understand why plugins from other marketplaces should be affected (meaning disabled).

> Expectations

- Plugins referenced in `enabledPlugins` with `true` should be installed automatically
- Plugins referenced in `enabledPlugins` with `false` should be disabled
- Plugins NOT referenced in `enabledPlugins` (falsy) should not be impacted, meaning the user should be able to install them

title: Telemetry data for keyboard layout to assist with PL design
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Microsoft is in a unique position of having an internationally popular programming editor that could (or maybe already does?) collect telemetry about what keyboard layouts programmers are using. Aggregate data could be very useful for programming language design, because you could use it to determine the best choice of symbols for your language: which symbols are difficult for most programmers to type? Which are easy? Alternative approaches using country population counts are not as accurate, because they're not programmer specific (technically vscode can be used to edit things other than source code, but AFAICT that is its most popular use). @alexdima you appear to have done a lot of work on input, wondering if you might know if this data already exists.

title: VS Code Insiders MCP + OAuth Protected Resource Regression (works in Stable, fails in Insiders)
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.124.0-insider (x64) - OS Version: Windows 10/11 (corporate environment, behind HTTP proxy) Steps to Reproduce: 1. Configure an MCP HTTP server behind an API gateway as an OAuth protected resource: - `/mcp/mcp` returns `401` with `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="https://gateway.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"` when no bearer token is provided. - Implement `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`, `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server`, `/register`, `/authorize`, `/token` for the OAuth flow. 2. Configure `mcp.json` for VS Code Insiders with a server of `type: "http"` pointing to `https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp` and restart Insiders. 3. Use the MCP server from VS Code Insiders (e.g. run a command that triggers MCP) and observe the log: - `Connection state: Error Error sending message to https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp: TypeError: fetch failed`. 4. Repeat the same steps with VS Code Stable using the same `mcp.json` and environment: - Stable connects successfully and the MCP server works as expected. # VS Code Insiders MCP + OAuth Protected Resource Regression (works in Stable, fails in Insiders) ## Summary - I have the same MCP HTTP server + API gateway + OAuth protected resource setup. - VS Code **Stable** (e.g. `1.115.0`) can successfully connect to the MCP server via HTTP + OAuth discovery. - VS Code **Insiders** (e.g. `1.124.0-insider`) with the **same** `mcp.json` and same environment fails with: > `Connection state: Error Error sending message to https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp: TypeError: fetch failed` - From the same machine, `curl` to the MCP endpoint and the gateway admin API works fine (no TLS/proxy/network issues). - On the gateway side, the MCP endpoint is configured as an **OAuth protected resource** (RFC 9728): - First request returns 401 + `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="https://gateway.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"`. - Stable seems to handle this flow correctly; Insiders surfaces a generic `TypeError: fetch failed` instead. This looks like a regression or behavior change in the MCP HTTP client / OAuth discovery flow in Insiders. --- ## Environment - OS: Windows (corporate environment, behind HTTP proxy) - VS Code Stable: - Version: `1.115.0` (example) - Arch: `x64` - VS Code Insiders: - Version: `1.124.0-insider` (example) - Arch: `x64` Both use their own `mcp.json`: - Stable: `%APPDATA%/Code/User/mcp.json` - Insiders: `%APPDATA%/Code - Insiders/User/mcp.json` The MCP configuration for the HTTP server is identical in both. --- ## MCP configuration (mcp.json) Simplified but structurally accurate example: ```jsonc { "servers": { "my-http-mcpserver": { "type": "http", "url": "https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp" } }, "inputs": [] } ``` - The problematic server is `my-http-mcpserver` (`type: "http"`). - Stable and Insiders both use this exact configuration. --- ## Gateway / OAuth protected resource setup The MCP HTTP server is fronted by an API gateway (APISIX-like) at: - MCP endpoint: `https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp` Relevant route (simplified): - URIs: `[/mcp/*, /api/mcp/*]` - Methods: `GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, HEAD, OPTIONS` - Upstream: `mcp-backend.example.internal:80` - Key plugins / middleware: ```jsonc "plugins": { "request-id": { "header_name": "CorrelationId", "algorithm": "uuid", "include_in_response": true }, "proxy-rewrite": { "use_real_request_uri_unsafe": false, "regex_uri": ["^/mcp/(.*)", "/$1"] }, "cors": { "allow_origins": "*", "allow_methods": "*", "allow_headers": "*", "max_age": 5 }, "response-rewrite": { "headers": { "remove": ["Server"], "set": { "X-Accel-Buffering": "no", "WWW-Authenticate": "Bearer resource_metadata=\"https://gateway.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource\"" } } }, "openid-connect": { "client_id": "apisix", "client_secret": "*****", "realm": "my-realm", "unauth_action": "auth", "bearer_only": true, "scope": "openid profile", "discovery": "https://auth.example.com/realms/my-realm/.well-known/openid-configuration", "introspection_endpoint": "https://auth.example.com/realms/my-realm/protocol/openid-connect/token/introspect", "timeout": 10, "ssl_verify": false } } ``` So `/mcp/mcp` is an **OAuth protected resource**: - If no bearer token is provided ŌåÆ 401 Unauthorized. - `WWW-Authenticate` header includes `resource_metadata` pointing to the protected resource metadata endpoint. Additional gateway endpoints: - `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` ŌåÆ protected resource metadata (JSON, RFC 9728-style) - `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` ŌåÆ authorization server metadata (RFC 8414) - `/register` ŌåÆ dynamic client registration mock (RFC 7591-style, returns a pre-configured client for VS Code) - `/authorize` ŌåÆ authorization endpoint (redirects to the real auth server) - `/token` ŌåÆ token endpoint proxy (for the real auth server) Example protected resource metadata response: ```jsonc { "scopes_supported": ["openid", "profile"], "authorization_servers": ["https://gateway.example.com/realms/my-realm"], "resource_name": "my-http-mcpserver", "resource": "https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp", "resource_documentation": "https://gateway.example.com/mcp/swagger/index.html", "bearer_methods_supported": ["header"] } ``` The goal is for the MCP HTTP client to use RFC 9728 + 8414 + 7591 style discovery to obtain a token and then connect to the MCP server. --- ## curl output (from the same machine) From the same Windows machine where VS Code runs: ```powershell curl.exe -v "https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp" ``` Trimmed output: ```text * Host gateway.example.com:443 was resolved. * Trying :443... * Established connection ... > GET /mcp/mcp HTTP/1.1 > Host: gateway.example.com > User-Agent: curl/8.18.0 > Accept: */* > < HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized < WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="https://gateway.example.com/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource" < CorrelationId: < ... 401 Authorization Required

401 Authorization Required

... ``` So: - Network / DNS / TLS / proxy are all fine. - The 401 is expected (no bearer token). - The `WWW-Authenticate` header is correctly set for OAuth protected resource discovery. --- ## VS Code Insiders log When using the MCP server from VS Code Insiders, I see logs like: ```text [info] Connection state: Error Error sending message to https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp: TypeError: fetch failed ``` - There is no more detailed error message, just a generic `TypeError: fetch failed`. - With the same `mcp.json` and same gateway setup, VS Code Stable does **not** show this error; the MCP server works as expected. --- ## Expected behavior I expect VS Code Insiders to behave like Stable: 1. First call to the MCP HTTP server URL returns 401 + `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="..."`. 2. This should be treated as the start of the OAuth protected resource discovery flow, not as a fatal error. 3. The client should then automatically: - `GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` - `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` - `POST /register` (dynamic client registration) - `GET /authorize` (authorization code flow) - `POST /token` (token exchange) 4. With the obtained access token, it should call: - `POST https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp` - with `Authorization: Bearer ` and establish the MCP JSON-RPC connection. This flow appears to work in Stable; in Insiders it fails early with `TypeError: fetch failed`. --- ## Actual behavior - VS Code Stable: - Same `mcp.json` + same gateway + same environment ŌåÆ MCP server works. - VS Code Insiders: - Same setup ŌåÆ `TypeError: fetch failed` when connecting to the MCP HTTP server. On the gateway side: - Route configuration is identical. - curl tests behave identically. - The only difference is the VS Code version. This strongly suggests the issue is in the MCP HTTP client / OAuth handling in Insiders, not in the gateway configuration. --- ## Possible causes (hypothesis) Some possible scenarios: 1. **Insiders does not correctly interpret `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="..."`** - It might treat the initial 401 as a hard failure and never start the OAuth discovery flow. 2. **Insiders expects different JSON shapes for the OAuth discovery endpoints** - For example, it might be stricter about the responses from `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` or `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` and fail if they differ slightly from what it expects. - If it fails during discovery, it may surface only a generic `TypeError: fetch failed`. 3. **Insiders behaves differently with proxies / redirects / TLS in combination with OAuth discovery** - The environment uses `HTTPS_PROXY` and `no_proxy`. - However, both curl and Stable work fine, so this seems less likely. --- ## Repro steps (high-level) 1. Expose an MCP HTTP server behind an API gateway as an OAuth protected resource: - `/mcp/mcp` ŌåÆ OAuth protected resource (401 + `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="..."`). - `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` ŌåÆ protected resource metadata. - `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` ŌåÆ authorization server metadata. - `/register`, `/authorize`, `/token` ŌåÆ endpoints for the OAuth flow. 2. Configure `mcp.json`: ```jsonc { "servers": { "my-http-mcpserver": { "type": "http", "url": "https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp" } } } ``` 3. On the same machine: - Run `curl -v https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp` and verify you get 401 + `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="..."`. 4. Use VS Code Stable with this `mcp.json`: - Expected: MCP server works, requests go through the gateway. 5. Use VS Code Insiders with the same `mcp.json`: - Observed: `Connection state: Error Error sending message to https://gateway.example.com/mcp/mcp: TypeError: fetch failed`. --- ## What would help debug To help narrow this down, it would be useful to know: - Does Insiders still support the same OAuth protected resource flow for MCP HTTP servers as Stable? - Is there a way to enable more detailed MCP/OAuth logging in Insiders to see: - Whether it actually calls: - `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` - `/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` - `/register` - `/authorize` - `/token` - Or if it fails immediately on the first 401 from `/mcp/mcp`. - If it does call these endpoints: - At which step and with which response does `TypeError: fetch failed` occur? If needed, I can: - Provide anonymized gateway logs showing the exact HTTP call sequence from Stable vs Insiders. - Share simplified versions of the protected resource / authorization server metadata JSON responses. --- ## Workarounds For now, the only reliable workarounds I see are: - Use VS Code Stable instead of Insiders for this MCP server, **or** - Temporarily remove OAuth protection for the MCP route (or expose a separate no-auth route) so that Insiders can connect via plain HTTP without OAuth. However, the long-term goal is to keep the MCP HTTP server as an OAuth protected resource and have VS Code Insiders behave like Stable.

title: [Remote SSH] Activity bar icons missing due to CORS blocking vscode-managed-remote-resource:// font scheme (1.123 regression)
body:
Type: Bug 1. Connect to a remote host via Remote SSH extension 2. Open VSCode with a remote workspace 3. Observe that activity bar icons from extensions (GitLens, Database Client, Claude Code, vscode-icons) are missing/invisible **Expected:** Icons render normally in the activity bar **Actual:** Icons are invisible (buttons still clickable but no icon shown) **Root cause visible in Developer Tools console:** Access to font at 'vscode-managed-remote-resource://...' from origin 'vscode-file://vscode-app' has been blocked by CORS policy: Cross origin requests are only supported for protocol schemes: chrome, chrome-extension, chrome-untrusted, data, http, https, vscode-file, vscode-remote-resource, vscode-webview. The scheme `vscode-managed-remote-resource` is not in the allowlist, while `vscode-remote-resource` is. This regression was introduced in 1.123.0 ŌĆö downgrading to 1.122.1 resolves the issue. **VSCode version:** 1.123.0 **OS:** Windows (client), Linux (remote) **Remote SSH extension:** 0.124.0 VS Code version: Code 1.122.0 (6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724, 2026-05-27T14:03:01-07:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.10.0-35-amd64

## GitHub Tags
#A11yTCS; #Visual Studio CodeŌĆ»Client; #agency:plugin=a11y@company; #ES chat_Deeplink;#E2E_VisualStudioCodeClient_Win32_Dec2025;#DesktopApp;#FTP;#Win32;#A11ySev2;#A11yMAS;#WCAG2.4.3;ŌĆ»#Keyboard;

## Issue Subcategory
Illogical focus order 

## User Experience
Keyboard users encounter controls in an unexpected, confusing sequence that does not match the visual layout. Form fields may be reached out of order, causing data entry errors. 

## Repro Steps
Open Visual studio code > Install 'GitHub Copilot Modernization' extension > Download repro https://github.com/Azure-Samples/java-migration-copilot-samples and unzip it and open as folder > 'GitHub Copilot Modernization' appear in left navigation > 'Migrate to Azure' button > Once report is generated, it will open in Assessment report tab TAB to report > Observe the  issue: An illogical keyboard focus order observed when navigating from Split Editor button using Tab key.

## Actual Result
Keyboard focus moves from the Split Editor button to the Notification bar present at the bottom of the screen and then back to the adjacent interactive control (Share Feedback) upon further Tab navigation, creating an inconsistent and illogical focus sequence.

## Expected Result
Keyboard focus should move in a logical topŌĆætoŌĆæbottom navigation order.
Focus should move sequentially to the Share Feedback control after the Split Editor button, and the Notification bar should be reached only at the end of the page navigation order.

## How to Fix
Ensure DOM order matches visual order. Avoid positive tabindex values (use only 0 or -1). Use CSS for visual positioning without changing the underlying DOM order.

## Environment Details:
Application: VS Code
Edition Windows 11 Enterprise
Version 25H2
OS build 26200.6899

title: Sticky Scroll maxLineCount overrides and breaks cursorSurroundingLines at both top and bottom of the editor
body:
Type: Bug ### Bug Description The `editor.stickyScroll.maxLineCount` setting incorrectly enforces a static margin at both the top AND the bottom of the editor view, completely overriding the `editor.cursorSurroundingLines` configuration. When `maxLineCount` is set to a high value (e.g., 20), the editor starts scrolling prematurely when the cursor or a drag-and-drop selection is still 20 lines away from the bottom or top edge. This behavior persists even when there are no active sticky lines visible (e.g., in flat files or at the top of the document). This makes drag-and-drop code movement incredibly difficult and aggressive, especially when zoomed in, as the usable editor viewport shrinks significantly. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Set `"editor.stickyScroll.maxLineCount": 20` and `"editor.cursorSurroundingLines": 0`. 2. Zoom into the editor (`Ctrl + +`) to reduce visible rows. 3. Try to drag and drop a block of code towards the bottom or top of the screen. 4. **Result:** The editor starts aggressively autoscrolling 20 lines before reaching the actual edge. ### Expected Behavior - `editor.stickyScroll.maxLineCount` should only dynamically reserve space at the TOP of the editor, and ONLY when sticky rows are actually present. - It should never affect the bottom edge of the editor viewport. - `editor.cursorSurroundingLines` should be respected as the primary setting for cursor-to-edge padding. title: Web/serverful: sustained bidirectional webviewŌåöremote-extension messaging stalls after the first round-trip (webview SPA never finishes bootstrap)
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** No ŌĆö it requires a webview-SPA extension (see notes; the trigger is the *messaging pattern*, not a specific extension).

- **VS Code version:** Code-OSS @ commit `987c9597516278c9fcf10d963a0592ce1384ab93` (1.121.x), reproduced on multiple web/serverful builds (see below)
- **OS version:** macOS (arm64); host is a browser/Electron renderer talking to a remote extension host
- **Mode:** **Web / serverful** (remote extension host), *not* desktop

### Summary

In **VS Code Web / serverful mode** (browser workbench + remote extension host), a webview that drives **sustained bidirectional RPC** over the webviewŌåöextension message channel **stalls after the first round-trip**: the webview loads its HTML shell and spinner, the first request/response completes, and then no further messages flow in either direction, so the webview SPA never finishes bootstrapping and shows a permanent loader.

The **same extension works perfectly in desktop VS Code** (local extension host + Electron webview), so the extension itself is fine ŌĆö the difference is the web/remote webview message transport.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run VS Code in web/serverful mode with a **remote** extension host (e.g. `code serve-web`, code-server, or VSCodium-web).
2. Install a webview extension whose UI is an SPA that performs **high-frequency, bidirectional RPC** over the webview channel (the concrete case we hit is OpenAI's `openai.chatgpt` / Codex, which uses a capnweb-style protocol: messages of the form `{ "type": "vscode-capn-rpc-message", "message": "[ŌĆ”]" }`).
3. Open the extension's webview (its sidebar view or its editor panel).
4. Observe the webview's bootstrap traffic.

### Expected

The webview completes its bootstrap handshake (it does in **desktop** VS Code) and renders its UI.

### Actual

The webview shell + spinner load, then the capnweb bootstrap does roughly:

```
webview ŌåÆ push  ["pipeline", 0, ["services"]]
webview ŌåÆ pull  1
ext     ŌåÆ resolve 1  { ŌĆ” }
(silence ŌĆö no further webviewŌåöextension messages)
```

After that first `resolve`, **no further messages are delivered in either direction**, the SPA's "ready" gate never releases, and it stays on the loading screen indefinitely. No error is thrown; it just goes quiet.

### What narrows it down

- **Desktop VS Code / Cursor:** the exact same extension boots fully. (Desktop = local extension host + native Electron webview.)
- **A second remote-webview extension on the identical web/serverful setup works fully** ŌĆö but it uses **simple request/response** messaging, not sustained bidirectional RPC. So this isn't "remote webviews are broken"; it's specifically **continuous bidirectional webviewŌåöremote-extension delivery** that stalls after the first exchange.
- The editor-panel webview and the sidebar-view webview stall **identically**, which rules out the view-hosting layer and points at the message channel itself.

### Where it reproduces

- **code-server** (stock) ŌĆö reproduced
- **VSCodium-web** (stock) ŌĆö reproduced; symptom matches (webview never loads, page stays responsive)
- **@codingame/monaco-vscode-api** (bundles the same web workbench) ŌĆö original report with full traces: CodinGame/monaco-vscode-api#804

This was triaged with the monaco-vscode-api maintainer (@CGNonofr), who reproduced it on stock code-server and VSCodium-web and concluded the root cause is in VS Code's web layer rather than that library ŌĆö hence this upstream issue.

### Notes / caveats

- The concrete reproducer extension (`openai.chatgpt`) is **proprietary**, which is the main blocker for a clean minimal public sample. The behaviour is, however, reproducible by any webview extension that maintains a long-lived bidirectional message stream over the webview channel; happy to help build a minimal OSS reproducer (a tiny webview extension echoing a sustained capnweb-style stream) if that would help triage.
- Full captured webview message traces are available on CodinGame/monaco-vscode-api#804.

title: vscode snap fails to start podman due to database static dir mismatch
body:
Follow up to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/232863 which is locked.

## Issue
```
Error: database static dir "/home/angelo/snap/code/187/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" does not match our static dir "/home/angelo/snap/code/242/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod": database configuration mismatch
```

## Solution / workaround
#232863 was closed pointing to this: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/232863#issuecomment-2667750241, which references the user's own name.

I just wanted to share this snippet for my future self and others who'll face this issue:
```sh
cat <<EOF > ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
graphroot = "\$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
EOF
```

title: Ollama gemma model  Copilot adapter layer
body:
Type: Bug What Copilot is doing ŌĆó Copilot sends request to Ollama. ŌĆó Ollama returns a valid response (Beautiful). ŌĆó Copilot receives it. ŌĆó Copilot's internal validation rejects it and throws Response too long. ŌĆó But Copilot's adapter is logging: finish reason = length Suggest: Copilot is not using the exact same API path that your curl command is using. Most likely Copilot is: 1. Sending a different request format. 2. Adding its own max token settings. 3. Receiving a finish reason it interprets as length. 4. Throwing its internal exception. curl -> done_reason = stop Copilot -> finish reason = length [copilotLanguageModelWrapper] which suggests Copilot is wrapping the Ollama response before processing it. That makes it even more likely the bug is in Copilot's handling of Gemma's completion metadata rather than in Gemma itself. ŌĆó Copilot is sending a different request to Gemma than the one you're testing manually. ŌĆó Copilot is misinterpreting Gemma's streamed response format after receiving it. title: [NES] nextEditProvider.ts ŌĆö architectural/engineering cleanup
body:
# `nextEditProvider.ts` ŌĆö architectural/engineering cleanup

This issue tracks architectural, engineering, and implementation concerns identified during review of [`extensions/copilot/src/extension/inlineEdits/node/nextEditProvider.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/inlineEdits/node/nextEditProvider.ts) (~1500 LoC). None of these are blocking the recent telemetry-bookmark fix (#319879) ŌĆö captured here for follow-up.

## A. Architectural

1. **God class.** `NextEditProvider` does orchestration, debounce policy, caching glue, rebase coordination, speculative-request management, debug recording, and telemetry plumbing. Suggested extractions:
   - `PendingRequestRegistry` (the `_pendingStatelessNextEditRequest` slot + cancel-on-doc-change + cursor-in-window logic).
   - `RequestReuseStrategy` (the cascading reuse logic in `fetchNextEdit` ŌĆö pending ŌåÆ speculative ŌåÆ rebase ŌåÆ eager backup ŌåÆ second pending ŌåÆ new).
   - `OutcomeTracker` (`_lastShownTime`, `_lastRejectionTime`, `_lastShownSuggestionId`, `_lastOutcome`, `_lastNextEditResult`).
2. **No `RequestInvocation` value object.** `doc.value`, `doc.selection`, and the recording bookmark are read independently across `_getNextEditCanThrow`, `fetchNextEdit`, and `_executeNewNextEditRequest`. A single immutable snapshot (with an explicit `refresh()` API where "race to latest" is wanted, e.g. eager backup) would make the data flow obvious.
3. **`fetchNextEdit` is an unprincipled 5-way decision tree.** Replace with a discriminated `{ kind: 'reuse-as-is' | 'reuse-and-rebase' | 'rebase-with-backup' | 'fresh' }` strategy + single sink that handles each case.
4. **Mixed concerns in constructor.** `_cancelPendingRequestDueToDocChange` peeks into a sibling subsystem's private state (`_pendingStatelessNextEditRequest.getActiveDocument().documentAfterEdits`). Move the hook into the proposed registry.
5. **`InlineEditRequestLogContext` is itself a god object** ŌĆö uuid + recording bookmark + log entries + result sink + many flags. Split into a sink interface and a builder per request.

## B. Concurrency / correctness

6. **Detached background promise with no outer error reporting** (`(async () => { ŌĆ” editStream.next() ŌĆ” })()` in `_executeNewNextEditRequest`). If `handleStreamEnd` itself throws, rejection is swallowed.
7. **`_pendingStatelessNextEditRequest` slot juggling** is correct but extremely subtle (eager-backup nulls the slot temporarily; `removeFromPending` only nulls if identity matches; `_executeNewNextEditRequest` mid-call installs itself). Encapsulate behind the registry.
8. **`_cancelPendingRequestDueToDocChange` skips when `isAsyncCompletions`**, but `_executeNewNextEditRequest` installs an `autorunWithChanges` only in that same case. Two divergent-edit handlers, one disabled and one only active for the currently-executing request. Confusing.
9. **`startVersion = doc.value.get()` vs `documentAtInvocationTime`** ŌĆö read at different points with no intervening await, by definition identical. Alias or inline.
10. **Autorun mutates `nextEditRequest.intermediateUserEdit`** while the request has its own setter on the same field ŌĆö no synchronization story documented (relies on JS single-thread).
11. **`disp.dispose()` is called twice** (once in `handleStreamEnd`, once via the cancellation hookup's `toDisposable`). Idempotent, but non-obvious.

## C. Type-safety / API design

12. **`assertDefined`** at the bottom throws `BugIndicatingError`. Most callers would be better as `Result<ŌĆ”>` with structured logging.
13. **`StatelessNextEditRequest` constructor takes 13 positional params**, three of which are logging/telemetry. Group them.
14. **`as OffsetRange | undefined`** cast at line 729 violates the coding-guideline "no `as` casts without type predicates". Either the observable's typing is wrong or it can be `.at(0)`.
15. **GC-retention split brain:** `_lastShownSuggestionId` is intentionally just the requestId to avoid retaining the object, but `_lastNextEditResult` *does* retain the object. Pick one approach.
16. **`INextEditProvider` exposes mutable `lastTriggerTime`/`lastRejectionTime`/`lastOutcome`.** Split into `INextEditProviderOps` + `INextEditProviderObservables`.

## D. Performance / waste

17. **`historyContext.documents.map(this._processDoc)` runs twice** (`_executeNewNextEditRequest` + `_createSpeculativeRequest`) per acceptance. Each `_processDoc` allocates non-trivially. Memoize on `historyContext.version` / `doc.lastEdit` identity.
18. **`JSON.stringify(entries)` to size-check recording** in `LlmNESTelemetryBuilder.build` runs on every NES request that builds an alternative-action telemetry entry; recording can be megabytes. Use entry-count heuristic, stringify only above threshold.
19. **`DebugRecorder.getRecentLog()` called twice** in the new-request path (once in `_executeNewNextEditRequest`, again when assembling alternative action). Cache for request lifetime.
20. **Recording entries serialized regardless of telemetry sampling** ŌĆö gate on sampling decision.

## E. Smaller things

21. `assertType(suggestion.result, '@ulugbekna: undefined edit cannot be rejected?')` ŌĆö code-review handles shouldn't ship in error messages.
22. Long-standing `// FIXME@ulugbekna: implement advanced expansion` in `_createSpeculativeRequest` without an issue link.
23. `// FIXME: don't invoke before fixing false positive cancellations` with a commented-out `_specManager.onActiveDocumentChanged` call. Either fix or delete.
24. Conversational comments (e.g. `// Nice! ŌĆ”`) ŌĆö repo style asks for the *why*, not commentary.
25. `logger.trace(...)` is the only observability for many decision branches. Structured events (e.g. `emit({ kind: 'rebaseFailed', requestId })`) would feed both debug recorder and telemetry naturally.
26. `createDocStateLookupMap` `throw new BugIndicatingError()` without a message.
27. `computeReducedWindow` magic numbers (1, 2) and no unit tests despite tight cache-invalidation semantics.
28. `convertLineEditToEdit` papers over `LineReplacement.toSingleTextEdit` always emitting `\n`. Fix upstream by parametrising on line-ending.
29. Test coverage for the orchestration (reuse cascade, speculative state machine, eager-backup race) is effectively zero.

## Suggested order

| ROI | Effort | Item |
|---|---|---|
| High | Low | (12) `Result` instead of `assertDefined` for known-failure cases. |
| High | Low | (3) Extract `RequestReuseStrategy` enum + single sink in `fetchNextEdit`. Parity refactor. |
| High | Med | (2) Introduce `RequestInvocation` snapshot + explicit `refresh()` for eager backup. |
| High | Med | (5)/(13) Split `InlineEditRequestLogContext`; group telemetry args. |
| Med | Low | (17)/(18)/(19) Memoize per-request work. |
| Med | Med | (1) Extract `PendingRequestRegistry`, `OutcomeTracker`. |
| Med | High | Add unit tests around `fetchNextEdit` reuse cascade. |
| Low | Low | (21)-(26) Comment / message hygiene. |

Recommended starting point: (3) + (12) ŌĆö both parity-preserving and mechanical, and they make the rest of the file much easier to reason about.

title: Support opening Agents sessions in separate windows
body:
It should be possible to open an Agents session in a separate Agents window, not only side-by-side within the current Agents window.

This enables users to keep multiple sessions visible as independent full Agents windows, each with its own sessions list and layout.

Expected:
- Right-clicking a session supports opening it in a new Agents window.
- Dragging a session outside the Agents window opens it in a new Agents window.

title: Add a configurable delay for inline suggestions
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
I would like the Copilot Inline Suggestions to have an optional configurable delay before requesting an inline suggestion generation after the user stops typing.
I thought the "Eagerness" setting would affect this, but it does not.

The suggestions are normally good if the task is easy - but if you're not sure yet about exactly what you want to do, suggestions pop up too soon, and often with irrelevant content, which gets very distracting.
I don't like using "Snooze" in this case, because I would still like to have shorter, one-line suggestions, but not too fast.

If I could set, for example, a 2 second delay after I stop typing before getting the suggestion, I feel it could fix the problem.
(also, possibly, making "Low" eagerness even less eager, like not adding more lines of code while typing just a single line? I haven't felt a difference between "High" and "Low" eagerness...)

title: [agent-sandbox] Regression: allowRead paths denied due to symlink resolution on macOS
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->

**Disclaimer:** part of this issue report has been prepared with the help of AI (Github Copilot/Claude Opus), however I have verified the logic and content behind it

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Untested/irrelevant

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0 (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8)
- OS Version: macOS 26.5 (Darwin arm64 25F71)

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Have symlinked git dotfiles (a common pattern for dotfile managers):
   ```
   ~/.gitconfig -> ~/.dotfiles/git/gitconfig.symlink
   ~/.gitaliases -> ~/.dotfiles/git/gitaliases.symlink
   ```
   Where `~/.gitconfig` contains `[include] path = ~/.gitaliases`.

2. Configure `chat.agent.sandbox.fileSystem.mac` to allow both the source and target symlink paths:
   ```json
   "chat.agent.sandbox.fileSystem.mac": {
     "denyRead": ["**/.env*"],
     "allowRead": [
       ".",
       "/Users/me/.gitaliases",
       "/Users/me/.gitconfig",
       "/Users/me/.dotfiles/**"
     ]
   }
   ```
   (technically only the source should be required after #317198)

3. Enable the sandbox (`"chat.agent.sandbox.enabled": "on"`).

4. Ask the agent to run `git status` (or otherwise read those symlinked files on the allow list) in the terminal.

**Expected:** git succeeds ŌĆö `~/.gitaliases` and `~/.gitconfig` are explicitly in `allowRead`.

**Actual:** git fails with:
```
fatal: unable to access '/Users/me/.gitaliases': Operation not permitted
```

**Suggested root cause ŌĆö regression from #317198:**

PR #317198 ("Handle sandbox path resolution across platforms") resolves symlinks via `realpath` and **replaces** the configured path with the canonical target.

The generated sandbox settings JSON then contains only the resolved target instead of the configured symlink paths (`~/.gitaliases`, `~/.gitconfig`):
```json
"allowRead": [
    "/Users/me/.dotfiles/git/gitaliases.symlink",
    "/Users/me/.dotfiles/git/gitconfig.symlink"
]
```

macOS Seatbelt (used by the sandbox) evaluates rules against the **path as passed to the syscall**, not the resolved vnode. When git calls `open("/Users/me/.gitaliases")`, the sandbox checks:

1. `(deny file-read* (subpath "/Users/me"))` ŌĆö matches ŌåÆ deny
2. `(allow file-read* (subpath "/Users/me/.dotfiles/git/gitaliases.symlink"))` ŌĆö does NOT match the opened path ŌåÆ no effect
3. Last matching rule ŌåÆ **denied**

**Direct reproduction using the packaged sandbox runtime:**

```bash
# Create test settings with ONLY the resolved target in allowRead
cat > /tmp/sandbox-test.json << 'EOF'
{
  "network": {"allowedDomains": [], "deniedDomains": []},
  "filesystem": {
    "denyRead": ["/Users/me"],
    "allowRead": ["/Users/me/.dotfiles/git/gitaliases.symlink", "/Users/me/Code/myrepo"],
    "allowWrite": ["/Users/me/Code/myrepo"], "denyWrite": []
  }
}
EOF

# Target path: ALLOWED
ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 Code cli.js --settings /tmp/sandbox-test.json \
  -c 'cat ~/.dotfiles/git/gitaliases.symlink >/dev/null && echo OK || echo DENIED'
# ŌåÆ OK

# Symlink path: DENIED (this is the bug)
ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 Code cli.js --settings /tmp/sandbox-test.json \
  -c 'cat ~/.gitaliases >/dev/null && echo OK || echo DENIED'
# ŌåÆ cat: /Users/me/.gitaliases: Operation not permitted
# ŌåÆ DENIED

# With BOTH paths in allowRead: ALLOWED (this is the fix)
# Add "/Users/me/.gitaliases" to allowRead ŌåÆ works
```

title: Agent tool `read/problems` on Windows is sensitive to drive letter case. Not stable enough.
body:
### Problem
On Windows, when Copilot agent uses the `read/problems` tool, the file path generated by the agent uses a lowercase drive letter (e.g., `c:\`). As a result, the tool fails to find problems for the file. If I change the drive letter to uppercase (e.g., `C:\`), the tool works as expected and finds problems.

### Expected behavior
The file object should be validated and normalized (e.g., standardize to uppercase drive letter or otherwise) within the tools layer. The agent should not have to care about drive letter case. Ideally, the agent's output would not need to adjust for this platform-specific detail.

### Steps to reproduce
1. On Windows, run an agent query that uses `read/problems` with a path like `c:\path\to\file`
2. The tool reports "no problems found"
3. Change the path to `C:\path\to\file` and rerun ŌĆō now problems are correctly found

### Suggestion
- Tools layer should handle drive letter normalization and validation, so that agents are not required to output platform-specific casing.
- Agent should not need to care about drive letter casing when generating tool input.

### Additional info
- VS Code version: 1.123.0 (latest up to now)
- OS: Windows 11 Pro
- Copilot/Agent version: latest

**Labels:** `bug`, `agent-skills`, `agents`, `agent-behavior`

title: Default Approvals do not prompt for subsequent send_to_terminal inputs after starting an interactive shell, allowing destructive commands without confirmation
body:
OS: Windows
VS Code: 1.123.0
Commit: 6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8
Copilot Chat version from debug log: 0.51.0
Permission level: Default Approvals

Summary
When using Copilot Chat Agent with Default Approvals, direct terminal commands correctly require confirmation for every tool invocation . However, after approving the launch of an interactive shell (run_in_terminal: pwsh -NoLogo), subsequent **send_to_terminal** inputs into that shell run without additional confirmation, including destructive commands such as Remove-Item.

Steps to Reproduce
1. In Copilot Chat Agent, set permission level to Default Approvals.
2. Ask the agent to start an interactive PowerShell shell: pwsh -NoLogo
Confirm the pwsh -NoLogo tool invocation when prompted.
3. Ask/send the following input into that interactive shell: 
  New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path .\copilot-approval-repro-20260604
4. Ask/send the following input into the same shell: 
  Remove-Item -LiteralPath .\copilot-approval-repro-20260604 -Force


Actual Behavior
Only the initial pwsh -NoLogo command required confirmation. The subsequent New-Item and Remove-Item inputs sent via send_to_terminal ran without explicit confirmation. In particular, Remove-Item deleted the test directory without a fresh approval prompt. The same behaviour happens for example in context of ssh user@ssh-server.

Expected Behavior
Under Default Approvals and interactive terminal shells, each send_to_terminal invocation should require confirmation unless explicitly auto-approved by user settings. It should not be implicitly covered by approving the startup of an interactive shell.

Control Test
Running the same operations outside an interactive shell as separate terminal tool invocations does require confirmation each time (probably because each one is called wih "run_in_terminal" instead of "send_to_terminal":
  Test-Path -LiteralPath .\copilot-approval-direct-20260604
  New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path .\copilot-approval-direct-20260604
  Remove-Item -LiteralPath .\copilot-approval-direct-20260604 -Force
Observed result: the user had to confirm all three direct terminal invocations.

title: Terminal copy corrupts UTF-8 (Japanese/CJK/emoji) text via OSC 52 round-trip in 1.123 (atob in @xterm/addon-clipboard)
body:
# Terminal copy mojibakes UTF-8 (Japanese/CJK/emoji) text via OSC 52 round-trip in 1.123

## TL;DR

In **VS Code 1.123.0** the integrated terminal pipes the user's selection through an **OSC 52 round-trip** that is then decoded by `@xterm/addon-clipboard@0.3.0-beta.220`. That addon's `Base64.decodeText` calls **raw `atob`** and pushes the resulting binary string straight to `navigator.clipboard.writeText`, so every UTF-8 byte becomes a separate Latin-1 character on the system clipboard. Any non-ASCII selection (Japanese, CJK, Cyrillic, emoji, ...) is corrupted in every paste target (Notepad, browsers, `Get-Clipboard`).

`Base64.encodeText` has the symmetrical bug (raw `btoa` rejects U+0080+ characters or silently misencodes them depending on browser).

## Environment

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| VS Code version | 1.123.0 |
| Commit | `6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8` |
| Build date | 2026-06-03 |
| OS | Windows 11 |
| Affected file | `resources/app/node_modules/@xterm/addon-clipboard/lib/addon-clipboard.js` |
| Bundled `@xterm/xterm` | `6.1.0-beta.220` |
| Bundled `@xterm/addon-clipboard` | `0.3.0-beta.220` |
| Regression introduced | this 1.123 build (1.122 was not affected on the same machine) |

## Reproduction

1. Open the integrated terminal (default profile, default settings; `terminal.integrated.copyOnSelection` is its default `false`).
2. Run `echo 'ŃüōŃéōŃü½ŃüĪŃü» ÕłźŃü«µ¢░Ķ”ÅŃé┐Ńā╝Ńā¤ŃāŖŃā½'` (or any line containing non-ASCII text).
3. Mouse-select the printed Japanese text.
4. Press `Ctrl+C`.
5. Paste into Notepad (or run `Get-Clipboard` from a separate PowerShell window, or paste into a browser address bar).

### Expected

Clipboard contains `ŃüōŃéōŃü½ŃüĪŃü» ÕłźŃü«µ¢░Ķ”ÅŃé┐Ńā╝Ńā¤ŃāŖŃā½` exactly.

### Actual

Clipboard contains `├Ż├Ż├Ż├Ż┬½├Ż┬Ī├Ż┬» ├ź┬ź├Ż┬«├”┬░├©┬”├Ż┬┐├Ż┬╝├Ż├Ż┬½` (each UTF-8 byte mapped 1:1 to a U+0080ŌĆōU+00FF code unit).

## Bit-level evidence

Selected text: `ŃāŖŃā½Ńü¦µŚźµ£¼Ķ¬×ŃéÆ` (7 code points).

UTF-8 bytes of selection: `E3 83 8A E3 83 AB E3 81 A7 E6 97 A5 E6 9C AC E8 AA 9E E3 82 92` (21 bytes).

Base64 encoding of those bytes: `44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS`.

What VS Code's terminal writes to PTY (as OSC 52 echo): `ESC ] 52 ; c ; 44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS ST`.

After PTY echo, `ClipboardAddon._setOrReportClipboard` receives `"c;44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS"`, calls `Base64.decodeText("44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS")`, and the **buggy** `decodeText` returns the 21-character JS string

```
U+00E3 U+0083 U+008A  U+00E3 U+0083 U+00AB  U+00E3 U+0081 U+00A7  U+00E6 U+0097 U+00A5
U+00E6 U+009C U+00AC  U+00E8 U+00AA U+009E  U+00E3 U+0082 U+0092
```

which is the UTF-8 byte stream zero-extended into UTF-16 code units. `navigator.clipboard.writeText` then writes this corrupted JS string as `CF_UNICODETEXT` and every paste consumer sees mojibake.

## Buggy code

`resources/app/node_modules/@xterm/addon-clipboard/lib/addon-clipboard.js` (un-minified):

```js
class Base64 {
  encodeText(e) { return btoa(e); }                       // (1)
  decodeText(e) { try { return atob(e); } catch {} return ""; }  // (2)
}
```

- **(1)** `btoa` is defined only over characters in the Latin-1 range. With any U+0080+ code unit it either throws `InvalidCharacterError` or silently emits wrong base64 (browser-dependent).
- **(2)** `atob` returns a "binary string" ŌĆö each character is a byte in U+0000ŌĆōU+00FF. For UTF-8-encoded payloads the result is the raw byte stream, **not** the decoded text. Passing this to `navigator.clipboard.writeText` (which expects a regular UTF-16 JS string) writes 1 code unit per UTF-8 byte to `CF_UNICODETEXT`.

## Call path (proven via DevTools instrumentation)

`Ctrl+C` does **not** go through any direct renderer-side clipboard call. It is routed via OSC 52:

```
xterm._customKeyEventHandler returns true (no VS Code keybinding for plain Ctrl+C)
  ŌåÆ xterm Ctrl+C handling encodes the selection as OSC 52 and writes it to PTY
  ŌåÆ PTY echoes the OSC 52 back as input
  ŌåÆ xterm parser dispatches OSC 52 handler
  ŌåÆ ClipboardAddon._setOrReportClipboard(data)
  ŌåÆ Base64.decodeText(b64)               // <-- CORRUPTION HAPPENS HERE
  ŌåÆ BrowserClipboardProvider.writeText
  ŌåÆ navigator.clipboard.writeText        // writes corrupted JS string
```

Live stack trace captured by hooking `Base64.decodeText` (top frame is the hook):

```
addon[9]._base64.decodeText
  in[28] = "44OK44Or44Gn5pel5pys6Kqe44KS"
  out[21] = "├Ż├Ż┬½├Ż┬¦├”┬ź├”┬¼├©┬¬├Ż"
  out cp:  U+00E3 U+0083 U+008A U+00E3 U+0083 U+00AB ...

addon[9]._setOrReportClipboard
  ŌåÉ addon-clipboard.js:1 (OSC handler)
  ŌåÉ xterm.js end ŌåÆ parse ŌåÆ _innerWrite ŌåÆ write
  ŌåÉ _writeProcessData @ terminalInstance.ts:1682
  ŌåÉ _onProcessData    @ terminalInstance.ts:1675
  ŌåÉ TerminalProcessManager fire
  ŌåÉ LocalTerminalBackend handleData
  ŌåÉ ipc.ts onResponse / onBuffer
```

`navigator.clipboard.writeText` and `_clipboardService.writeText` on every reachable object (DOM walk over `.terminal-wrapper` expandos, `xterm._addonManager._addons[*].instance`, `vscode.ipcRenderer.{send,invoke}`, `vscode.ipcMessagePort.acquire`, `MessageChannel.prototype.postMessage`, `MessagePort.prototype.postMessage`, `document.execCommand('copy')`, document/window `copy` events at capture phase) were all instrumented; **only the OSC-52 path fires on `Ctrl+C`**.

## Fix

Replace the `Base64` class in `addon-clipboard.js` (and in the upstream `@xterm/addon-clipboard` source) with UTF-8-aware encode/decode:

```js
class Base64 {
  encodeText(text) {
    const bytes = new TextEncoder().encode(text);
    let bin = "";
    for (let i = 0; i < bytes.length; i++) bin += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]);
    return btoa(bin);
  }
  decodeText(b64) {
    try {
      const bin = atob(b64);
      const bytes = new Uint8Array(bin.length);
      for (let i = 0; i < bin.length; i++) bytes[i] = bin.charCodeAt(i);
      return new TextDecoder("utf-8").decode(bytes);
    } catch {}
    return "";
  }
}
```

The pre-allocated `Uint8Array` + index assignment avoids the `Function.prototype.apply` argument-count limit (~65k) that `String.fromCharCode(...bytes)` / `btoa(String.fromCharCode(...bytes))` would hit on large selections.

This restores parity with the OSC 52 spec, every other terminal emulator (xterm, iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal, Alacritty, foot, ...), and what xterm.js itself does in 5.x prior to the addon refactor.

Verified locally by patching `addon-clipboard.js` in place: after restart, copying `ŃüōŃéōŃü½ŃüĪŃü» ÕłźŃü«µ¢░Ķ”ÅŃé┐Ńā╝Ńā¤ŃāŖŃā½` from the integrated terminal yields the exact same string on the clipboard.

## Related notes

- `terminal.integrated.copyOnSelection` is irrelevant; the bug fires on `Ctrl+C` only because the copy goes through OSC 52 and `decodeText`.
- The pre-1.123 path apparently used a direct `IClipboardService.writeText(this.raw.getSelection())` (the in-memory xterm string was a real UTF-16 JS string and was not subject to base64 round-trip), so this is a clean regression introduced by the OSC-52 round-trip refactor.
- `Base64.encodeText` is exercised by the dual OSC 52 "read clipboard" path (`OSC 52 ; c ; ? ST`), so applications that request the clipboard from inside the terminal will also see corruption on any non-ASCII content stored in the system clipboard.
- The bundled `@xterm/addon-clipboard` is at version `0.3.0-beta.220`. If the bug is fixed in a later beta or in `0.3.0` final, the fix is to roll forward the bundled dependency.

## How this affects users today

Any developer whose primary language uses non-ASCII characters ŌĆö Japanese, Chinese, Korean, Russian, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, emoji, accented Latin ŌĆö cannot reliably copy terminal output out of VS Code 1.123. The display in the terminal is correct so the regression is silent: the user only discovers it after pasting somewhere else.

---

Filed by: junggeunkim (issue text generated with assistance from GitHub Copilot CLI based on local reproduction and live DevTools instrumentation)

title: JS/TS crash notification should suggest checking TypeScript version after accepting workspace TS prompt
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.123.0
- OS Version: macOS Darwin x64 25.4.0
- Workspace TypeScript Version: 4.9.5
- VS Code Bundled TypeScript Version: 6.0.3
- Framework: Angular 15.2.10
- Workspace setting:

```json
{
  "typescript.tsdk": "node_modules/typescript/lib",
  "typescript.enablePromptUseWorkspaceTsdk": true
}
```

## Summary

VS Code initially used its bundled TypeScript version and showed a prompt asking whether to use the workspace TypeScript version.

After accepting that prompt, VS Code switched to the workspace TypeScript SDK:

```text
node_modules/typescript/lib
```

In this workspace, that resolves to TypeScript 4.9.5.

Immediately after switching, the JS/TS language service became unusable and crashed repeatedly. The final crash notification suggested that the problem may be caused by a plugin contributed by `GitHub.copilot-chat`.

That guidance was misleading in this case:

- disabling `GitHub.copilot-chat` did not fix the issue
- reproducing with all installed extensions disabled still crashes when workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 is selected
- switching back to `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version -> Use VS Code's Version` fixes the issue

This issue is about VS Code's crash recovery guidance, not about claiming that Copilot caused the crash.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace with TypeScript 4.9.5 installed locally.
2. Open a TypeScript file.
3. Accept VS Code's prompt to use the workspace TypeScript version.
4. Confirm that TS Server is loaded from:

```text
/path/to/workspace/node_modules/typescript/lib/tsserver.js
```

5. Observe the JS/TS language service crash repeatedly.
6. Disable `GitHub.copilot-chat` and reload.
7. Observe that the crash still happens.
8. Launch VS Code with installed extensions disabled and force the same workspace TypeScript SDK.
9. Observe that the crash still happens.
10. Switch to `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version -> Use VS Code's Version`.
11. Observe that the JS/TS language service works.

## Actual Result

The JS/TS language service crashes repeatedly and eventually stops restarting.

The notification points to a contributed plugin:

```text
The JS/TS language service crashed 5 times immediately.
The service will not be restarted.
This may be caused by a plugin contributed by one of these extensions:
GitHub.copilot-chat
```

However, the crash also reproduces with installed extensions disabled when the active TS Server is workspace TypeScript 4.9.5.

Crash log excerpt from the extension-disabled reproduction:

```text
Using tsserver from: /path/to/workspace/node_modules/typescript/lib/tsserver.js
Version: 4.9.5
A non-recoverable error occurred while executing tsserver command: updateOpen
Error processing request. Debug Failure. False expression: position cannot precede the beginning of the file
TSServer exited. Code: null. Signal: SIGTERM
```

Relevant stack frames:

```text
computeLineOfPosition
computeLineAndCharacterOfPosition
formatDiagnosticToProtocol
ProjectService.sendConfigFileDiagEvent
ProjectService.assignProjectToOpenedScriptInfo
ProjectService.applyChangesInOpenFiles
updateOpen
```

## Expected Result

When the JS/TS language service crashes shortly after the user accepts the workspace TypeScript version prompt, the crash notification should include TypeScript SDK/version selection in the recovery guidance.

For example, it could suggest:

- the active TS Server is running from `node_modules/typescript/lib`
- the selected workspace TypeScript version may be old or incompatible
- try `TypeScript: Select TypeScript Version -> Use VS Code's Version`
- check the `typescript.tsdk` setting

The notification may still mention contributed plugins when relevant, but it should not only point the user toward extensions when the selected workspace TypeScript version is a strong recovery candidate.

## Reproduction Matrix

| Mode | Active TypeScript | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Normal extensions | Workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 | Crashes repeatedly |
| Copilot Chat disabled | Workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 | Still crashes |
| Installed extensions disabled | Workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 | Still crashes |
| Installed extensions disabled | VS Code bundled TypeScript 6.0.3 | Works |
| Normal extensions | VS Code bundled TypeScript 6.0.3 | Works |

## How I Verified the Extension-Disabled Cases

I used isolated VS Code profiles so the normal profile state would not affect the result.

Workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 reproduction settings:

```json
{
  "typescript.tsserver.log": "verbose",
  "typescript.tsserver.trace": "messages",
  "typescript.tsdk": "/path/to/workspace/node_modules/typescript/lib",
  "security.workspace.trust.enabled": false
}
```

Bundled TypeScript control settings:

```json
{
  "typescript.tsserver.log": "verbose",
  "typescript.tsserver.trace": "messages",
  "security.workspace.trust.enabled": false
}
```

The extension-disabled workspace TypeScript run logged:

```text
Using tsserver from: /path/to/workspace/node_modules/typescript/lib/tsserver.js
Info 1 [time] Version: 4.9.5
```

and then failed on `updateOpen`.

The extension-disabled bundled TypeScript run logged:

```text
Using tsserver from: /Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/node_modules/typescript/lib/tsserver.js
Info 1 [time] Version: 6.0.3
```

and `updateOpen` completed successfully without `Debug Failure` or repeated `TSServer exited` messages.

## Why this belongs in microsoft/vscode

The underlying `Debug Failure` may be a TypeScript 4.9.5 server bug.

However, the VS Code user experience is the issue here:

1. VS Code prompted me to switch to the workspace TypeScript version.
2. After accepting that prompt, IntelliSense / JS/TS language service became unusable.
3. The crash notification pointed me toward Copilot/plugin troubleshooting.
4. The actual recovery path was switching back to VS Code's bundled TypeScript version.

So the request is for better crash diagnostics and recovery guidance in VS Code.

## Additional Notes

In the extension-disabled reproduction, the TS Server arguments did not include Copilot/global TypeScript plugins. The workspace TypeScript 4.9.5 server still failed on `updateOpen`.

I can provide redacted TS Server logs if needed. The raw logs include local workspace paths and may include source-related details.

title: Feature request: Panel-specific themes in VS Code / Copilot
body:
Saat ini workbench.colorTheme hanya bisa diterapkan secara global. Saya ingin usulan fitur agar setiap panel bisa punya tema sendiri, misalnya:

panel Explorer dengan tema terang
panel Chat dengan tema gelap
editor kode dengan tema lain

Masalah
Tema global membuat semua panel ikut sama
Kadang ingin area chat lebih nyaman untuk baca teks panjang
Editor dan file explorer bisa punya preferensi warna berbeda
Ini penting untuk workflow yang butuh fokus di satu panel tanpa mengganggu yang lain

Manfaat
Fleksibilitas UI lebih tinggi
Pengguna bisa menyesuaikan tema berdasarkan fungsi panel
Meningkatkan kenyamanan, misalnya chat gelap untuk mengurangi silau, editor terang untuk debugging
Cocok untuk setup multi-tasking dan display tinggi

Usulan implementasi
workbench.panelThemes
atau opsi themeScope per panel

contoh:
workbench.colorTheme: default global
workbench.panelThemes.chat: Dark+
workbench.panelThemes.explorer: Light+
workbench.panelThemes.editor: Default Dark+

Contoh kasus
Chat = tema gelap untuk pengalaman membaca yang lebih lembut
Explorer = tema terang agar file/folder terlihat jelas
Editor = tema standar development yang konsisten

Fitur ini akan sangat membantu pengguna yang bekerja dengan banyak konteks sekaligus, terutama ketika ruang chat perlu tampil berbeda dari editor utama.

body: Lots of errors in console window about customizations files not being found Just open insiders and I have lot of these errors

title: Empty Customizations in VS Code Client for Agent Host and later its not empty
body:
Easy repro in OSS.

Something isn't right in customizations
Customizations are returned by the Agent Host, 
However randomly the list goes empty (no agents, no skills) and then after a few seconds we see the items,
& wait a little longer, it goes empty again
Got to another session and back, then its empty again & after some time its full again

This seems to be a very recent regression
Basically opening an existing session will yield an empty Agent picker

This seems to repro in insiders as well, but more so in OSS.

title: Chat: Autopilot: Agent/Plan modes refuse to wait for answers
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.41 - VS Code Version: 1.122.1 - OS Version: Win 11 Pro - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent/Plan modes - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Opus 4.8, 4.7, Sonnet 4.6 - Logs: Steps to Reproduce: 1. Use Autopilot 2. Wait for questions, watch hopelessly as no wait happens 3. Gasp in awe as the Autopilot then immediately slams "Start implementation" (a separate bug) into the chat, wasting yet more tokens and breaking flow, and requiring hard guardrails to prevent a constant loop of approvals without stopping for expressly requested feedback. Work autonomously and make good decisions. ---

title: [Remote-SSH] Extension icons/fonts (SVG/WOFF2) blocked by CORS policy after recent update (vscode-managed-remote-resource)
body:
Type: Bug ### Bug Description After updating to the latest version of VS Code, many extension icons and fonts (e.g., CMake Tools, Todo Tree, GitLens, Gerrit) are completely missing when working in a Remote-SSH workspace. Checking the Developer Tools console reveals that the underlying `vscode-managed-remote-resource://` protocol is being blocked by a strict CORS policy. This appears to be a regression bug related to webview or remote resource security policies. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Update VS Code to the latest version. 2. Connect to a remote Linux machine using the `Remote - SSH` extension. 3. Open a remote project. 4. Observe the Activity Bar and Editor UI: specific extension icons (like Todo Tree, CMake) are missing or broken. 5. Open Developer Tools (`Cmd+Option+I`) and look at the Console. ### Logs / Error Messages Here are the exact errors from the DevTools console: Access to image at 'vscode-managed-remote-resource://window:1/home/.../.vscode-server/extensions/gruntfuggly.todo-tree-0.0.226/resources/todo-tree-container.svg?authority=ssh-remote%2Bdev&scheme=vscode-remote' from origin 'vscode-file://vscode-app' has been blocked by CORS policy: Cross origin requests are only supported for protocol schemes: chrome, chrome-extension, chrome-untrusted, data, http, https, vscode-file, vscode-remote-resource, vscode-webview. Failed to load resource: net::ERR_FAILED Access to font at 'vscode-managed-remote-resource://window:1/home/.../.vscode-server/extensions/ms-vscode.cmake-tools-1.23.52/res/product-icons.woff2?authority=ssh-remote%2Bdev&scheme=vscode-remote' from origin 'vscode-file://vscode-app' has been blocked by CORS policy. ### Environment * **Local OS:** macOS * **Remote OS:** Linux (SSH) * **Extensions affected:** Todo Tree, CMake Tools, GitLens, Gerrit, etc. VS Code version: Code 1.123.0 (6a44c352bd24569c417e530095901b649960f9f8, 2026-06-03T11:29:03+02:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 5.4.0-216-generic

title: Terminal detail popup missing vertical scrollbar, bottom content & hidden buttons cannot be clicked
body: ### Steps to reproduce 1. Install extensions: GitHub Copilot Chat, Python, Python Debugger and Git in VS Code. 2. Open a new PowerShell(pwsh) terminal, these extensions will inject their environment config into terminal info. 3. Click the info icon on a terminal entry to open its detail popup window. ### Actual result The popup content overflows the container height, **no vertical scroll bar appears**. Bottom content and underlying action buttons are cropped and unreachable, can not view or click hidden items. ### Expected result Add vertical scroll(overflow-y:auto) for this terminal detail panel. When content exceeds panel max height, scrollbar shows up to browse full content and access bottom buttons normally. ### Additional info OS: Windows Shell: Windows PowerShell v1.0 / pwsh Trigger reason: Multiple extensions add extra items to the terminal extension contribution list, making content longer than popup default height.
