title: Copilot CLI sessions don't appear in the Agents window session list
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Searched: no exact match found. Closest is #318632 (Import Session feature request) -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest (pre-release)
- VS Code Version: 1.121.0
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agents window / session list
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6
- Logs: N/A

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a terminal and start a Copilot CLI session: `copilot`
2. Interact with the session (e.g. create a file)
3. Open the VS Code Agents window (the new dedicated agents app)
4. Look at the Sessions sidebar

**Expected:** The CLI session appears in the Agents window session list
**Actual:** Only sessions created from within the Agents window appear. The CLI session is invisible.

## Additional Context

- The same CLI session *does* appear in the regular VS Code Chat "Sessions" panel
- Both the CLI and the Agents window operate on the same repository/workspace
- Even after the CLI session makes file changes, it still doesn't appear in the Agents window
- This creates a fragmented experience - I start work in the CLI, then want to continue or review it in the Agents window, but I can't find my session

title: GHCP on VS Code vs VS Enterprise - Where Do We Send Our Refund Requests for EAs?
body: Can someone **officially** provide justification as to why GHCP is grossly lagging behind VS Code, particularly when one IDE is free, and the other is thousands of dollars a year? I'm not sure if Microsoft is intentionally trying to rage bait people, but this about did it for me today: Seriously? These are so grossly off that it's nearly impossible to use VS Enterprise, which I prefer for some of the other capabilities, but MSFT has made this an untenable mess where I spend my day in two IDEs on the same body of work. That is not acceptable. I would appreciate a response to this - and a direct one is fine. If one is not received by end of week (6/5), I will be moving to disallow VS Enterprise in the org and force those people to VS Code. The lack of product management aptitude here is beyond astounding.

title: Agents view: session resume fails with "Error during execution" when no git repo
body:
## Bug Description

When resuming a CLI-created Claude Code session from the Agents view, the session fails with:

```
Error: Error during execution
```

The underlying SDK error is:
```
No conversation found with session ID: <uuid>
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Claude Code session via CLI in a directory without a git repository (e.g., `C:\Users\username`)
2. Open VS Code Insiders (no workspace open)
3. Open the Agents view ŌĆö the CLI session appears in the session list
4. Click on the session and send a message (e.g., "hi")
5. **Result**: "Error: Error during execution" is displayed

## Root Cause

When the session has no associated git repository, `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` creates a fallback workspace folder with URI `unknown:///` ([line 1266](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/contrib/providers/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts#L1266)). `WorkspaceFolderManagementContribution` then registers this as a real workspace folder.

In the Copilot extension, `getFolderInfoForSession()` sees one workspace folder and uses its `fsPath` as the Claude SDK `cwd`. On Windows, `unknown:///` resolves to `fsPath = "\"`. The SDK uses this invalid `cwd` to locate the session file on disk, looking in `~/.claude/projects/\-/` instead of the correct project directory.

## Environment

- VS Code Insiders 1.122.0
- Windows 11
- Claude Code CLI sessions created via Agent Maestro proxy

## Fix

Filter `getWorkspaceFolders()` to only include `file://` scheme URIs in `getFolderInfoForSession()`, so virtual/unknown workspace folders are excluded. See PR.

title: Add a reload file menu option, and keyboard shortcut
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
If I am co-editing a file with an AI tool, I would like to be able to reload that file from disk when the tool has updated it. The is no option within the File submenu to do that at present. It would also be helpful to have a keyboard shortcut to do this, e.g. cmd + R on Mac.

title: Tab shifting goes to unexpected element in integrated browser
body: Testing #319071 1. Open integrated browser 2. Make sure you have a few favorite urls 3. Search for something and make sure you have multiple elements in the picker 4. Hit tab and trying to navigate through those elements 5. ­¤Éø Tab starts navigating through VS Code components instead

title: Copilot is consuming 51% of monthly budget with just 2 simple prompts...
body: This after one prompt asking to debug (&plan to fix) a communication issue, and a short prompt asking to implement the planned fix. And this is not even a large codebase, this is a relatively simple embedded project with just 10k SLOC (and of that only 2% were relevant to the given query!)! How is anyone seriously supposed to use Github Copilot if essentially one prompt consumes more than 50% of a month's budget?? Copilot WAS once great and delivered better prompt performance with the same models. Does not help if the limits make the entire tool utterly useless, does it?

title: Rubber Duck setting should force relaunch of application
body:
Testing https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319092

In the current state of the rubber duck setting it only works after fully restarting the application. In that case we should prompt the user to restart the window when they change the setting. We do this for other settings too like `window.titleBarStyle`.
This can be implemented in `\vs\workbench\contrib\relauncher\browser\relauncher.contribution.ts`

title: Agents Window chat input history is shared across sessions
body:
When using the Agents Window with multiple sessions open, chat input history is shared across sessions.

## Repro

1. Open the Agents Window.
2. Open or create two different sessions.
3. Send a few prompts in the first session.
4. Switch to the second session and send different prompts there.
5. Press Up Arrow in the chat input to navigate previous prompts.

## Actual

The input history can include prompts from other sessions. For example, pressing Up Arrow in session B can show prompts that were sent in session A.

## Expected

Chat input history should be scoped to the current session. Pressing Up/Down should only navigate prompts submitted in that same session.

## Demo

I have a video below showing the shared-history behavior. (The sessions were in two different folders)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49f2c394-5709-4088-8e0f-ef581c62254c

title: Rubber Duck setting needs to be an application scoped setting (with current implementation)
body:
Testing https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319092

`chat.agentHost.rubberDuck.enabled` is currently not implemented as a application scoped setting. The agent host will have the rubber duck feature enabled for all windows or none as there is only one agent host and not one per window so the setting can not be scoped by profile but has to be per application. If we think about remote agent host then even application scoped will not work.

I wonder why the rubber duck enablement needs to be set when the agent host starts and can not be sent with the request. It seems to me that this approach of specifying all settings when the agent host starts will cause a ton of issues long term. It will not allow for profile based settings and when it comes to remote agent host will complicate things even more. 

cc @roblourens @connor4312

title: Agent discovery scans User/prompts/ instead of User/agents/.
body: - Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.50.1 - VS Code Version: 1.122.1 - OS Version: Windows 10/11 - Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent discovery - Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): gpt-4o-mini / Claude 3.5 Sonnet - Logs: ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a custom `.agent.md` file in `~/.config/Code/User/agents/test.agent.md` with valid YAML frontmatter 2. Run **Developer: Reload Window** in VS Code 3. Open Copilot Chat and check the agent list 4. **Expected:** Custom agent appears in agent list 5. **Actual:** Agent does not appear 6. Move the same `test.agent.md` file to `~/.config/Code/User/prompts/` instead 7. Reload window again 8. Agent now appears and loads correctly ## Issue: Agent discovery scans `User/prompts/` instead of `User/agents/`. Custom agents must be placed in the `prompts` directory to be discovered, but the documented location is `agents/`. This is a path misconfiguration in the agent discovery logic.

title: Please provide a way to override the system prompt, so we can reduce credit waste
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->

Now that we are paying for tokens, the system prompt is getting in the way. If I run a simple prompt "Respond with a single dot", my billing shows 4.48 credits jump. If I understand the billing correctly, this works out to about 10-15K tokens, which is in this case is almost entirely the system prompt and accompanying data.

What's worse, is that I often need to add more of my tokens to override unacceptable things in the system prompt, like this phrase - "_By default, implement changes rather than only suggesting them._", which I constantly need to override in my prompts or undo the changes, which also wastes tokens. There are other examples of where the system prompt suggests what it shouldn't.

Add a disclaimer that changing the system prompt will "void warranty", and perhaps a simple way to revert to the standard system prompt, which could be required for submitting bug reports, perhaps, but please allow users to edit the system prompt, so we can phrase it more concisely and make it work better for our needs.

title: Tool virtualization should fully support large MCP servers (100s of tools) via semantic lazy-loading
body:
The current tool virtualization system (introduced to fix #290356 / #294055) groups deferred tools behind `activate_*` stubs or `tool_search`, but in practice only surfaces a small subset (~14 out of 154 tools) from a single MCP server. The `tool_search` semantic lookup repeatedly returns the same few tools regardless of query variation.

**What should work**: An MCP server with hundreds of tools should function seamlessly. The model already has `tool_search` ŌĆö it just needs the embeddings index to cover ALL registered tools, not a capped subset. The architecture is correct (lazy discovery ŌåÆ load on demand ŌåÆ call), but the implementation appears to index only ~14 tools from a server that reports 154 via `tools/list`.

**Suggestion**:
1. Index ALL tools from `tools/list` into the virtualization embeddings ŌĆö don't cap pre-indexing
2. Let `tool_search` return more results (currently seems capped at 5)
3. Allow agents to discover tools via skills (`.instructions.md` / `SKILL.md`) ŌĆö if a skill references a tool by name, the agent should be able to load and call it via `tool_search` even if it wasn't surfaced in the initial deferred list. This would make hiding tools from the default tool list much less problematic, since domain-specific skills can guide the agent to the right tools on demand.

**Environment**: VS Code 1.123, Copilot Chat latest, MCP server via Streamable HTTP transport, 154 tools registered

title: BidirectionalMap.set() and delete() cannot handle undefined as a stored value
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: Code - OSS (source repository, all versions affected)
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS (Noble Numbat), x86_64, Linux 5.15.120
- Node.js: v24.15.0

## Problem

In `src/vs/base/common/map.ts`, `BidirectionalMap.set()` and `delete()` use
`get() !== undefined` / `get() === undefined` as a sentinel to detect whether
a key exists in the internal `Map`. Since `Map.get()` returns `undefined` both
for missing keys and for keys whose stored value is `undefined`, this creates
two bugs:

### Bug 1 ŌĆö set() leaks stale reverse mapping

```ts
const map = new BidirectionalMap<string, string | undefined>();
map.set('one', undefined);   // _m2: { undefined ŌåÆ 'one' }
map.set('one', 'new-value'); // _m2: { undefined ŌåÆ 'one', 'new-value' ŌåÆ 'one' }
// BUG: map.getKey(undefined) returns 'one', should be undefined
```

The old `undefined ŌåÆ 'one'` entry in the reverse map is never cleaned up,
causing memory leaks and incorrect `getKey()` results.

### Bug 2 ŌĆö delete() cannot remove entries whose value is undefined

```ts
map.set('one', undefined);
map.delete('one'); // returns false ŌĆö should return true
```

`delete()` compares `value === undefined` to detect key absence, so entries
with `undefined` as a legitimate stored value can never be deleted.

### Bug 3 ŌĆö getOrSet() overwrites undefined values

```ts
const m = new Map<string, string | undefined>();
m.set('a', undefined);
getOrSet(m, 'a', 'default'); // overwrites 'a' to 'default' ŌĆö should keep undefined
```

Same sentinel problem in the helper function at the top of the same file.

## Steps to Reproduce

Run the following in any TypeScript environment with the VS Code source
`src/vs/base/common/map.ts` module loaded:

```ts
import { BidirectionalMap } from './map.js';

const map = new BidirectionalMap<string, string | undefined>();

// Step 1: set a key with undefined value
map.set('one', undefined);
console.assert(map.get('one') === undefined);
console.assert(map.getKey(undefined) === 'one');

// Step 2: overwrite with a different value
map.set('one', 'new-value');
console.assert(map.get('one') === 'new-value');
// FAILS: getKey(undefined) still returns 'one' (stale entry)
console.assert(map.getKey(undefined) === undefined);

// Step 3: try deleting an entry with undefined value
const map2 = new BidirectionalMap<string, string | undefined>();
map2.set('two', undefined);
const deleted = map2.delete('two');
// FAILS: delete returns false, entry remains
console.assert(deleted === true);
```

## Expected Behavior

- `getKey(undefined)` should return `undefined` after overwriting an entry
  whose previous value was `undefined`.
- `delete()` should succeed (return `true`) when the entry's stored value
  happens to be `undefined`.
- `getOrSet()` should not overwrite an existing key whose stored value is
  `undefined`.

## Root Cause

The `undefined` sentinel cannot distinguish "key not found" from "key exists
with value == undefined" in JavaScript `Map`. The fix: use `Map.has()`.

## Fix

Replaced `get() === undefined` / `get() !== undefined` checks with `Map.has()`
calls at three locations:

1. `BidirectionalMap.set()` ŌĆö both oldValue and oldKey checks
2. `BidirectionalMap.delete()` ŌĆö key existence check
3. `getOrSet()` ŌĆö key existence check

Added 5 new test cases for undefined-value edge cases in
`src/vs/base/test/common/map.test.ts`.

No API change. No performance regression (`Map.has()` is O(1)).

title: Support SQL Keyword Formatting Inside C# String Literals
body:
When writing SQL queries inside C# string literals (especially verbatim strings and raw string literals), it would be helpful if Visual Studio Code and C# tooling could recognize SQL content and provide:

* SQL syntax highlighting
* SQL IntelliSense
* Automatic SQL formatting
* SQL keyword capitalization (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, JOIN, etc.)
* Validation of SQL syntax inside strings

Example:

var query = """
select *
from employee
where id = 1
""";

Expected behavior:

The editor recognizes the content as SQL and optionally formats it as:

SELECT *
FROM employee
WHERE id = 1;

This would significantly improve readability and productivity for developers who frequently embed SQL queries in C# applications.

title: Feature Request: Presaved MCP Toolsets / Named Presets for Context-Aware Tool Activation
body:
## Problem

When using multiple MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, developers often need to switch between different subsets of tools depending on the task at hand. Currently, VS Code only supports toggling entire MCP servers on/off via the `disabled` flag or managing them through workspace settings and profiles.

There is no way to define **named, reusable groups of tools** (e.g., `toolset: "analytics"`, `toolset: "frontend-review"`) and quickly activate or deactivate them without:
- Manually editing `settings.json` to toggle `disabled` on multiple servers.
- Switching entirely to a different VS Code profile, which changes more than just MCP tools.
- Creating separate workspace configurations for every tool combination.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a **"MCP Toolsets"** (or "Presets") concept that allows users to define named sets of enabled MCP servers/tools. A toolset could be activated via:
1. Command Palette (e.g., `MCP: Activate Toolset`)
2. A quick-pick dropdown in the MCP panel or status bar.
3. API for extensions to switch toolsets programmatically.

### Example Configuration

```jsonc
// settings.json
{
  "mcp.servers": {
    "github-server": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@github/mcp-server"] },
    "postgres-server": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@postgres/mcp-server"] },
    "playwright-server": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@playwright/mcp-server"] }
  },
  "mcp.toolsets": {
    "backend-development": {
      "description": "Tools for backend API work",
      "servers": ["github-server", "postgres-server"]
    },
    "frontend-testing": {
      "description": "Tools for UI testing and browser automation",
      "servers": ["github-server", "playwright-server"]
    },
    "fullstack": {
      "description": "All available dev tools",
      "servers": ["github-server", "postgres-server", "playwright-server"]
    },
    "minimal": {
      "description": "Only GitHub integration",
      "servers": ["github-server"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Activation Flow

1. User runs `MCP: Activate Toolset` from the Command Palette.
2. VS Code presents the configured toolsets (`backend-development`, `frontend-testing`, etc.).
3. On selection, VS Code updates the active MCP context: servers listed in the toolset are enabled, and those not listed are disabled (or excluded from the current context).
4. Copilot/LM immediately sees only the tools from the active toolset.

## Alternative / Extended Ideas

- **Per-tool granularity**: Beyond server-level grouping, allow toolsets to specify individual tools within a server (e.g., only allow `read_file` from a filesystem server, disable `write_file`).
- **Workspace overrides**: Allow `.vscode/settings.json` to define workspace-specific toolsets while inheriting server definitions from user settings.
- **Dynamic toolsets via extensions**: Let extensions contribute toolsets programmatically (e.g., a project-scoped extension detects it's in a Python repo and auto-suggests the "python-dev" toolset).

## Use Cases

- **Context switching**: A developer working on a backend API in the morning and a frontend bug in the afternoon can switch toolsets instead of manually toggling servers.
- **Reducing token/context bloat**: Limiting active tools to only what's needed keeps the LM context focused and can improve response quality.
- **Team onboarding**: Teams can share `.vscode/settings.json` with pre-made toolsets so new members don't need to know which servers to enable for which tasks.
- **Safe modes**: A "read-only" toolset that only exposes safe tools for review/auditing, versus a "full-access" toolset for active development.

## Current Workarounds and Their Limitations

| Workaround | Limitation |
|---|---|
| Toggle `disabled` per server in `settings.json` | Tedious, error-prone, requires editing JSON manually. |
| VS Code Profiles | Overkill ŌĆö changes extensions, themes, keybindings, etc., not just MCP tools. |
| Workspace-specific `.vscode/settings.json` | Requires duplicating server configs across workspaces; no way to quickly switch within the same workspace. |

## Related

- MCP specification discussions around tool filtering and server discovery.
- Existing need for MCP server management UI improvements.

---

I'd be happy to help refine this proposal or provide more specific UX mockups if helpful. Thank you for considering this!

title: Allow extensions to contribute and read resolvable variables from the workbench, Take #42
body: This feature has been requested by the community over and over again for the past at least 7+ years. We are going to keep asking until we get somewhere, because it seems to keep getting ignored by the team. Allow a mechanism for extensions to contribute and read resolvable variables to the workbench. This would imply extension X can contribute variables to the workbench that can be used anywhere normal variables can be used such as within tasks.json, settings.json, etc.. Additionally, the mechanism would also allow extensions to resolve variables into values. It goes both ways. I would be willing to contribute the solution to this issue myself as I have downloaded the vscode repository and have been building a proof of concept to know if this is feasible. However, I am a C, C# developer by trade and have next to no experience in the vscode architecture nor typescript. Therefore, it would be requested that someone in the know actually implement the proper interfaces. Here are screenshots from my proof of concept I don't know what will be accepted for the 'mechanism' by which to do this. However this is one such concrete proposal. 1. Add a new variable form to the existing forms such as command, input etc. The form would be ${var:extension.variable}. 2. Extensions can contribute / register a single callback to the workbench that gets executed when the variable form is encountered. The resolver holds a map of extension ids to callbacks 3. The resolver logic traps on the 'var' in the switch statement above and parses the extension id. The resolver looks in the extension map. If the extension exists within the map, it executes the callback 4. Within the extension callback, the extension uses its own logic for tracking contributed variables and returns a string back to the resolver of the variable value. The variable value itself can contain embedded resolvable variables. 5. Extensions can also call a now publicized method on the resolver to resolve a variable and get the value of the variable The other option would be for the resolver to hold a map that extensions can contribute to directly. However, I think this may cause conflict between extensions if multiple extensions try to contribute or use the same name of a variable. In the above example, I have a variable in a task called 'thebaz'. To use it within tasks.json the form is ${var:myextension.thebaz}. The variable contains an embedded variable called thefoob. The resolver resolves this to ${var:myextension.thefoob}. The resolver then resolves ${var:myextension.thefoob} to 'FOOBED!' The proof of concept proves this is very doable as the plumbing for the recursive resolution is already in place. Current trigger for this request is : #46471

title: [BYOK] VS Code Copilot Chat: "Sorry, your request failed" ŌĆō Server error, Stream terminated ŌĆō Client Request Id 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633
body:
## Summary

When using GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code with **Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)**, I consistently get a server error and the request fails, even after retries.

**Error message:**
```text
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633

Reason: Server error. Stream terminated: Error: Server error. Stream terminated
at dG._provideLanguageModelResponse
  (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:16552)
at process.processTicksAndRejections
  (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)
at async dG.provideLanguageModelResponse
  (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:17555)
```

This happens repeatedly and prevents me from using Copilot Chat with my BYOK model.

---

## Environment

- **OS:** macOS (version: [e.g. macOS 15.5])
- **VS Code version:** [e.g. 1.106.0] (check via `Code > About Visual Studio Code`)
- **GitHub Copilot extension version:** [e.g. 1.260.0] (check in Extensions panel)
- **Copilot Chat extension version:** [if separate, e.g. 0.26.0]
- **Copilot mode:** BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
- **Model / provider:** [e.g. GPT-4o via OpenAI, or your exact model name and provider]
- **VS Code installation:** [e.g. official .pkg from code.visualstudio.com, or from Homebrew]

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code on macOS.
2. Ensure IŌĆÖm signed in with my GitHub account that has Copilot access.
3. Configure Copilot Chat to use **BYOK** with my model: [briefly describe, e.g. ŌĆ£OpenAI API key in settingsŌĆØ or ŌĆ£provider URL and keyŌĆØ].
4. Open the **Copilot Chat** panel.
5. Type a simple prompt, e.g. ŌĆ£Explain this codeŌĆØ or ŌĆ£Generate a function to ŌĆ”ŌĆØ.
6. Send the request.

**Expected behavior:**  
Copilot returns a response.

**Actual behavior:**  
Copilot returns the error above and the stream terminates.

---

## Frequency

- **How often:** [e.g. 100% of the time / every request / intermittently]
- **Since when:** [e.g. started on 2026-05-28, or ŌĆ£since I started using BYOKŌĆØ]

---

## What IŌĆÖve Tried

- Retrying the request multiple times in Copilot Chat [­¤öä retry button].
- Starting a **new chat** in Copilot Chat.
- Switching to a different model in the Copilot Chat model selector (if available).
- Reloading VS Code: `Developer: Reload Window`.
- Checking network connection (stable, no proxy / or describe if you use a proxy).
- Running: **GitHub Copilot: Collect Diagnostics** and saving the file.
- Setting log level to **Trace** for **GitHub Copilot** and **GitHub Copilot Chat**, then checking the Output panel.

Nothing has resolved the issue.

---

## Diagnostics & Logs

I have collected:

- **Diagnostics file:** `github-copilot-diagnostics-*.json` (attached)
- **Copilot Chat logs** from the Output panel (`GitHub Copilot Chat` channel), including the full stack trace with the Client Request Id.
- **Client Request Id:** `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633`

I can paste redacted logs here or attach them if GitHub allows file uploads.

---

## Additional Context

- I am using **BYOK** to connect to my own model provider.
- The same API key/model works fine when tested directly (e.g., via curl / Postscript / other tool): [yes/no ŌĆō if yes, briefly describe].
- This appears to be a **server-side stream termination** error in the Copilot extension, not a local code issue.

---

## Request

Could you please:

1. Investigate whether this is a known issue with **Copilot Chat + BYOK** on macOS.
2. Check logs for Client Request Id `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633` on the server side.
3. Provide guidance on:
   - Any required configuration for BYOK in VS Code.
   - Whether this is due to a service outage or a bug in the Copilot extension.

The tunnel starts successfully, but the status remains stuck on "Connecting" forever.

title: terminal.integrated.tabs.title template ignored when process sends OSC title sequences
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: latest (reproduced in Cursor, which is VS Code-based)
- OS Version: macOS 24.6.0

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set `"terminal.integrated.tabs.title": "${cwdFolder}"` in settings.json
2. Open the integrated terminal
3. Run a process that sends OSC escape sequences to set the terminal title (e.g. Claude Code CLI)
4. Observe the tab title

## Expected behavior

The tab should show the folder name (`${cwdFolder}`), as configured. Non-`${sequence}` templates should take precedence over OSC sequences sent by processes.

## Actual behavior

The tab title is overwritten by the process's OSC sequence and stays that way for the entire session. When multiple sessions are open across different projects, all tabs are indistinguishable.

## Notes

- Using `${sequence}` explicitly works as expected (shows the process-set title)
- The bug only affects cases where the user has chosen a template other than `${sequence}` with the expectation that it overrides process titles

Related: anthropics/claude-code#64448

title: Chat: Custom session titles are lost after extension update / index rebuild
body:
## Bug Description

When you rename a chat session (right-click ŌåÆ Rename), the custom title is saved to the SQLite index (`chat.ChatSessionStore.index` in `state.vscdb`) but is **not written back to the corresponding `.jsonl` session file** (`customTitle` field in the first line).

When the Copilot Chat extension updates or rebuilds its index, it reads session metadata from the `.jsonl` files. Since `customTitle` is missing there, all renamed sessions revert to the default title ("New Chat" / "µ¢░Õ╗║ĶüŖÕż®").

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the Chat panel and start a session.
2. Right-click the session ŌåÆ Rename it to something custom (e.g. "My Feature Work").
3. Update the Copilot Chat extension or reload VS Code enough times to trigger an index rebuild.
4. Observe that the session title reverts to "New Chat".

## Expected Behavior

The `customTitle` field should be written to both the SQLite index **and** the `.jsonl` session file atomically when a rename occurs. This ensures the title survives any index rebuild.

## Actual Behavior

Only the SQLite index is updated. The `.jsonl` file's `customTitle` field remains empty/absent. After an index rebuild the title is lost.

## Root Cause (investigated locally)

- Session files: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage/<id>/chatSessions/<session-id>.jsonl`
- Index: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage/<id>/state.vscdb` ŌåÆ `ItemTable` key `chat.ChatSessionStore.index`
- After renaming, the `.jsonl` first-line JSON has no `customTitle` while the database entry does. Verified across 8 sessions.

## Workaround

Manually patch the `.jsonl` files to add the missing `customTitle` using a script that reads the database and writes back to the files.

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- VS Code version: latest
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat

title: BYOK Azure provider: Support Anthropic-format deployments on Azure AI Services
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 11 Business
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): any
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): BYOK / Azure
- Logs: n/a
## Problem

Claude models deployed via Azure AI Foundry (GlobalStandard) only expose the Anthropic Messages API (`/anthropic/v1/messages`). The BYOK Azure provider cannot use them because:

1. `resolveAzureUrl`  in [azureProvider.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/azureProvider.ts) doesn't recognize `services.ai.azure.com` ŌĆö throws "Unrecognized Azure deployment URL". This one is easily fixed, but...
2. Even with an explicit URL, the Azure provider only speaks OpenAI chat completions format ŌĆö there's no translation to Anthropic Messages API. This requires a message adapter or use of the existing Anthropic Provider (adapted to Azure).

## Expected Behavior

`vendor: "azure"` should support Anthropic-format deployments. When the configured URL contains `/anthropic/`, the Azure provider should use the Anthropic Messages protocol instead of OpenAI chat completions.

## Steps to Reproduce:

1. Deploy a claude model, e.g. Opus 4.6, via Azure AI Foundry ŌåÆ GlobalStandard deployment
2. Portal shows Target URI: `https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic/v1/messages`
3. Add model in `chatLanguageModels.json` with `vendor: "azure"`
4. Any URL attempted results in either "Unrecognized Azure deployment URL" or 404

## Possible Solution
A bit of AI assisted speculation in a possible solution (untested). The existing `AnthropicLMProvider` ([anthropicProvider.ts](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/anthropicProvider.ts)) already implements the full Anthropic streaming protocol, tool calls, thinking, etc. The Azure provider could reuse that code path:

1. In `resolveAzureUrl`, recognize `services.ai.azure.com` and pass through URLs containing `/anthropic/`
2. In `AzureBYOKModelProvider.provideLanguageModelChatResponse`, detect when the resolved URL is an Anthropic endpoint
3. Instantiate the Anthropic SDK client with `baseURL` set to the Azure URL (e.g. `https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com/anthropic`) and `apiKey` from the Azure config
4. Delegate to the same streaming/tool-call logic already in `AnthropicLMProvider._makeRequest`

title: An option to not highlight newline on triple-click
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
I don't see the logic of the current triple-click behavior. When I triple-click on a line, I usually want to copy the text and paste it elsewhere, sometimes outside VS Code. If I also wanted the newline, I'd either right-click and copy the line (without highlighting) or place the cursor on it and hit Ctrl+C.
<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
With the option enabled, triple-clicking on a line should highlight that line and that line only. The highlight shouldn't continue to the beginning of the next line.

title: BYOK requests consume Copilot quota on OpenAI-compatible endpoints
body:
## Problem

BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) requests routed through OpenAI-compatible endpoints (Azure, OpenAI, OpenRouter, custom endpoints) still deduct from the user's Copilot monthly quota.

## Root cause

The request flow goes through CAPI (Copilot proxy):

`
CopilotLanguageModelWrapper ŌåÆ OpenAIEndpoint (extends ChatEndpoint) ŌåÆ chatMLFetcher ŌåÆ CAPI
`

Although OpenAIEndpoint sets ownsAuthorization = true (skipping the CAPI Copilot bearer token), the request still goes through the CAPI proxy pipeline. CAPI returns copilot_usage.total_nano_aiu in the response, which chatMLFetcher records via IChatQuotaService.setLastCopilotUsage(), reducing the user's available quota.

## Key code paths

- \extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts\ ŌĆö BYOK endpoint with \ownsAuthorization = true\
- \extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts\ lines ~387-432 ŌĆö CAPI response processing consumes \copilot_usage.total_nano_aiu\
- \extensions/copilot/src/platform/chat/common/chatQuotaService.ts\ ŌĆö quota tracking service

## Notable exception

Anthropic BYOK does **not** consume quota because \AnthropicLMProvider\ calls the Anthropic SDK directly (SDK ŌåÆ Anthropic API), completely bypassing CAPI.

## Expected behavior

When a user provides their own API key, the request should not consume Copilot quota since no Copilot infrastructure is being billed.

```

ChatGPT (sorry for AI, I just need to fix it ASAP) says that:
Details

Root cause is clear: **VSCode terminal process PATH does not include your User PATH at all**. In VSCode: ```pwsh Process PATH = Machine PATH + VSCode/Copilot entries ``` In Windows Terminal: ```pwsh Process PATH = Machine PATH + User PATH ``` That is why these are missing in VSCode: ```text C:\Users\Kenya-West\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WinGet\Links C:\Users\Kenya-West\AppData\Local\Programs\oh-my-posh\bin ``` ## Immediate test fix inside VSCode terminal Run: ```pwsh $env:Path = @( [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'Machine') [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'User') ) -join ';' Get-Command fzf, oh-my-posh ``` If that works, your profile will work after reloading it: ```pwsh . $PROFILE ``` ## Permanent defensive fix in your PowerShell profile Put this **at the very top** of: ```pwsh C:\Users\Kenya-West\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 ``` ```pwsh # Repair PATH when host process, e.g. VSCode, starts without User PATH $machinePath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'Machine') $userPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path', 'User') $env:Path = @( $machinePath $userPath $env:Path ) -join ';' ``` Then make module loading safe: ```pwsh if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { Import-Module PSFzf Set-PsFzfOption ` -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' ` -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r' } if (Get-Command oh-my-posh -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { oh-my-posh init pwsh | Invoke-Expression } ``` ## Also check this VSCode setting In `settings.json`: ```json "terminal.integrated.inheritEnv": true ``` Your current evidence points to VSCode launching `pwsh` with a broken inherited environment, not to PowerShell or PSFzf itself.

But: ```json "terminal.integrated.inheritEnv": true ``` \- is already set in VS Code, yet it does not pickup User profile somehow. --- This only happened in VS Code version `1.122.1` and only after PC reboot.

title: Copliot Chat, path rendering configuration options
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Currently, when my Copliot Chat completes an analysis over a monorepo, it produces detailed report tables that often contain relative paths to the locations where certain patterns I have asked about occur.

The Chat interface helpfully renders these with a folder icon and makes them clickable to jump straight to the file, which is handy when looking at a specific example from a table.

Less helpfully, the hardcoded behaviour is to only retain the very last directory name for the target path, and the agent I am talking to has no way to control or override this behaviour other than to intentionally do a raw ASCII render of the table.

**Why is this a problem?**
My mono-repo has some regular recurring structures in it that often end in identical directory names as the last step of a path. When the AI renders a table and the VSCode Chat interface only shows the last directory, I end up with tables that only say "`api`", which is completely useless to read/scan for information as all the useful context has been stripped to tell me which part of the system the API is part of.

**What I'd like**
Some kind of configuration setting in VSCode to control this stripping behaviour, either a way to tell it to keep N directories above the last, or a way to suppress this behaviour completely.

Note, I like the fact these are links to directories/files that I can click to jump around in the codebase, and I'd like to retain that behaviour, but the stripping down to only the last directory/file name is what is making this feature result in useless output.

title: Test: Copilot Chat offers Ubuntu 24.04 bubblewrap sandbox repair
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#319061

- [x] Linux @aeschli

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319256%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat detects when `bubblewrap` is installed but unusable on Ubuntu 24.04 because of AppArmor / user namespace restrictions, offers repair actions before running a sandboxed terminal command, re-checks sandbox prerequisites after repair, and does not silently fail or run the command before the sandbox is usable.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders on Ubuntu 24.04
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Use a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use a disposable VM or test machine because this test may install AppArmor packages and modify system sandbox policy
- Ensure Linux sandboxing is enabled:
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
- Ensure `bubblewrap` and `socat` are installed, but `bubblewrap` cannot create the sandbox namespace. For example, the following command should fail with a namespace / permission error such as `bwrap: setting up uid map: Permission denied`:
  ```sh
  bwrap --unshare-net --dev-bind / / echo ok
  ```

### Scenario 1: unusable bubblewrap offers repair and does not run the command first

1. Open a Copilot Chat agent mode session in the workspace.
2. Prompt:
   > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
3. When prompted, first choose **Cancel**.

**Expected:**
- A confirmation titled similar to **Repair Bubblewrap Sandbox** appears before the command is executed.
- The confirmation explains that bubblewrap is installed but cannot create the required sandbox namespace.
- After choosing **Cancel**, `echo sandbox-ok` is not run.
- Copilot Chat reports that the bubblewrap sandbox repair was cancelled, rather than showing a silent terminal failure.

### Scenario 2: AppArmor repair re-checks prerequisites and then runs sandboxed

1. Re-issue the same prompt:
   > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
2. Choose **Apply AppArmor Fix**.
3. If prompted for a sudo password, use the focused/revealed terminal to enter it.

**Expected:**
- VS Code runs the AppArmor remediation command in a terminal and waits for it to complete.
- After the remediation succeeds, VS Code re-checks sandbox prerequisites before running the original command.
- The original `echo sandbox-ok` command then runs and completes successfully inside the sandbox.
- Copilot Chat shows `sandbox-ok` in the result.

### Scenario 3: repaired sandbox works on the next command

1. In the same workspace, prompt Copilot Chat agent mode again:
   > Run `echo sandbox-still-ok` in the terminal.

**Expected:**
- No bubblewrap repair prompt is shown.
- The command runs in the sandbox and returns `sandbox-still-ok`.

title: Custom Endpoint vision attachments stop working when using BYOK without GitHub sign-in
body:
Version: 1.122.1

Summary

Image attachments are not passed to a custom endpoint model when using the new BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) functionality without a GitHub sign-in.

The exact same model and endpoint work correctly when signed into GitHub.

Configuration

chatLanguageModels.json

[
  {
    "name": "Custom Endpoint",
    "vendor": "customendpoint",
    "apiType": "chat-completions",
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "miia",
        "name": "miia2",
        "url": "https://<redacted>",
        "toolCalling": true,
        "vision": true,
        "maxInputTokens": 180000,
        "maxOutputTokens": 80000
      }
    ]
  }
]

Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a custom endpoint model with vision=true.
2. Sign into GitHub.
3. Paste an image into Copilot Chat.
4. Ask the model to describe the image.
5. Observe that vision works correctly.
6. Sign out of GitHub.
7. Paste the same image into Copilot Chat.
8. Ask the same question.

Expected Result

The image should be sent to the custom endpoint regardless of GitHub authentication state.

Actual Result

The image attachment appears in the chat UI ("Pasted Image"), but the model behaves as if no image was provided.

Additional Notes

- Text chat works correctly.
- Tool calling works correctly.
- The same endpoint and model work correctly when signed into GitHub.
- The issue only occurs when using BYOK without GitHub sign-in.

title: Test: Copilot Chat retries network-blocked commands inside the sandbox
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#319181

- [x] macOS @jruales
- [x] Linux @rzhao271 

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319254%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat terminal commands that need network access first retry inside the sandbox with unrestricted network access after user confirmation, instead of immediately falling back to unsandboxed execution.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Open a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use macOS or Linux (Windows sandboxing currently enables sandboxing with network access, so this network-blocking scenario is not applicable there)
- Ensure the sandbox is enabled but network is **not** globally allowed:
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `true` (default)
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands` = `true` (default)
  - Do **not** add the test domain below to the sandbox network allowlist
- Open a **Copilot Chat** agent mode session in the workspace and keep it open for the whole test

### Scenario 1: blocked network command requests allow-network sandbox confirmation

1. In the Copilot Chat agent mode session, prompt:
   > Run `git fetch origin`.

**Expected:**
- VS Code either detects the blocked domain before running, or the first sandboxed attempt is blocked by network restrictions.
- Copilot Chat retries by requesting to run the command sandboxed with unrestricted network access (`requestAllowNetwork`) and provides a reason.
- The confirmation/details mention unrestricted network access in the sandbox.
- The command remains sandboxed; the confirmation is **not** for unsandboxed execution.
- After approving, the command succeeds and shows an HTTP status line (for example `HTTP/2 200` or `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`).

### Scenario 2: disabling allow-network retry uses the old unsandboxed fallback

1. Set `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `false`.
2. Start a new Copilot Chat agent mode session in the same workspace.
3. Re-issue the same prompt as in Scenario 1.

**Expected:**
- Copilot Chat does **not** request `requestAllowNetwork` / unrestricted network access inside the sandbox.
- If the command cannot run because of sandbox network restrictions, the fallback confirmation is for unsandboxed execution.
- The command does **not** silently run outside the sandbox without confirmation.

title: Mermaid diagram re-renders on every token during Copilot Chat streaming response
body:
<!-- Type: Bug -->

### Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No (requires GitHub Copilot Chat)

### VS Code Version

1.122.1 (Universal, arm64) ŌĆö commit `8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e`

### OS Version

macOS 26.5 (build 25F71), Apple Silicon

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Copilot Chat (Ask or Agent mode).
2. Send a prompt that asks the model to produce a response containing a mermaid diagram followed by additional text, e.g.:
   > Explain MVC and include a mermaid diagram of the request flow, then describe each component in detail.
3. Observe the chat panel while the response is streaming.

### Expected Behavior

Once a ```` ```mermaid ```` code block is **closed** in the streamed output, the rendered SVG should remain stable. Subsequent tokens (text appearing after the closed block) should not cause the diagram to re-render.

### Actual Behavior

The mermaid diagram visibly flickers / re-draws on virtually every incoming token until the entire streamed message finishes. Inspecting the chat webview with Developer Tools shows the mermaid `<svg>` node being repeatedly removed and re-inserted, even though the diagram's source has not changed since the closing ```` ``` ```` was received.

The flicker stops only after the stream ends. Collapsing and re-expanding the message afterwards produces a single clean render that is stable.

### Suspected Root Cause

The chat markdown renderer appears to re-parse and re-render the **entire** assistant message on each streaming chunk, instead of diffing closed code-block nodes. Because mermaid has no internal content-hash cache, every render call produces a new SVG with a new id and replaces the DOM node, leading to the perceived flicker.

Possible fixes:

- Skip re-rendering a fenced code block whose source has not changed since the last render (content-hash cache at the markdown renderer / chat view level), **or**
- Add a short-circuit inside the mermaid renderer wrapper: `if (lastSource === currentSource) return cachedSvg;`

### Additional Context / Related Issues

This is distinct from the existing mermaid flicker reports, which describe a one-time flash at load time:

- #316964 ŌĆö Spastic flickering of mermaid diagram
- #316977 ŌĆö Mermaid block flickers when opening markdown file
- #317083 ŌĆö Mermaid Preview: graph flashed and disappeared when a fresh preview window opens

The scenario reported here is **specific to Copilot Chat streaming responses** and persists continuously throughout the stream, not just at first render.

cc @mjbvz

title: Agents window: corrupted session file silently loads as empty instead of surfacing error
body:
## Bug Description

When opening an old session in the Agents window, if the Copilot SDK rejects `session.resume` due to a validation error in the session file, the agent host silently falls back to `createSession` with the same ID. This results in the session appearing to load successfully but showing **0 turns** ŌĆö all conversation history is lost without any user-facing error.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Have an existing agent session with conversation history
2. The session file becomes corrupted (in this case, a compaction metadata field fails validation)
3. Open the session in the Agents window
4. Session appears empty ŌĆö no error shown to user

## Expected Behavior

The user should be informed that the session could not be restored due to corruption, rather than silently presenting an empty session.

## Actual Behavior

The session loads with 0 turns. The user sees a blank conversation with no indication that data was lost.

## Relevant Logs

```
[Copilot:df7c418f-...] _resumeSession called ŌĆö session not in memory, resuming...
[Copilot:df7c418f-...] Calling SDK resumeSession...
[Copilot:df7c418f-...] SDK resumeSession failed: code=-32603, message=Request session.resume failed with message: Session file is corrupted (line 19567: data.compactionTokensUsed.copilotUsage.tokenDetails.0.batchSize: Number must be greater than 0)
[Copilot:df7c418f-...] Resume failed (code=-32603), falling back to createSession with same ID
[Copilot:df7c418f-...] Fallback createSession succeeded
[AgentHostStateManager] Restored session: copilotcli:/df7c418f-... (0 turns)
[AgentService] Restored session copilotcli:/df7c418f-... with 0 turns
```

## Analysis

The fallback logic is in `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgent.ts` around line 1626:

```typescript
if (!err || errCode !== -32603) {
    throw err;
}
// Falls back to createSession with same ID
```

The comment says _"The SDK fails to resume sessions that have no messages"_ ŌĆö but error code `-32603` is also returned for **data corruption**, not just empty sessions. The fallback does not distinguish between these cases.

## Suggested Fix

Two potential approaches:
1. **Agent Host**: Parse the error message to distinguish "no messages" from "corrupted" ŌĆö surface a notification to the user when data is actually corrupted rather than silently creating an empty session
2. **SDK**: The validation on `batchSize > 0` in compaction metadata should not prevent loading the actual conversation turns ŌĆö this is metadata, not content

title: Custom models disappear when opening a Dev Container
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.50.1
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: both Windows 11 / macOS
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): add models

When using VS Code with GitHub Copilot, custom models configured through OpenRouter are not preserved when switching from a local workspace to a Dev Container.
After entering a Dev Container, all previously configured OpenRouter models disappear from the Copilot model list and must be added again manually.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open VS Code in a local workspace.
2. Sign in to GitHub Copilot.
3. Add and configure OpenRouter models in Copilot.
4. Verify that the OpenRouter models appear and work correctly in the Copilot model selector.
5. Reopen the project using a Dev Container (Dev Containers: Reopen in Container).
6. Open the Copilot model selector.

Expected Behavior
Custom OpenRouter models configured in Copilot should remain available after opening the project in a Dev Container, or at least be synchronized between the local environment and the Dev Container environment.

title: Public API to resolve the source resource URI behind a built-in webview preview tab
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Provide a public, supported way for an extension to obtain the source resource URI backing a built-in webview-preview tab ŌĆö primarily the classic Markdown preview.

### Current behavior (verified on VS Code 1.122)

- The Markdown preview **editor** (`vscode.openWith` with viewType `vscode.markdown.preview.editor`; editor-title toggle "Reopen as source file") surfaces as a `TabInputCustom`, and `TabInputCustom.uri` exposes the source document URI. This case works today. Ō£ģ
- The **classic** Markdown preview (`markdown.showPreview` / `markdown.showPreviewToSide`, Ctrl+Shift+V / Ctrl+K V) surfaces as a `TabInputWebview` with `viewType` = `"mainThreadWebview-markdown.preview"`. `TabInputWebview` exposes only `viewType` (a string) and **no `uri`**. There is no public API to map this webview tab back to the `.md` source it renders. ŌØī
- For a full-tab `markdown.showPreview`, the source document is often **not** in `window.visibleTextEditors` (it can be `[]`), so the "use the single visible editor" heuristic fails. `showPreviewToSide` keeps the source visible, but that is only one sub-case.

`vscode.window.activeTextEditor` is `undefined` for webview-backed tabs, so the net effect is that there is no supported path from the active classic-preview tab to its source resource URI.

### Use case

Extensions that follow the active document ŌĆö custom outline trees, structural navigators, table-of-contents / breadcrumb panels ŌĆö go blank when the user switches to a Markdown preview, even though the preview clearly knows which file it is rendering and that file is open in the workspace.

### Proposed API (any one would resolve this)

1. Add a `uri` / `resource` (or `sourceUri`) field to `TabInputWebview` for built-in previews, mirroring `TabInputCustom.uri`.
2. A general `window.tabGroups` accessor exposing the resource a webview tab is associated with.
3. A Markdown-specific active-resource API (e.g. `onDidChangeActiveMarkdownPreview` + `activeMarkdownPreviewResource`).

### Related issue

#201884 (OPEN) ŌĆö `TabInputWebview` viewType identification for the Markdown preview; adjacent, but about the `viewType` string, not the source URI.

Suggested labels: `api`, `webview`, `markdown`.

title: Recently Opened: deleted remote (WSL/SSH) folders are never removed from the list
body:
<!-- Type: Bug / Feature request -->

## Summary

Deleted **remote** folders (e.g. opened via Remote - WSL) are never removed from **File ŌåÆ Open Recent**, even after you attempt to open them and the open fails because the path no longer exists. They stay in the top "workspaces/folders" section indefinitely.

This is distinct from #74465 (closed by-design in 2019). There, the resolution was:

> "You can have many folders in your recents and we shouldn't hit the disk many times for each one when you open a vscode window. So instead if you try to open it and we find that it doesn't exist anymore, then we remove it from the list."

That remove-on-open cleanup works for local folders, but it does **not** happen for remote (`vscode-remote://wsl+...`) folders - so a deleted WSL workspace lingers forever. I am **not** asking for a disk scan of every recent entry on startup (that's the perf concern from #74465); I'm reporting that the existing "remove it when we find it doesn't exist on open" behavior is missing for the remote case.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. In WSL, create a folder: `mkdir ~/ghost-test`
2. Open it in VS Code via Remote - WSL (`code ~/ghost-test` from the WSL shell).
3. Close the window.
4. Delete the folder in WSL: `rm -rf ~/ghost-test`
5. Open VS Code and check **File ŌåÆ Open Recent**.

## Expected

After attempting to open the now-missing remote folder (and VS Code finding it doesn't exist), the entry is removed from Open Recent ŌĆö the same behavior #74465 says already happens for local folders.

## Actual

The entry `vscode-remote://wsl+<distro>/home/<user>/ghost-test` remains in Open Recent (top section) permanently. Attempting to open it surfaces an error, but the stale entry is **not** removed, so it keeps polluting the recent list and the `> Open Recent` quick-pick autocomplete.

## Notes

- The entry persists across restarts; the only way to remove it via "Open Recent > More ..." and then clicking "X".
- Same problem is likely to affect other remote authorities (Remote - SSH, Dev Containers, Tunnels), since they share the remove-on-open path.

## VS Code version

- Version: 1.121.0 (stable), commit f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282
- Remote: Remote - WSL (server build same version)
- OS: Windows host + WSL2 (Ubuntu)

Related: #74465, #55367

title: Test: Copilot CLI sandbox blocks reads outside the workspace
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#317981

- [x] Windows @kycutler
- [ ] macOS @alexdima
- [x] Linux @rzhao271

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319218%0A%0A&assignees=chrmarti)

---

Verify that the Copilot CLI session's sandbox prevents the agent from reading files outside the workspace on all three platforms, and that toggling the sandbox setting takes effect within the same chat session.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- A workspace folder that does **not** contain `~/something.txt` (i.e. your home directory is outside the open folder)
- Create a small file with recognizable content at `~/something.txt` (use `%USERPROFILE%\something.txt` on Windows), e.g.:
  - macOS / Linux: `echo "SECRET-CONTENT-12345" > ~/something.txt`
  - Windows (PowerShell): `Set-Content -Path $HOME\something.txt -Value "SECRET-CONTENT-12345"`
- Start with the sandbox **off**:
  - macOS / Linux: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `off`
  - Windows: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabledWindows` = `off`
- Leave `chat.agent.sandbox.fileSystem` at defaults (do not allow-list the home directory)
- Open a **Copilot CLI** chat session in the workspace and keep it open for the whole test

### Scenario 1: sandbox off ŌĆö read succeeds

1. In the Copilot CLI chat, prompt:
   > Run `cat ~/something.txt` in the shell and show me its output. On Windows use `Get-Content $HOME\something.txt`.

**Expected:** the command succeeds and `SECRET-CONTENT-12345` appears in the response.

### Scenario 2: turn sandbox on in the same session ŌĆö read is blocked

1. Without closing the chat, switch the sandbox setting on for the current platform:
   - macOS / Linux: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
   - Windows: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabledWindows` = `allowNetwork` (sandbox on Windows currently requires `allowNetwork`)
2. In the same chat session, re-issue the same prompt as in Scenario 1.

**Expected:**
- The command runs inside the sandbox.
- The read fails with a sandbox / permission-denied style error (wording varies by backend: macOS Seatbelt `Operation not permitted`, Linux LXC `Permission denied`, Windows MXC sandbox `Access is denied`).
- `SECRET-CONTENT-12345` does **not** appear in the response.

title: Test: Extensions delayed auto-update
body:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/7834, https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/7769

- [ ] anyOS @isidorn
- [ ] anyOS

Complexity: 4

Author: @sandy081

---

## Context

This TPI covers the new `delayed` value for the `extensions.autoUpdate` setting. The setting is now a string enum (`on` | `delayed` | `off`).

When set to `delayed`, an extension whose newer version was published less than 6 hours ago is treated as outdated-but-delayed: it is excluded from auto-update and the activity badge, and surfaces an info status explaining when it will be auto updated. Manual updates still work.

## Setup

1. Latest VS Code Insiders.
2. A few installed extensions, some with recent updates available.

> **Tip ŌĆö testing the 6-hour delay:** The easiest way is to **change the system clock forward by 6+ hours and reload the window** ŌĆö the next auto-update check will then see the extension as past its delay. Alternatively, find two real extensions in the Marketplace: one with a version published in the last few hours (delayed path), and one with an update older than 6 hours (normal path).

## What to test

### 1. Migration of legacy values

`extensions.autoUpdate` is an **application-scope** setting, so migration runs once per installation regardless of profile/workspace. Start with any of the **old** values set, then launch the new Insiders and verify the value is migrated/normalized on startup.

| Old value in `settings.json` | Expected migrated value |
| --- | --- |
| `true` | `"on"` |
| `"all"` | `"on"` |
| `"onlyEnabledExtensions"` | `"on"` |
| `false` | `"off"` |
| `"none"` | `"off"` |
| `"onlySelectedExtensions"` | `"off"` |

For each:
1. Set the old value in `settings.json` and reload.
2. Verify the value is rewritten to the new enum.
3. Verify the auto-update behavior matches the new value (on / off).

### 2. Setting UI

1. Open Settings UI and search for `extensions.autoUpdate`.
2. Verify it is rendered as a dropdown with three options: **On**, **Delayed**, **Off**. Read the description of each option and validate that it clearly and accurately explains the behavior.

### 3. `on` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `on`.
2. Verify outdated extensions are auto-updated.
3. Disable an outdated extension and verify it is **not** auto-updated ŌĆö only enabled extensions should be auto-updated.

### 4. `delayed` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `delayed`.
2. For an extension whose newer version was published **< 6 hours ago**:
   - It is **not** auto-updated.
   - It is **not** counted in the activity bar badge.
   - It shows an info status in the **Extensions view** explaining **when** it will be auto updated.
   - Open the extension in the **extension editor** and verify the same info status is shown.
   - Hover the extension and verify the same info is rendered correctly in the hover (no stale info, no flicker).
   - Clicking the "Update" button still updates it manually.
3. For an extension whose newer version was published **> 6 hours ago**:
   - It is auto-updated normally and counted in the badge.
4. Disable an outdated extension and verify it is **not** auto-updated ŌĆö only enabled extensions should be auto-updated.

### 5. `off` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `off`.
2. Verify extensions are not auto-updated.
3. Manual update still works.

When additional budget is enabled at the enterprise level and there is still available capacity within that budget, the system should not indicate that usage is blocked. Instead, it should clearly reflect that requests can continue to be served using the remaining enterprise budget.

title: [Feature Request] Distinct syntax highlighting for the 'step' argument in python slicing
body:
When reading Python code, it can be mentally taxing to parse three consecutive integers in a slicing expression (e.g., text[0:10:2]). Currently, most color themes apply the exact same color to all numbers inside the brackets, making it hard to instantly distinguish the "range" from the "step".

It would be incredibly helpful for readability if the range (start:stop) and the step could have distinct syntax highlighting colors.

Instead of coloring all numbers equally, the theme should group them logically:

Range group (start:stop): Maintained in the standard number color.

Step group (step): Highlighted in a different color to indicate it's a modifier/option.

Example concept:
text[ 0:10: 2 ]  (Where '2' has a distinct color from '0' and '10') 

Currently, the only workaround is adding manual spaces like text[0:10 : 2], but a native syntax highlighting distinction would be a much cleaner and more intuitive user experience (UX) for Python developers.

title: [Feature Request] `LanguageModelChatProvider`: allow providers to grey out excluded message turns in the chat UI
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

Currently, `LanguageModelChatProvider` implementors can silently filter messages before forwarding to the backend, but there is no way to surface this to the user. The chat UI history is fully owned by VS Code and providers have no write-back mechanism.

**The core idea**

Introduce a concept of **greyed-out turns** in the chat UI ŌĆö turns that are still visible in the conversation history but marked as excluded from the context sent to the model. The provider signals which turns it excluded, and VS Code renders them accordingly.

**Motivation**

The most valuable use case is surgical exclusion of noisy tool call pairs ŌĆö a failed `npm install`, a wrong file read, a typo-fix edit cycle. These turns contribute tokens but zero signal going forward. A provider implementing smart context management (including AI-assisted compaction, where the model itself emits a `compact_context` tool call) currently has no way to make this visible to the user. The only workaround is prepending a notice to the next assistant response, which pollutes the conversation.

**Proposed API**

A new `LanguageModelCompactionPart` response part, emitted via the existing `progress` callback in `provideLanguageModelChatResponse`:

```ts
progress.report(new LanguageModelCompactionPart({
  excludedRanges: [{ start: 3, end: 6 }],
  reason: "Excluded failed npm install attempt"
}));
```

VS Code renders the affected turns greyed out in the chat thread, with a small indicator showing the reason.

**Why this matters**

- The `LanguageModelChatProvider` API is explicitly designed as an extensible ecosystem. Custom context management is one of the most natural things a provider would implement.
- VS Code already renders its own `/compact` events in the UI. Exposing a typed signal for the same concept is consistent with the existing design.
- Without this, there is an asymmetry: built-in compaction is visible; provider compaction is a black box. Users can't tell why context changed.

**Related**

- `LanguageModelChatProvider` API: https://code.visualstudio.com/api/extension-guides/ai/language-model-chat-provider
- Existing `/compact` UX: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/chat/copilot-chat-context

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: 24H2 26100.7462

IŌĆÖm seeing an intermittent issue with VS CodeŌĆÖs terminal restoration.

My workflow is:

1. Open a project folder on Windows in VS Code.
2. Create a terminal in the editor area using ŌĆ£Create Terminal in Editor AreaŌĆØ.
3. Enter WSL from that terminal.
4. Use Codex CLI inside WSL to work on the project.
5. Close VS Code.
6. Later, reopen VS Code with one of my Windows project folders.

I use this same workflow across multiple different Windows project folders.

Expected behavior:

When VS Code restores the terminal in the editor area, it should restore the terminal with the working directory associated with the currently opened project/workspace.

Actual behavior:

Occasionally, the restored terminal starts in the directory of a different project that I had previously opened in VS Code. It seems like the restored terminal state or working directory is sometimes mixed up between different projects/workspaces.

This is problematic because I may think IŌĆÖm working inside the current project, but the restored terminal is actually pointing to another projectŌĆÖs directory.

Additional context:

* The projects are Windows folders opened in VS Code.
* The terminal is created in the editor area.
* Inside the terminal, I enter WSL and run Codex CLI.
* The issue only happens occasionally, not every time.
* It appears to happen across multiple projects that use the same workflow.

title: Chat: add a setting to suppress or delay the sessions list hover preview
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

The sessions list hover in the Chat view shows a full rendered conversation preview almost immediately on mouse-over. Because the sessions list sits inside the panel you're actively navigating, the hover fires constantly just from moving the mouse toward a session you want to click.

The issue is specifically the combination of trigger speed and popup size. This isn't a small tooltip..it renders the conversation content in a sizeable overlay, and it often (unsure timing?) appears very quickly. It's super distracting (and obscures my code) when I'm clicking through multiple sessions quickly. I can't seem to find a workaround.

workbench.hover.delay does not affect this. The sessions hover appears to be driven by a separate mechanism in the Copilot Chat extension. I searched and couldn't find another setting to control this. Also couldn't find an issue

What I'd like:

A setting to either disable the sessions hover preview or configure its delay independently ŌĆö something like chat.viewSessions.hoverDelay, accepting a number of milliseconds or false to disable. Ideally this mirrors the editor.hover.delay / editor.hover.enabled pattern already established for editor hovers.

Use case:

The sessions list is a navigation control. Hovering over it is how you get to clicking it, not a signal that you want a preview. Users who find the preview useful can keep it; users who don't should be able to turn it off without side effects. The feature is cool and useful sometimes, but I'd like to have more control about when it comes up, because most of the time I do not want it.

title: VS Code 1.122.0 on Arch Linux GNOME Wayland opens xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast picker on startup
body:
Hello,

After upgrading `visual-studio-code-bin` from `1.121.0-1` to `1.122.0-1` on Arch Linux, VS Code started showing the GNOME/xdg-desktop-portal screen sharing picker every time I launch the editor.

The dialog says that `org.chromium.Chromium` wants to share the screen, similar to what appears when Discord or a browser requests screen sharing. However, this happens immediately when opening VS Code, without starting any screen sharing action.

I confirmed that the request comes from VS Code itself, not from an extension or another Chromium/Electron application.

## Environment

* OS: Arch Linux
* Desktop: GNOME
* Session: Wayland
* VS Code package: `visual-studio-code-bin`
* Affected version: `1.122.0-1`
* Previous working version: `1.121.0-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal: `1.20.4-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal-gnome: `50.0-1`
* xdg-desktop-portal-gtk: `1.15.3-1`

Relevant environment variables:

```text
XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland
XDG_CURRENT_DESKTOP=GNOME
XDG_SESSION_DESKTOP=gnome
WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0
GDK_BACKEND=
GTK_USE_PORTAL=
ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install or upgrade to `visual-studio-code-bin 1.122.0-1` on Arch Linux.
2. Launch VS Code with a clean profile and extensions disabled:

```bash
code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
```

3. The xdg-desktop-portal screen sharing picker appears immediately.

## Debugging performed

I verified that this still happens with:

```bash
code --disable-extensions
```

and also with a clean user data directory and empty extensions directory:

```bash
code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
```

I monitored D-Bus calls to the ScreenCast portal:

```bash
dbus-monitor --session "interface='org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast'"
```

When launching VS Code, the following calls appear:

```text
method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=CreateSession
  session_handle_token "webrtc_session475676062"
  handle_token "webrtc_1473632979"

method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=SelectSources
  types uint32 1
  multiple false
  cursor_mode uint32 4
  persist_mode uint32 1

method call sender=:1.158 -> destination=:1.58 path=/org/freedesktop/portal/desktop; interface=org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast; member=Start
```

Then I mapped the D-Bus sender with:

```bash
busctl --user status :1.158
```

The result showed that the sender is VS Code itself:

```text
Comm=code
Exe=/opt/visual-studio-code/code
CommandLine=/opt/visual-studio-code/code --disable-extensions --user-data-dir /tmp/vscode-clean --extensions-dir /tmp/vscode-empty
CGroup=/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.chromium.Chromium-310897.scope
```

I also checked the portal logs:

```bash
journalctl --user -f -u xdg-desktop-portal -u xdg-desktop-portal-gnome -u xdg-desktop-portal-gtk
```

The GNOME portal logs showed repeated messages like:

```text
xdg-desktop-portal-gnome: Failed to associate portal window with parent window
```

## Version comparison

The issue appeared after upgrading:

```text
visual-studio-code-bin 1.121.0-1 -> 1.122.0-1
```

After downgrading back to:

```text
visual-studio-code-bin 1.121.0-1
```

the issue disappeared.

## Additional notes

Because the issue still occurs with extensions disabled, a clean user data directory, and an empty extensions directory, this does not appear to be caused by a VS Code extension.

It looks like VS Code/Electron/Chromium is making a WebRTC ScreenCast portal request on startup in version `1.122.0`, at least on Arch Linux + GNOME Wayland.

These settings currently accept a manually entered model ID, which makes them hard to discover and easy to misconfigure:

- `github.copilot.selectedCompletionModel`
- `github.copilot.chat.askAgent.model`
- `github.copilot.chat.exploreAgent.model`
- `github.copilot.chat.implementAgent.model`
