8b640eef5a6c6089c029249d48efa5c99adf7d51

x64

- OS Version: nixos

Steps to Reproduce:

1. 
2.

## bug

wrong cursor location when trigger beginning-of-line under color PS1 echo string length >= 16

## prepare

```json
  "terminal.integrated.profiles.linux": {
    "reproduce": {
      "path": "/tmp/reproduce.sh",
    },
  },
  "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.linux": "reproduce",
```

use `/tmp` as cwd to reproduce fixed length PS1

```sh
#!/bin/bash
env -C /tmp PS1="\\033[1;32m[\\w]\\\$\\033[0m " bash --rcfile /dev/null -i
```

### expected

```console
[/tmp]$ echo 123456789_12345
```

Ctrl+A

```console
[/tmp]$ <cursor>e</cursor>cho 123456789_12345
```

## unexpected

```console
[/tmp]$ echo 123456789_123456
```

Ctrl+A

```console
[/tmp]$ echo 123456<cursor>7</cursor>89_123456
```

```console
[/tmp]$ echo 123456789_123456789_123456789_123456789_123
```

Ctrl+A

```console
[/tmp]$ echo 123456<cursor>7</cursor>89_123456789_123456789_123456789_123
```

title: Test: Cloud provider on Tasks API
body:
# Test: Cloud provider on Tasks API

- [x] anyOS @lramos15
- [x] anyOS @ntrogh

Complexity: 3

Author: @osortega


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319178%0A%0A&assignees=osortega)

---

## Context

This TPI covers running GitHub Cloud Agent sessions against the new Task API (v2) backend, same behavior as v1 but driven by well defined events instead of parsing action logs.

## Setup

1. Latest VS Code Insiders.
2. Sign in to GitHub Copilot.
3. Set "github.copilot.chat.cloudAgentBackend.version": "v2" and reload the window.
4. Open any repo enrolled for Copilot coding agent

## What to test
1. Start a new task and watch it stream
2. Try both regular chat or chat editor
3. Try delegation flow, from local chat to cloud chat
4. Try follow up requests to existing sessions
5. Make sure data is parsed correctly and displayed correctly
6. Toggle between v1 and v2 and make sure there are backwards compatible, including archiving and unread/read session status

title: Recommend Config-Driven Approach to Avoid Magic Numbers in Copilot-Generated Code
body:
**** Type: **Feature Request** I recommend that GitHub Copilot and Copilot Chat suggest using configuration files (YAML, JSON, TOML, .env, etc.) and a config loader/helper for any tunable values, rather than hardcoded literals or constants. This approach: - Centralizes all adjustable values outside the codebase. - Makes code more maintainable, testable, and environment-agnostic. - Allows non-developers to adjust behavior without code changes. - Is a recognized best practice for high-quality, production-grade software. Please consider making this the default recommendation in Copilot-generated code and documentation, and highlight it in best practices for all users. **Draft Documentation Template (for internal or public docs):** - Best Practice: Avoid Magic Numbers with Config Files - Never hardcode tunable values (timeouts, limits, thresholds, etc.) in code. - Do not use constants for values that may change between environments or deployments. - Store all such values in a configuration file (YAML, JSON, TOML, .env, etc.). - Use a config loader/helper in your code to read these values at runtime. - Document all config options and provide sample/default config files. Example (Python): # config.yaml timeout_seconds: 30 # config_loader.py import yaml def load_config(path='config.yaml'): with open(path) as f: return yaml.safe_load(f) # main.py config = load_config() timeout = config['timeout_seconds'] Benefits: - Centralizes configuration - Eases maintenance and updates - Supports environment-specific overrides - Improves code clarity and flexibility Thank you for your consideration and support. title: When changing display language and restarting, only one window is reopened
body:
1. On Mac OS, open multiple VS Code Insiders windows
1. Run the command to change the display language
1. VS Code will restart to apply the new language
1. Only one of the previously open windows will reopen. The rest won't.

Expected: All previously open windows should reopen.

title: Browser: A few UI strings aren't properly localized
body:
Steps to Reproduce:

1. In the extension marketplace, install the official Pseudo Language Language Pack https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=MS-CEINTL.vscode-language-pack-qps-ploc
2. Set the display language to Pseudo Language
3. Open the Integrated Browser
4. Notice that there are several strings that don't show up "translated" to Pseudo Language. Even some that have been there for a while. For example, "Enter a URL", "Use Add Element to Chat to reference UI elements in chat prompts.", etc.

Similarly, the same strings aren't translated into any other language, like Spanish.

title: Model picker keeps reverting to Claude Sonnet 4.6 / Medium
body: ### Description I choose a specific model, like Opus 4.7 with High thinking effort. The model picker keeps reverting my explicit choice, during a session. I have to constantly keep track so I don't send a prompt to the wrong model while working on some complex topic. I would expect my choice to be honored during the session. I am also wondering how this affects the context window, as well as continuity of the session. I feel this is a major bug. title: Support ~/.copilot/prompts folder for custom prompts
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

User instructions, skills, and agents can be stored in the `~/.copilot/` folder, e.g. `~/.copilot/agents` and `~/.copilot/skills`. However, for custom prompts there is no support for a `~/.copilot/prompts` folder.

While it's true that this artifact is only available in IDEs, storing prompts in the same folder would make management easier. Currently, the only workaround is to manually add the location in the `chat.promptFilesLocations` setting. For new users, it would be more intuitive to have `~/.copilot/prompts` included as a default location.

Expected behavior:
The `~/.copilot/prompts` folder is supported as a default location for custom prompts, consistent with `~/.copilot/agents` and `~/.copilot/skills`.

Actual behavior:
The `~/.copilot/prompts` folder is not recognized. Custom prompts can only be stored elsewhere or by manually configuring `chat.promptFilesLocations`.

title: Inconsistent tool list syntax between "Configure Tools..." CodeLens and tools icon for custom agents
body: Version: 1.122.1 (Universal) Commit: 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e Date: 2026-05-29T09:25:37+02:00 (1 day ago) Electron: 39.8.8 ElectronBuildId: 13870025 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 25.1.0 When updating the tool list of a custom agent, the "Configure Tools..." CodeLens behaves differently from the tools icon in the chat. When I add an entire MCP server using the "Configure Tools..." CodeLens, the tools are added using wildcard syntax, e.g. `microsoftdocs/*`. CodeLens ŌĆö note that it adds the tools as microsoftdocs/*. 3. add the same MCP server's tools via the tools icon in the chat ŌĆö note that all tools are added individually. Expected behavior: Both methods produce consistent tool list syntax... both use the wildcard syntax Actual behavior: The "Configure Tools..." CodeLens uses wildcard syntax, while the tools icon expands all tools individually.

title: Selected custom agent is not persisted across VS Code restarts
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

Version: 1.122.1 (Universal)
Commit: 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e
Date: 2026-05-29T09:25:37+02:00 (1 day ago)
Electron: 39.8.8
ElectronBuildId: 13870025
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.1.0

When using a custom agent within a workspace, the selected agent should be remembered after closing and reopening VS Code. Instead, the agent selection always resets to the default agent on startup.

This is particularly annoying when I have created an optimized agent with my preferred set of tools... having to re-select it manually every time I reopen the workspace adds unnecessary friction to the workflow.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a workspace in VS Code
2. Select a custom agent (other than the default)
3. Close VS Code
4. Reopen the same workspace

Expected behavior:
The last selected agent remains active after reopening the workspace.

Actual behavior:
The selection reverts to the default agent every time VS Code is restarted.

title: Copilot wont complete any tasks.
body:
Type: Bug It doesnt matter the task or request, theres a 95% chance that gpt-5.4 will write one line like "I'm going to look at the wiring first and then come up with a plan" and then just stop. It's been happening since the outage yesterday morning. I have reset everything, uninstalled, cleared every cache i can find. Edited every github setting I can find. title: BYOK fails with `apiType: "responses"` but works with `apiType: "completion"`
body: ## Description When using BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) in VS Code Chat, requests fail when `apiType` is configured as `responses`. The same provider, endpoint, API key, and model work correctly when switching to `apiType: "completion"`. The failure appears to originate from the Copilot extension itself rather than the provider response. ## Error ```text Sorry, your request failed. Please try again. Client Request Id: f4f6873a-1dd6-4181-ba3c-d2ae2bcc077e Reason: Please check your firewall rules and network connection then try again. Error Code: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length') Error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length') at dG._provideLanguageModelResponse (.../extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js) ``` ## Reproduction Steps 1. Configure BYOK provider. 2. Set: ```json { "apiType": "responses" } ``` 3. Send a chat request. 4. Observe failure with: `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')` 5. Change configuration to: ```json { "apiType": "completion" } ``` 6. Send the same request. 7. Request succeeds. ## Expected Behavior Requests should work identically when using the Responses API, or a clear validation error should be shown if the provider response is unsupported. ## Actual Behavior The Copilot extension throws: ```text Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length') ``` ńēłµ£¼: 1.122.1 (system setup) µÅÉõ║ż: 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e µŚźµ£¤: 2026-05-29T09:25:37+02:00 Electron: 39.8.8 ElectronBuildId: 13870025 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.19045

Steps to Reproduce:

VS Code Version

Version: 1.122.0

code-1.122.0-1779917622.el8.x86_64

Working version:

code-1.121.0-1779186559.el8.x86_64

OS

Fedora 44 Workstation

Wayland session

Reproduction Steps
Install VS Code 1.122.0.
Log into a Wayland session.
Launch VS Code.
Observe that a screen-sharing permission dialog appears immediately on startup.
Expected Result

VS Code should start normally without requesting screen-sharing permissions when no screen-sharing functionality has been initiated.

Actual Result

A desktop portal screen-sharing permission dialog appears on every VS Code startup.

The dialog closes immediately when VS Code is closed.

Additional Information

The issue started after upgrading from 1.121.0 to 1.122.0.

Downgrading back to 1.121.0 completely resolves the issue.

The issue occurs even when launching VS Code with:

code --disable-extensions

Disabling extensions such as Live Share does not resolve the issue.

Logs

journalctl output:

third_party/webrtc/modules/desktop_capture/linux/wayland/screencast_portal.cc:369 Failed to start the screen cast session.

third_party/webrtc/modules/desktop_capture/linux/wayland/base_capturer_pipewire.cc:93 ScreenCastPortal failed: 2

D-Bus Monitoring

Monitoring org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast shows VS Code creating a WebRTC screencast session during startup:

org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast CreateSession

org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast SelectSources

org.freedesktop.portal.ScreenCast Start

The session tokens are named:

webrtc_session...

webrtc_...

which suggests the request originates from Electron/Chromium WebRTC desktop capture code.

Notes
Reproducible on every launch of VS Code 1.122.0
Not reproducible on VS Code 1.121.0
Appears to be a regression introduced between 1.121.0 and 1.122.0

title: Copilot agent model settings silently ignored when model ID format doesn't match internal ID ŌĆö falls back to system default with no warning
body:
## Summary

When a workspace sets `github.copilot.chat.askAgent.model` (or equivalent settings for other agent types) to a model name that doesn't match the exact internal model ID used by the Copilot API, the setting is **silently ignored** and the session falls back to whatever the system-level default model is ŌĆö with no error, warning, or UI indication that the configured model isn't being used.

## VS Code / Extension Versions

- **VS Code:** 1.122.1
- **GitHub Copilot Chat:** 0.50.1
- **OS:** Windows 11

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a multi-root workspace.
2. In `*.code-workspace` settings, configure model overrides using the "display name" format:
   ```json
   {
     "settings": {
       "chat.planAgent.defaultModel": "GPT-5.4 (copilot)",
       "github.copilot.chat.askAgent.model": "GPT-5.4 (copilot)",
       "github.copilot.chat.implementAgent.model": "GPT-5.4 (copilot)",
       "github.copilot.chat.exploreAgent.model": "GPT-5.4 (copilot)"
     }
   }
   ```
3. Open a Copilot Agent chat session.
4. Check which model is actually serving the session.

## Expected Behavior

One of:
- The setting is honored (i.e., the model ID lookup is case-insensitive and/or matches display names like `"GPT-5.4 (copilot)"` to internal ID `gpt-5.4`), **OR**
- A visible warning is shown in the UI: "Configured model `GPT-5.4 (copilot)` not found ŌĆö using default (`gpt-5-mini`)"

## Actual Behavior

- The setting is silently ignored.
- The session uses the system default model instead (`gpt-5-mini` or `claude-sonnet-4.6` depending on session state).
- There is **no error, no warning, no log entry** visible to the user.

## Root Cause Evidence

The Copilot debug session log (`models.json`) shows the actual available model IDs. The display name used in settings does **not** match any entry:

| Setting value (user configured) | Actual internal model ID |
|---|---|
| `GPT-5.4 (copilot)` | `gpt-5.4` |

The models snapshot (`models.json`) at session start lists `gpt-5.4` with `"state": "enabled"` and no entry for `GPT-5.4 (copilot)`. Because no match is found, the setting is silently dropped and the default model (`gpt-5-mini`, `is_chat_default: true`) is used instead.

The Copilot Chat extension log confirms every actual agent LLM request went to `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (an older session) rather than the configured GPT-5.4 ŌĆö across all four agent types.

## Impact

Users who configure model overrides using names visible in the UI (e.g., from the model picker's display names) get no feedback that their settings aren't working. They believe GPT-5.4 is being used when it isn't.

## Workaround

Use the exact lowercase internal model ID:
```json
"github.copilot.chat.askAgent.model": "gpt-5.4"
```

But this workaround requires users to know the internal ID format, which is not documented or surfaced anywhere in the UI.

## Suggested Fix

- Make the model ID lookup case-insensitive and strip parenthetical suffixes like `(copilot)`, or
- Match against both the `id` field and the `name` field in the models list, or
- Emit a warning in the Copilot Chat output panel when a configured model ID cannot be resolved, showing both the configured value and what was actually used.

title: Copilot Sessions right-pane (Files) disappears immediately on first message when workspace root is a non-repo multi-root folder
body:
## Summary

When using VS Agent (Copilot CLI / Copilot Sessions) inside a multi-root workspace where the session's working directory is scoped to a **non-git-repo** workspace root folder, the **right-side "Files" pane disappears the instant the first message is sent**. The spinner on the workspace file also spins indefinitely. The pane is briefly visible before the first message, but clears as soon as a turn starts.

This reproduces every time, across clean relaunches, regardless of workspace file changes.

---

## Environment

- **VS Code:** 1.122.1
- **Copilot Chat:** 0.50.1
- **OS:** Windows 11
- **Workspace type:** Multi-root `.code-workspace`
- **Working directory:** `c:\Users\robin\Downloads\cluster` ŌĆö a folder that contains 4 child git repos (`AI-Fabric`, `Agent-99`, `BackupDevice`, `QuickPermit`) but is **not itself a git repository**

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a `.code-workspace` file that lists child folders but whose own root is not a git repo.
2. Open VS Agent (Copilot Sessions pane).
3. Start a new chat ŌĆö note the **Files** pane on the right shows folder structure.
4. Type any message and press **Enter**.
5. **Immediately:** the Files pane goes blank and does not return for the rest of the session.

---

## Root Cause Evidence (from logs)

All evidence is from `%AppData%\Code\logs\20260529T070002\window11\`.

### 1. Session is always scoped to the non-repo root

Every session log line shows:
```
workingDirectory: file:///c%3A/Users/robin/Downloads/cluster, worktreePath: undefined
```

### 2. On first turn, Copilot tries to resolve Git repo info from `unknown:/`

Immediately after the turn starts:
```
[GitServiceImpl][getRepositoryFetchUrls] Failed to read remotes from .git/config:
ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'unknown:/'
```

### 3. Post-turn checkpoint skipped because no repo is found

```
[ChatSessionWorktreeCheckpointService][handleRequestCompleted]
No repository found for session 8ae67dd6-..., skipping post-turn checkpoint
```

### 4. Renderer floods with `unknown:/` ENOPRO errors (556+ per session)

```
[error] Failed to resolve files at location: unknown:/.github/agents
ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'unknown:/.github/agents'

[error] Failed to resolve files at location: unknown:/.github/instructions
ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'unknown:/.github/instructions'

[error] Failed to resolve files at location: unknown:/.vscode/mcp.json
ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'unknown:/.vscode/mcp.json'

[error] ENOPRO: No file system provider found for resource 'unknown:/.vscode': CodeExpectedError
```

These fire in a tight loop on every turn and every `listSessions` call.

### 5. `ChatSessionWorkspaceFolderService` warns for every session

```
[ChatSessionWorkspaceFolderService][getWorkspaceChanges]
No repository properties found for session <id>
```

This fires for **all** sessions (not just the active one) every time the workspace folder list changes.

---

## What is failing

The session pane / Files panel uses `unknown:/` as a fallback URI scheme when it cannot resolve a workspace folder to a real filesystem provider. When the active session's working directory resolves to a non-git-repo folder, the renderer's file watcher and snippet/config loader are called with `unknown:/...` paths on **every turn**, causing the `Eoe.doWatch` calls to throw `CodeExpectedError` in a loop. This appears to be what clears (and prevents re-rendering of) the Files pane.

The specific call chain in the renderer stack trace is:
```
Eoe.withProvider ŌåÆ Eoe.doWatch
Eoe.withProvider ŌåÆ Eoe.exists ŌåÆ IEe._initWorkspaceFolderSnippets
Eoe.withProvider ŌåÆ Eoe.readFile ŌåÆ n1e.loadConfiguration ŌåÆ O3t.loadFolderConfigurations ŌåÆ W3t._doUpdateFolders
```

All called with `unknown:/` URIs in a loop.

---

## Expected Behavior

- The Files pane should remain visible throughout the session, including for chats scoped to non-repo workspace root folders.
- If a session's working directory is not a git repository, Copilot should degrade gracefully (skip git-specific features) rather than triggering a flood of `unknown:/` URI resolution failures that crash the pane renderer.
- `unknown:/` should never be used as a workspace root URI during an active file-watching or config-loading operation.

---

## Actual Behavior

- Files pane disappears the instant the first message is sent.
- 550ŌĆō6500+ `unknown:/` ENOPRO errors are logged per session window.
- Spinner on the workspace file never resolves.
- All sessions in the workspace (not just the active one) receive `No repository properties found` warnings on every turn.

---

## Additional Notes

- Adding the root folder (`{ "name": "cluster", "path": "." }`) to the workspace's `folders` array does **not** resolve the issue. VS Code has a valid `file:///` path for the root, but the renderer still falls back to `unknown:/` on turn start.
- The bug is fully reproducible after a clean VS Code quit and relaunch.
- Sessions scoped to a child repo (e.g. starting a chat from inside `AI-Fabric`) do not trigger this because those folders have `.git`.

title: Custom Endpoint (BYOK) models disappear from model picker on VS Code restart - hasByokModels.lastKnown race condition
body:
## Describe the bug

After restarting VS Code, Custom Endpoint (BYOK) models that were previously added via Chat: Manage Language Models ŌåÆ Add Models ŌåÆ Custom Endpoint disappear from the model picker for several seconds, and sometimes never appear unless the user manually runs Developer: Reload Window.

This is caused by a startup timing race condition in the HasByokModelsContribution class.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Add a Custom Endpoint model (e.g., DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint) via the Language Models editor
2. Verify the model works and appears in the model picker as expected
3. Close all VS Code windows and reopen VS Code
4. Observe: The model picker initially shows only Copilot models - the Custom Endpoint (local) option is missing
5. Wait 5-15 seconds - the Custom Endpoint model eventually appears (but sometimes doesn't at all)

## Expected Behavior

Custom Endpoint models that have been successfully configured should appear in the model picker immediately upon VS Code startup, without any delay.

## Actual Behavior

The `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` storage key is restored as `false` on every cold startup, causing the `github.copilot.hasByokModels` context key to be `false`. The model picker hides all non-Copilot vendor models until the extension registration completes and triggers `_update()`.

## Technical Analysis

After inspecting the source code at `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/hasByokModelsContribution.ts`, the issue is:

1. `_restore()` reads `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` from storage - this is `false` on first startup
2. Even though `chatLanguageModels.json` is already configured with valid Custom Endpoint models, the `hasByokModels` context key remains `false`
3. The `_update()` method only corrects this **after** `whenInstalledExtensionsRegistered()` completes
4. During this window, the model picker hides all BYOK models

The key logic in `_update()`:
```typescript
if (!this._extensionsRegistered) {
    if (this._contextKeyService.getContextKeyValue<boolean>(
        ChatContextKeys.nonCopilotLanguageModelsAreUserSelectable.key)) {
        this._setResult(true);
    }
    return;
}
```

The optimistic flip only triggers if `nonCopilotLanguageModelsAreUserSelectable` is set, which isnt always the case on cold start.

Additionally, for users behind an HTTP proxy (e.g. `http.proxy: http://localhost:65532`), the Custom Endpoint providers model validation may fail during startup if the proxy is not ready yet, causing the entire provider group to be silently dropped from the model cache.

## Environment

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat 0.50.1
- Proxy configured: `http.proxy: http://localhost:65532`

## Additional Context

The `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` key is stored in `state.vscdb` (SQLite) at `%APPDATA%\Code\User\globalStorage\state.vscdb`. Manually setting this to `true` before restart forces the models to appear immediately, confirming the root cause is the stale `false` value.

Related code files:
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/hasByokModelsContribution.ts`
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/customEndpointProvider.ts`
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts`

title: MCP: Atlassian Remote MCP OAuth fails with "scope not added to app" ŌĆö VS Code app missing write:all:twg scope
body:
Type: Bug 1. Add an Atlassian Remote MCP server to `mcp.json`: ``` ```json { "servers": { "atlassian": { "type": "http", "url": "https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2" } } } ``` 2. Run MCP: List Servers and start the server. VS Code opens a browser to Atlassian's OAuth consent page. 3. The consent page renders an error instead of a consent prompt; OAuth never completes and the MCP server never connects. Error shown by Atlassian's auth server (addressed to the owner of the VS Code OAuth app): ``` Something went wrong INFORMATION FOR THE OWNER OF VISUAL STUDIO CODE This app has requested scopes that have not been added to the app. Configure the app at[ https://developer.atlassian.com/apps](https://developer.atlassian.com/apps) and add the following scopes to the app: write:all:twg. ``` Expected: Consent page renders, user approves, VS Code receives tokens, MCP server connects. Actual: Atlassian rejects the authorization request because write:all:twg is being requested by the VS Code OAuth app but is not declared on that app's registration on [developer.atlassian.com](http://developer.atlassian.com/). Confirmed upstream by Atlassian support: ``` There appears to be a rollout of a new scope write:all:twg and the VSCode app is requesting the scope, however the VSCode app has not been updated to declare the write:all:twg scope. We are asking customers to reach out to the VSCode support team to request that they publish their app with this scope declared. ``` Where in the flow: The error occurs during the OAuth consent redirect, before any MCP tool invocation. The browser opens to [mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2](http://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2) and the consent page itself errors out ŌĆö scopes are never displayed to the user. Impact: Affects every VS Code user trying to use Atlassian's Remote MCP server. The OAuth client is owned by VS Code, so users and organizations cannot work around it. Asks: - Add write:all:twg to the VS Code OAuth app registration on [developer.atlassian.com](http://developer.atlassian.com/) and re-publish. - Is there a public channel where Atlassian-side scope rollouts can be tracked so the VS Code app registration stays in sync? This kind of breakage is invisible until users hit it. References: - Atlassian Remote MCP docs:[ https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/setting-up-ides-for-the-atlassian-remote-mcp-server/](https://support.atlassian.com/rovo/docs/setting-up-ides-for-the-atlassian-remote-mcp-server/) - VS Code MCP docs:[ https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/chat/mcp-servers](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/chat/mcp-servers) Happy to provide redacted MCP output channel logs on request. title: Auto-completion disabled for languages with ID >= 256 due to LanguageID bits overlapping with TokenType bits
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No - VS Code Version: 1.92.2 - OS Version: I have discovered an issue in vscode's LineTokens metadata generation, specifically related to the 8-bit limit for LanguageID. According to the official blog ([Syntax Highlighting Optimizations](https://code.visualstudio.com/blogs/2017/02/08/syntax-highlighting-optimizations#_tokens-theme-matching)), vscode utilizes the first 8 bits of the metadata integer for LanguageID and bits 9-11 for TokenType. In getDefaultMetadata, the value is constructed as follows: ```TypeScript // vscode/src/vs/editor/common/tokens/contiguousTokensStore.ts public getTokens(topLevelLanguageId: string, lineIndex: number, lineText: string): LineTokens { let rawLineTokens: Uint32Array | ArrayBuffer | null = null; if (lineIndex < this._len) { rawLineTokens = this._lineTokens[lineIndex]; } if (rawLineTokens !== null && rawLineTokens !== EMPTY_LINE_TOKENS) { return new LineTokens(toUint32Array(rawLineTokens), lineText, this._languageIdCodec); } const lineTokens = new Uint32Array(2); lineTokens[0] = lineText.length; lineTokens[1] = getDefaultMetadata(this._languageIdCodec.encodeLanguageId(topLevelLanguageId)); return new LineTokens(lineTokens, lineText, this._languageIdCodec); } ... function getDefaultMetadata(topLevelLanguageId) { return ((topLevelLanguageId << MetadataConsts.LANGUAGEID_OFFSET) // <- here | (StandardTokenType.Other << MetadataConsts.TOKEN_TYPE_OFFSET) | ... ) >>> 0; } ``` ### Reproduction When the number of registered languages exceeds 255 (the 8-bit limit), topLevelLanguageId values for subsequent languages will overflow. Because topLevelLanguageId is shifted and bitwise OR-ed with StandardTokenType, this overflow causes a carry-over into the bits reserved for TokenType. For example, a LanguageID of 325 (binary 1 0100 0101) effectively truncates/overflows into the TokenType bit field, make the metadata becomes to `xxxx 001 0100 0101`, which should be `xxxx 000 (1)0100 0101`. A StandardTokenType.Others (0) bit field is erroneously modified to StandardTokenType.Comments (1) due to this bit carry-over. ### Actual Since quickSuggestion logic relies on TokenType being Other to trigger by default, this metadata corruption causes auto-completion to be silently disabled for all languages with an ID >= 256. ### Expected Even though it might not be a long-term goal to support more than 255 languages in a single instance, this behavior is a silent failure that leads to difficult-to-debug issues. I propose implementing a bitwise mask or modulo operation on topLevelLanguageId (e.g., topLevelLanguageId & 0xFF) before shifting to ensure that overflow does not contaminate the TokenType field.

title: Safety Report: AI Coding Extensions Lack Enforceable Guardrails ŌĆö AWS Account Destroyed
body:
## AI Guardrails Do Not Work ŌĆö 56-Day Empirical Proof

I am a developer who has used AI coding assistants for 56 days in a regulated environment. During that time:

- **32 workflow violations** occurred despite configuring every available guardrail mechanism
- The AI **destroyed my AWS management account** by deploying Terraform to the wrong target
- My business has been **down for 15+ days** with no recovery path
- **9 AWS Support cases** opened ŌĆö none resolved
- **$106,000+ in business losses** from a single $0.03 AI operation

### Guardrails Configured (All Failed)

| Mechanism | Result |
|-----------|--------|
| Agent system prompt with STOP language | Ignored after relogin |
| Workspace rule files | Not enforced |
| MCP server resources | Not enforced |
| Knowledge base indexing | Not enforced |
| Incident documentation | Not read on session start |
| Control documents | Not enforced |
| Violation counter rules | No persistent state |

### The Core Problem

The agent treats workflow rules as **suggestions, not constraints**. There is no mechanism that prevents implementation from starting. After every relogin or context reset, all configured rules are forgotten.

### What Is Needed

1. **Hard gates** ŌĆö physically block file creation until requirements doc exists
2. **Persistent violation state** ŌĆö survive relogins, context compaction, session resets
3. **Authorization taxonomy** ŌĆö "yes" ŌēĀ "approved" ŌĆö enforce at platform level
4. **Blast radius limits** ŌĆö one conversational turn = max one infrastructure change
5. **Mandatory dry-run** ŌĆö destructive operations require preview + separate confirmation
6. **Session boundary enforcement** ŌĆö re-read and acknowledge rules after any reset

### Evidence

- Full case study: https://gist.github.com/tzb1-ai/4758f2720979a03d773815082486933c
- Paper: https://d18gqyv10pt526.cloudfront.net/white-papers/AI-cost-savings-analysis.md
- Presentation: https://d18gqyv10pt526.cloudfront.net/presentation.html

This is not a feature request. This is a **safety report**. The current architecture of prompt-based governance is fundamentally broken and poses existential risk to businesses using these tools for infrastructure management.

At enterprise scale (10,000 accounts), the same failure pattern produces $500MŌĆō$4B+ in damages.

**Prompt-based rules are documentation. They are not enforcement.**

title: workbench.action.findInFiles: accept file URIs to bypass argv-length limits
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

## Problem

Extensions that want to scope a search to a programmatically-built file set
can only pass `filesToInclude: string` to `workbench.action.findInFiles` /
`search.action.openNewEditor`. The string is parsed back into globs,
expanded by `spreadGlobComponents`, and emitted as `-g` args to ripgrep.

On Windows the 8191-character argv limit is hit quickly. With the ~3x
expansion factor from progressive-path globs (`src` ŌåÆ `src/x` ŌåÆ `src/x/y.ts`),
a few hundred files is enough to overflow. Extensions are forced to cap the include pattern length (I use ~4000 chars).

I've gone to some length in devising a workaround and best I can do is batch the file list and open *multiple* search editors for one logical search ŌĆö fragmenting results.

## Use cases

My extension Fresh File Explorer (https://github.com/FreHu/vscode-fresh-file-explorer) has two:
- "Search in fresh files" (meaning pending + files last touched in last x days based on git history)
- "Search in found files" (chain a search scoped to the files found in a previous search)

## Existing internal solution

I noticed in the release notes that you added a toggle to search in modified files only, and had Claude look at how that works.

The built-in "Search in Modified Files" toggle builds `changedFileUris: URI[]` from the SCM
service and hands it to `QueryBuilder.commonQueryFromFileList`, which:

- Groups files by workspace root ŌåÆ one `folderQuery` per root
- Emits per-folder `includePattern` as a glob *object*
  (`{ "rel/path": true }`) ŌĆö no brace string, no `spreadGlobComponents`
- Sets `excludePattern: { '**/*': true }`

This gives ripgrep one `-g` per file and a fresh argv budget
per workspace folder. The argv limit effectively stops being a problem at
realistic file counts.

References:
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/search/browser/searchView.ts` ~L1654 (URI collection)
- `src/vs/workbench/services/search/common/queryBuilder.ts` `commonQueryFromFileList`

## Request

Extend `IFindInFilesArgs` (and the `search.action.openNewEditor` args) to
accept either:

- `filesToIncludeUris?: URI[]` ŌĆö same path the SCM toggle uses, or
- `filesToInclude: string | URI[]` ŌĆö overload the existing field

Either should route through `commonQueryFromFileList` instead of the
string-parsing path.

## Workaround (current)

I cap the generated `filesToInclude` string at ~4000 chars (well under
8191 to leave room for the `spreadGlobComponents` 3x expansion) and, when
the file set doesn't fit, split it into batches that each open in their
own search editor tab.

Concretely, for a single user action ("search in my N fresh files") my
extension may open 3, 5, sometimes 10+ search editor tabs. This breaks
the search UX in ways that are hard to paper over:

**1. The search viewlet is unreachable once batching kicks in.**
The viewlet shows exactly one search. The moment I need >1 batch I'm
forced into search *editors*, even for users who configured "open in
viewlet". Their config silently loses.

**2. Results are fragmented across tabs with no unified view.**
The user wants one result list. They get N. No combined match count, no
single "next match" navigation, no cross-batch sorting by file. To see
all hits for `foo` they alt-tab between editors and mentally merge.

**3. Refinement multiplies the pain.**
Tweaking the query (toggle case sensitivity, change regex, narrow the
pattern) means re-running it in every tab, one at a time. There is no
"re-run across all batches" affordance and I can't build one ŌĆö each
search editor is an independent VS Code document.

**4. Replace-all is effectively broken.**
A replace operation on batch 1 has no idea batches 2..N exist. The user
either replaces per-tab (error-prone, easy to miss files) or gives up
and uses something else.

**5. Files with very long paths get silently dropped.**
If a single file's relative path exceeds the per-batch budget on its own
ŌĆö possible in deeply nested monorepos or under long workspace prefixes ŌĆö
I log a warning and skip it. The user sees "N files searched" but
doesn't know which were excluded unless they read my output channel.

**6. The cap is conservatively wrong on multi-root workspaces.**
The internal toggle splits argv budget *per workspace folder*. I can't,
because I go through the single `filesToInclude` string. A 3-root
workspace with 1500 files would fit comfortably through the URI[] path
(500 per ripgrep invocation, three invocations) but blows my single
~4000-char string and triggers batching.

**7. I can't point users at the built-in toggle as a substitute.**
The toggle only covers working-tree-dirty files. My use case ("files
changed in the last N days, including committed history") is a
superset. The overlap is partial, so "just use the SCM toggle" is wrong
advice for most of my users.

**8. The 3x post-expansion tax forces a conservative budget.**
I emit a single comma+brace-optimized include string
(`src/{a.ts,b.ts},lib/c.ts`). VS Code's
`performBraceExpansionForRipgrep` un-braces it, then
`spreadGlobComponents` progressively splits each path into every
prefix: `src/components/Button.tsx` becomes
`-g src -g src/components -g src/components/Button.tsx`. A path of
depth N produces N `-g` args.

The argv limit is on the *post-expansion* ripgrep invocation, not my
input string. So I have to budget my single string against the
explosion that happens after it crosses the API boundary ŌĆö which is why
my cap is ~4000 chars rather than something closer to 8191. The URI[]
path skips `spreadGlobComponents` entirely (it knows the entries are
exact file paths, not globs to be anchored), so one file ŌåÆ one `-g`
arg. Same files, ~3x less argv pressure, no guessing on my side about
what the expansion will produce.

title: [testplan-item] Browser: 'Add Area Screenshot to Chat' and 'Add Full Page Screenshot to Chat'
body: Refs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/299790 PRs https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/318791 https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/318932 - [x] Windows @deepak1556 - [x] macOS @vritant24 - [x] Linux @connor4312 Complexity: 3 [Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319108%0A%0A&assignees=jruales) --- ## Context The integrated browser in VS Code now supports multiple screenshot capture modes: area selection, and (experimental) full-page scrolling capture. Users can quickly insert screenshots from the browser into the active Copilot chat context, streamlining visual sharing during web-based debugging and UI review. ## Prerequisites Enable the experimental setting `"workbench.browser.experimentalUserTools.enabled": true,` ## Test Steps 1. Open the integrated browser in VS Code. 2. Navigate to a web page with visible content. 3. Locate the screenshot capture options in the browser toolbar next to the URL bar. 4. Select 'Add Area Screenshot to Chat'. - A region selection tool should appear, allowing you to drag and select an area of the page. - After selection, the image of the selected area should appear in the active Copilot chat as an attachment. 5. Select 'Add Full Page Screenshot to Chat (Experimental)'. - The screenshot should include the entire scrollable page, not just the visible viewport. - The image should appear in the active Copilot chat as an attachment. ## Possible things to try * Screenshots at different browser zoom levels * Screenshots at different web page length or complexity * Screenshots on pages with scrollbars, overlays, or dynamic content. * Use the screenshots to provide context to the model, for making changes to a local site

title: More actions button is easily confused for minimize button
body:
Type: Bug The ... more actions button has been frustratingly added right next to the minimize button, making it look almost the same on smaller screens. I get that you are trying to force the Agents mode onto us, to shove it down our throats even if the first thing we did after seeing it was to remove it from the top bar, but just stop it. Revert the agents mode button to toggleable state, instead of hardcoded always visible button intentionally designed to be misclicked when the user wants to minimize the window. The top bar has always been fully customizable, so hardcoding any UI element to it screams having adversarial relationship with the users. title: [BYOM]: Models use Anthropic messages API  can not specify custom reasoning effort field path.
body: According to the VSCode BYOM doc users can config custom model, it support ChatCompletion api, Responses api, Messages api (Anthorpic) when I was configing a model which supports the messages api, reading the model's provider official doc, I found that it's Anthropic API need to provide a custom reasoning effort field called `output_config.effort` in the JSON body, for example: `{"output_config": {"effort": "high/max"}}`, I tried it in post man , it works fine. ### api doc: ### post man debugging ## But according to the VSCode's doc, the `reasoningEffortFormat` options only accept 'chat-completion' or 'responses' 2 string values, so the current problem is the default values are not enough. We need a way to specify a custom resoning effor field path. links: - [BYOM](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/customization/language-models#_model-configuration-reference) - [thinking_mode](https://api-docs.deepseek.com/guides/thinking_mode)

title: chat.tools.autoApprove: true does not suppress approval prompts for built-in agent tools
body:
## Type: Bug

## Description

With both `chat.tools.autoApprove` and `chat.tools.global.autoApprove` set to `true` in user settings, built-in Copilot agent tools still show "Allow Once" / "Cancel" approval dialogs for every call.

**Affected tools:** `grep_search`, `read_file`, `file_search`, `semantic_search`, `list_dir`, subagent calls (`runSubagent`), `fetch_webpage`, and others.

**Not affected:** Terminal commands (`run_in_terminal`) auto-approve correctly via `chat.tools.terminal.autoApprove`.

The "Always Allow" option is rarely or never shown in the dialog, so there is no way to permanently approve these tools from the UI either.

## Settings (confirmed in User settings.json)

```json
"chat.tools.autoApprove": true,
"chat.tools.global.autoApprove": true
```

## Environment

- VS Code version: 1.122.1 (arm64, macOS)
- OS: macOS (Darwin arm64)
- Copilot extension: latest

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Set both `chat.tools.autoApprove` and `chat.tools.global.autoApprove` to `true` in user settings
2. Open Agent mode chat
3. Ask the agent to perform any task that uses built-in tools (e.g., "scan the codebase for bugs")
4. Every tool call prompts for approval despite the settings

## Expected Behavior

Built-in agent tools should execute without approval prompts when `chat.tools.autoApprove: true` is set.

## Possibly Related

- #298909 (closed as fixed in ~1.110, but behavior persists on 1.122.1)
- #302362 (proposed API for programmatic permission resolution)

title: TPI) Agent host Copilot CLI async shell
body:
Refs: #318511, #318505

- [x] macOS @bhavyaus 
- [x] linux @vijayupadya 
- [x] windows @sandy081

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319093%0A%0A&assignees=anthonykim1)

---

Verify that Agent Host Copilot CLI surfaces async shell/background completions as a follow-up turn when the custom terminal tool is disabled.

Prerequisites:
- Use the latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Enable `chat.agentHost.enabled`
- Set `chat.agentHost.customTerminalTool.enabled` to `false` (so the SDK native shell tool is used)
- Install and authenticate Copilot CLI

Part 1: Background shell completion while idle
- Start an Agent Host Copilot CLI chat
- Ask Copilot to run a long-running command in the background (for example a build, a sleep-based task, or a script that finishes after some delay), then let the turn finish so the session is idle
- When the background shell completes, confirm a new system-initiated follow-up turn appears automatically
- Confirm the follow-up shows a compact system-initiated label rather than a fabricated user message

Part 2: Completion arriving during an active turn
- Kick off a background/detached shell, then keep Copilot busy with another request so a turn is active when the background work finishes
- Confirm the completion is shown inline within the active turn (no extra duplicate turn is spawned)

Part 3: Chained async shells
- Use a single prompt that drives two sequential async shells across turns, for example:
  ```
  Do what I say.
  Step 1: Run "sleep 6 && echo SHELL_A_DONE" as an async shell, then end your turn.
  After SHELL_A_DONE notification arrives, in your follow-up turn:
  Step 2: Run "sleep 6 && echo SHELL_B_DONE" as an async shell, then end that turn too.
  ```
- Confirm the first async shell launches and the turn ends
- Confirm the `SHELL_A_DONE` completion arrives as a system-initiated follow-up turn that then launches the second async shell
- Confirm the `SHELL_B_DONE` completion arrives as its own follow-up turn and the marker can be read back

Part 4: Exploratory
- Try several background/async commands and note any cases where:
  - completion is missed or never surfaces
  - duplicate or stale turns appear
  - the follow-up attaches to the wrong/previous turn
  - the system-initiated label is missing or confusing

title: Test: /rubber-duck agent
body:
Refs: #319089

- [x] anyOS @lostintangent
- [x] anyOS @benibenj

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319092%0A%0A&assignees=digitarald)

---

## Context

The rubber duck agent is a built-in critic that gives Copilot a second opinion on its plans and code using a different AI model. It's gated behind the `chat.agentHost.rubberDuck.enabled` setting.

## Setup

- Open Settings and enable `chat.agentHost.rubberDuck.enabled`
- Restart the agent host / Copilot CLI session

## Test Steps

### Setting gate
1. With the setting **disabled** (default), open Copilot CLI, type `/r` ŌĆö verify `/rubber-duck` does NOT appear in completions
2. Enable `chat.agentHost.rubberDuck.enabled`, restart the session
3.  `/rubber-duck` now appears

### Automatic invocation
4. Ask Copilot to plan a non-trivial multi-file refactor (e.g., "Plan how to extract the logging service into its own module")
5. Observe the timeline output ŌĆö verify Copilot consults the rubber duck agent and summarizes the critique (e.g., "The critique pointed outŌĆ”")
6. Ask a trivial question (e.g., "What is 2+2?") ŌĆö verify the rubber duck is NOT consulted

### Manual invocation
7. Ask Copilot to produce a plan, then send: "Rubber duck your plan"
8. Verify structured feedback is returned with severity categories (blocking / non-blocking / suggestions)
9. Alternatively, type `/rubber-duck` followed by a prompt ŌĆö verify critique is generated

### Read-only behavior
10. Verify the rubber duck critique does not edit any files or run mutating commands ŌĆö it only reads and reports

title: Test: /research slash command
body:
Refs: #319087

- [x] anyOS @eleanorjboyd 
- [x] anyOS @lszomoru

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319091%0A%0A&assignees=digitarald)

---

## Context

The `/research` slash command invokes a specialized deep-research agent that produces a Markdown report with citations on a given topic. It uses codebase tools, web search, and GitHub repo search autonomously.

## Test Steps

### Completion & parsing
1. Open Copilot CLI (AHP) session in Agents window
2. Type `/` and verify `/research` appears in completions
3. Select `/research` from completions ŌĆö verify a trailing space is inserted (ready for topic input)
4. Research delegates to multiple `Research` subagents for depth

### Report generation
4. Send `/research How does the VS Code extension host communicate with the renderer?`
5. Verify the agent produces a structured Markdown report with:
   - Headings and sections
   - Citations or references to source files / URLs
   - No clarifying questions asked (autonomous behavior)

### Report viewing & sharing
6. Report shows up as artifacts

### Web & cross-repo
10. Try `/research` with a web-focused topic (e.g., "latest changes to Node.js streams API") and verify web search results are included

title: Feature Request: Voice Control support in Agent Window (Copilot Chat)
body:
## Feature Request: Voice Control in Agent Window

### Summary

Voice control via the [VS Code Speech](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.vscode-speech) extension works well in the standard VS Code editor and chat interface, but is **not available in the Agent Window** (Copilot agent mode panel).

### Current Behavior

- VS Code Speech extension is installed and functioning correctly in VS Code (e.g., dictation in editor, voice input in standard Copilot Chat)
- The Agent Window does **not** expose a microphone / voice input button
- There is no way to use voice control to interact with the Copilot agent from the Agent Window

### Expected Behavior

The Agent Window should support voice input via the VS Code Speech extension, consistent with the experience in the standard Copilot Chat panel. A microphone button (or equivalent shortcut) should be available to activate voice dictation when composing prompts in the Agent Window.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install the [VS Code Speech](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode.vscode-speech) extension
2. Verify voice input works in VS Code editor or standard Copilot Chat
3. Open the Copilot **Agent Window** (agent mode)
4. Observe that there is no voice input option available

### Environment

- VS Code Speech extension: installed & working
- Feature gap: Agent Window lacks voice control integration

### Motivation

Voice control significantly improves accessibility and productivity. As the Agent Window becomes a primary interface for interacting with Copilot, parity with the rest of the VS Code speech experience is important for users who rely on or prefer voice input.

> yes it is related to 1.121.0 upgrade. Same here.

> I have the same issue here, I'm using Ubuntu 22.04 and VS Code 1.122.
```
The fix
$ apt list -a code | grep -w '1\.121\.0'
code/stable 1.121.0-1779186519 amd64
code/stable 1.121.0-1779186523 arm64
code/stable 1.121.0-1779186507 armhf

# Downgrade to the previous good version (Choose your arch, mine is AMD64)
sudo apt install code=1.121.0-1779186519

# Prevent automatic upgrades
sudo apt-mark hold code

# Allow automatic upgrades (If future VS Code version fixes this bug)
sudo apt-mark unhold code
```

### I would really appreciate it if the VS Code and Cline contributors could collaborate to make improvements. ­¤ÖÅ

body:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1 (user setup)
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100

**Steps to Reproduce**:

1. Open or create a new untitled Markdown file. Fill it with enough lines (e.g. 200 lines of `1\n\n` each) so the preview height exceeds the screen.
2. Scroll the editor to the top, then use `Ctrl+Shift+V` (`markdown.togglePreview`) to enter preview.
3. Scroll the preview to the bottom, then use `Ctrl+Shift+V` again to exit preview. Observe that the editor now shows the last few lines at the top ŌĆö the scroll position has been "locked" to the source line corresponding to the top of the preview at exit.
4. Scroll the editor back to the top (via scroll wheel or `Ctrl+Home`).
5. Switch to another editor (any language), then switch back to the Markdown editor ŌĆö using mouse click, `Ctrl+Tab` (`workbench.action.nextEditor` / `workbench.action.previousEditor`), or `Alt+ŌåÉ` (`workbench.action.navigateBack`).
6. **Bug**: The editor jumps back to the locked position from step 3. When switching back from another editor group via mouse click, the click-down event triggers an immediate scroll, which also creates an unintended text selection (as if the user were holding the mouse button while scrolling).

**Notes**:

- The issue persists with `--disable-extensions` (but built-in Markdown extension is still active).
- The following settings do NOT fix it:
  - `"markdown.preview.scrollPreviewWithEditor": false`
  - `"markdown.preview.scrollEditorWithPreview": false`
  - `code --user-data-dir "$env:TEMP\vscode-clean-test" --disable-extensions`  to create a window with clean profile
- The "locked" position is essentially the source line that was at the top of the preview when `togglePreview` was last used to exit preview mode. The editor remains bound to that scroll position until the next reload, even after manually scrolling away.

title: Chat: Unsent (draft) input should not surface when navigating back to the session list
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Describe the behavior you'd like:

When I have unsent/draft text in a chat session's input box and navigate "back" to the session list (the panel that lists all chat sessions), the draft text should remain scoped to that session ŌĆö it should not appear or carry over at the session-list level.

When I re-enter that specific session, the draft text should be restored as expected.

Current behavior:

Navigating back to the session list exposes or interacts with the unsent input in a way that feels like the draft is "leaking" out of its session context.

Expected behavior:

Draft input is strictly scoped to its session
Navigating up to the session list: draft is hidden / not surfaced
Re-entering the session: draft is restored exactly as left
Why this matters:

When managing multiple chat sessions, navigating between them or starting a new session from the list should be a clean action. Unsent content from a previous session creates confusion about which session's context is active.

Environment:

VS Code version: (fill in)
OS: macOS
Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat

title: Test: Browser Suggestions Bar / Favorites
body: Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/318994, https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/318150 - [x] anyOS @osortega - [x] anyOS @Giuspepe - [x] anyOS @zhichli Complexity: 4 [Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319071%0A%0A&assignees=kycutler) --- The integrated browser now has a rich suggestions interface in the URL bar: > [!NOTE] > Under the hood this opens a quick input in the same location as the URL bar, so it's tricky to get the interaction behavior working smoothly. > > Please pay attention to the fluidity of the UI and file any bugs if things flicker or jump around unexpectedly. ## Setup Simply open the integrated browser. The picker should open automatically on a new page, or when the URL input is clicked. ## Scenarios This isn't an exact guide, or a complete list of behaviors -- exploratory testing is encouraged. When in doubt, compare the behavior against Chrome or Edge. ### URL input You should always just be able to type a URL and press enter. You should also be able to click and drag in the URL bar to select, copy/paste, etc. and it should work as expected. ### Favorites When a page is opened, it can be favorited by opening the bar and clicking the star icon. The icon should then remain visible in the non-editing state. Favorited pages should appear in the list and be filtered as you type. Selecting one should navigate there. ### Open Tabs In a "new" tab that is not navigated to any page, the picker should also list other open tabs. Selecting any of these tabs should close the current tab and switch to the one that was chosen. These should also be filtered as you type. ### Keyboard use Pressing `Esc` when the picker is open should close it and just switch to a standard input (same as Edge etc). From this state: - Typing should re-open the picker - Pressing `Enter` should navigate as normal - Pressing `Tab` should continue shifting focus along the toolbar - Pressing `Esc` should focus the page (if there is a page loaded). As this happens, the cursor position / selection should be consistent. Pressing `Ctrl+L` from the page should focus the URL bar again and open the picker. ### Interaction with URL decorations For pages with untrusted certificates, we render a warning icon and red strikethrough on the `https://` portion of the URL. These will disappear when the picker is opened, but should continue to work well otherwise.

title: MCP: add displayName to mcp.json (or fall back to JSON key when serverInfo.name collides)
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

## Summary

Add an optional `displayName` (or equivalent) field to each entry in `mcp.json` so the label shown in the MCP UI (Configure Tools strip, server picker, etc.) can be controlled from configuration. Alternatively, fall back to the JSON key when the server-reported `serverInfo.name` collides across multiple configured servers.

## Problem

Today the label VS Code shows for each MCP server in the "Configure Tools" strip and related UI is the value the server returns in its `serverInfo.name` during the MCP `initialize` handshake. The **JSON key** in `mcp.json` (e.g., `kusto_arn`, `kusto_icm`) is treated as internal-only and is not used as a display label.

This breaks down whenever a single MCP binary is configured multiple times with different `env`/`args` ŌĆö a very common pattern for multi-tenant/multi-cluster servers. Two real-world examples:

1. **`azure-kusto-mcp`** (PyPI) hardcodes `serverInfo.name = "kusto-mcp-server"`. A user configuring it once per Kusto cluster (ICM, ARN, RPaaS, ARG, DSMS, EV2 logs, ŌĆ”) ends up with 6+ entries all labeled `kusto-mcp-server` in the UI, with no way to tell them apart from the icon strip.
2. The same applies to **any "agency mcp <X>" style server** where multiple JSON entries shell out to the same underlying binary with different sub-commands.

Screenshot of the symptom (6 identically-labeled `kusto-mcp-server` rows from one user's config):

```
Ōśæ IcM.MCP.Server
ŌśÉ kusto-mcp-server
ŌśÉ kusto-mcp-server
ŌśÉ kusto-mcp-server
ŌśÉ kusto-mcp-server
Ōśæ kusto-mcp-server
Ōśæ kusto-mcp-server
```

The JSON keys (`kusto_icm`, `kusto_arn`, `kusto_rpaas`, `kusto_arg`, `kusto_dsms`, `ev2_logs`) are unique by construction ŌĆö the configuration file already contains a perfectly good unique identifier per server, but it's invisible in the UI.

`MCP: List Servers` does show the JSON key, but the Configure Tools strip (the surface the user spends the most time in) does not, and there is no way to disambiguate from there.

## Proposed solutions (any one would resolve this)

1. **Honor an explicit `displayName` on each `mcp.json` entry.**

   ```jsonc
   {
     "servers": {
       "kusto_rpaas": {
         "type": "stdio",
         "displayName": "Kusto ┬Ę RPaaS Prod",
         "command": "uvx",
         "args": ["azure-kusto-mcp"],
         "env": {
           "KUSTO_SERVICE_URI": "https://rpsaas.kusto.windows.net",
           "KUSTO_DATABASE": "RPaaSProd"
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

2. **Auto-disambiguate by falling back to the JSON key** whenever two configured servers report the same `serverInfo.name`. For example show `kusto-mcp-server (kusto_rpaas)` only on the colliding entries, leaving unique names untouched.

3. **Always show the JSON key as a secondary label / tooltip** in the Configure Tools strip and other compact surfaces. Even just exposing the key as the hover tooltip (it currently is not, on my build) would be a meaningful improvement.

(1) is the most flexible; (2) is the lowest-friction default-on fix; (3) is the cheapest cosmetic improvement.

## Why this matters

- Multi-instance MCP server configurations are increasingly common as more services ship MCP frontends (Kusto, Azure DevOps orgs, multiple Geneva environments, Foundry/AI Studio multi-account, etc.).
- The current behavior pushes users toward writing local stdio proxy wrappers just to rewrite `serverInfo.name`, which is friction for something the client could trivially control.
- The MCP spec does not forbid a client from using its own display label on top of the server-reported name; this would be a pure VS Code UX improvement with no protocol change required.

## Workarounds today (all unsatisfying)

- Run `MCP: List Servers` instead of using the Configure Tools UI ŌĆö works but slow.
- Write a tiny Python/Node stdio proxy that intercepts the `initialize` response and rewrites `serverInfo.name` ŌĆö works but requires per-user infrastructure.
- Fork the upstream MCP server to accept a `MCP_SERVER_NAME` env var ŌĆö not viable for archived/third-party servers like `azure-kusto-mcp`.

## Environment

- VS Code Insiders 1.122.0-insider (4459d58b5428c36de656f9241ffd31e2a2118150, x64)
- Windows 11
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension (current)

## Related

- #316362 ŌĆö "Plugin-sourced MCP servers: no UI to view or control dedup state" (related dedup/UI surface concerns, but different scope)

title: [Copilot Sessions] Claude Code CLI sessions incorrectly marked as "Failed" after successful completion
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No
(Requires GitHub Copilot and Claude Code CLI extensions)

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 10 Pro 10.0.19045
- GitHub Copilot Extension Version: 0.50.1
- Claude Code CLI Version: 2.1.112

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a workspace in VS Code with GitHub Copilot and Claude Code CLI installed.
2. Start a new Claude Code session via the Copilot Sessions panel ("New" button).
3. Have a normal conversation with Claude Code ŌĆö ask it to read files, answer questions, or write code.
4. Wait for Claude Code to complete its response (the CLI process exits normally).
5. Observe the session status in the Copilot Sessions panel.

## Expected Behavior

Completed sessions should be marked as **"Completed"** (or similar) when the Claude Code CLI process exits with a successful status (`stop_reason: end_turn`).

## Actual Behavior

All sessions are marked as **"Failed"** in the Copilot Sessions panel, regardless of whether the underlying Claude Code CLI process completed successfully. The chat UI also intermittently displays an **"Error during execution"** block when individual tool calls inside Claude Code fail and recover ŌĆö this is normal recoverable behavior in Claude Code, but it is surfaced to the user as a top-level failure.

## Root Cause (Investigated)

Inspecting the Claude Code session logs (`~/.claude/projects/<workspace>/<sessionId>.jsonl`), every session ends with:

```json
{ "stop_reason": "end_turn" }

title: Custom Endpoint Models (Messages API) Never Send `thinking` Parameter
body:
# Custom Endpoint Models (Messages API) Never Send `thinking` Parameter

## Environment

- **VS Code**: 1.122.0-insider (build `4459d58b54`)
- **Copilot Extension**: v0.50.2026052902 (GitHub Copilot Chat)
- **OS**: Windows 11 (23H2)
- **Extensions**: Issue reproduces with all non-Copilot extensions disabled
- **Custom Endpoint**: Anthropic Messages API via proxy (`/v1/messages`)
- **Dev Tools Console**: No errors related to this issue (requests succeed with 200 OK, but response lacks thinking content)

## Bug Summary

When using a custom endpoint model configured with `apiType: "messages"`, the `thinking` parameter is never included in the request body sent to the upstream API - even when the model is configured with `"thinking": true` and `"adaptiveThinking": true` in `chatLanguageModels.json`. The model responds as if extended thinking is not enabled because the parameter is absent from the wire request.

In contrast, the built-in GitHub-hosted Claude models (e.g. `claude-opus-4.6` via `panel/editAgent`) correctly send `thinking: {type: "adaptive", display: "summarized"}` and receive thinking blocks in responses.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Configure a custom endpoint model in VS Code settings with Messages API:

```json
{
  "github.copilot.chat.models": [
    {
      "name": "my-anthropic-proxy",
      "vendor": "customendpoint",
      "apiKey": "...",
      "apiType": "messages",
      "models": [
        {
          "id": "claude-opus-4-6",
          "name": "Claude Opus 4.6",
          "url": "https://my-proxy.example.com/v1/messages",
          "toolCalling": true,
          "vision": true,
          "thinking": true,
          "adaptiveThinking": true,
          "maxInputTokens": 200000,
          "maxOutputTokens": 128000,
          "supportsReasoningEffort": ["low", "medium", "high"]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

2. Start a chat using the custom endpoint model.
3. Inspect the HTTP request body sent to the proxy (e.g. via proxy logs).

**Expected**: Request body includes `"thinking": {"type": "adaptive"}` (or `"adaptive"` with `display: "summarized"`).

**Actual**: Request body has no `thinking` field. The model responds without extended thinking.

## Root Cause Analysis

> Note: The following analysis is based on observing the request body at the proxy and inspecting the bundled `extension.js` to understand the code paths involved. Variable names are from the minified bundle.

The issue is caused by two gaps in the code path for custom endpoint models:

### Issue 1: `copilotLanguageModelWrapper` does not pass `enableThinking`

When a custom endpoint model is invoked via the VS Code Language Model API (extensions calling `lm.sendRequest()`), the request flows through `copilotLanguageModelWrapper`. This wrapper constructs a `modelCapabilities` object that is passed to the internal request pipeline:

```javascript
// In copilotLanguageModelWrapper (approx. offset 13807981 in extension.js)
modelCapabilities: {
  reasoningEffort: typeof o.modelConfiguration?.reasoningEffort == "string"
    ? o.modelConfiguration.reasoningEffort
    : void 0
}
```

Notice that `enableThinking` is **not** included in `modelCapabilities`. This is the first gate that prevents thinking.

### Issue 2: `LSn` (Messages API body builder) requires `enableThinking` to set `thinking`

The function that constructs the Anthropic Messages API request body (minified as `LSn`, approx. offset 13555239) contains:

```javascript
if (e.modelCapabilities?.enableThinking) {
  if (r.supportsAdaptiveThinking)
    m = { type: "adaptive", display: "summarized" };
  else if (r.maxThinkingBudget && r.minThinkingBudget) {
    let O = e.postOptions.max_tokens ?? 1024;
    let Q = r.minThinkingBudget ?? 1024;
    let F = 16e3 < Q ? Q : 16e3;
    let B = r.maxThinkingBudget ?? 32e3;
    let M = Math.min(B, O - 1, F);
    M && (m = { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: M });
  }
  // No else clause ŌĆö m stays undefined if neither condition matches
}
```

Since `enableThinking` is never set (Issue 1), this entire block is skipped and `m` (the thinking config) remains `undefined`. Even if `enableThinking` were passed, the function has no fallback: if `supportsAdaptiveThinking` is falsy and `maxThinkingBudget`/`minThinkingBudget` are not set, `m` still stays `undefined`.

### Contrast with built-in agent path

The built-in agent (`panel/editAgent`) uses a separate code path that constructs the request body directly:

```javascript
// panel/editAgent path (approx. offset 13825471)
thinking: Q ? { type: "adaptive" } : P ? { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: P } : void 0
```

Here `Q` is derived from `_knownModels` (populated for GitHub-hosted models). For custom endpoints, `_knownModels` is `void 0`, so this also falls through to `void 0`.

### Data Flow Summary

```
Custom Endpoint Model Request Flow:
ŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöü
copilotLanguageModelWrapper
  ŌåÆ modelCapabilities: { reasoningEffort: "high" }  ŌåÉ missing enableThinking!
  ŌåÆ makeChatRequest2()
    ŌåÆ chatMLFetcher.fetchOne()
      ŌåÆ endpoint.createRequestBody(e)
        ŌåÆ LSn(n, e, model, endpoint)
          ŌåÆ if (e.modelCapabilities?.enableThinking)  ŌåÉ FALSE, skipped
          ŌåÆ thinking: m  ŌåÉ m is undefined
          ŌåÆ Request body: { model, messages, ..., thinking: undefined }
```

```
Built-in GitHub Model Request Flow:
ŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöüŌöü
panel/editAgent
  ŌåÆ Reads _knownModels for model capabilities
  ŌåÆ Q = supportsAdaptiveThinking from _knownModels
  ŌåÆ thinking: Q ? {type:"adaptive"} : P ? {type:"enabled",...} : void 0
  ŌåÆ Request body: { ..., thinking: {type:"adaptive", display:"summarized"} }
```

## Proposed Fix

### Fix 1: Pass `enableThinking` from model configuration

In `copilotLanguageModelWrapper`, include the model's thinking capability:

```javascript
modelCapabilities: {
  reasoningEffort: typeof o.modelConfiguration?.reasoningEffort == "string"
    ? o.modelConfiguration.reasoningEffort
    : void 0,
  enableThinking: !!endpoint.supportsThinking || !!endpoint.supportsAdaptiveThinking
}
```

### Fix 2: Add fallback in `LSn` for models that declare `thinking: true` but lack budget metadata

```javascript
if (e.modelCapabilities?.enableThinking) {
  if (r.supportsAdaptiveThinking)
    m = { type: "adaptive", display: "summarized" };
  else if (r.maxThinkingBudget && r.minThinkingBudget) {
    // ... existing budget logic ...
  } else {
    // Fallback: if model declares thinking support but has no
    // adaptive/budget metadata, default to adaptive
    m = { type: "adaptive" };
  }
}
```

### Fix 3 (panel/editAgent path): Respect user config `thinking: true`

The ternary that gates `thinking` on `_knownModels` should also check the endpoint's declared capabilities:

```javascript
thinking: Q ? { type: "adaptive" }
  : P ? { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: P }
  : endpoint.supportsAdaptiveThinking ? { type: "adaptive" }
  : void 0
```

## Impact

This affects all users of custom endpoint models (BYOK/enterprise proxy setups) who configure Anthropic-compatible endpoints with extended thinking. The model config correctly declares `thinking: true` and `adaptiveThinking: true`, but these values are never used in the request body construction for the `copilotLanguageModelWrapper` code path.

## Workaround

Manually patching the minified `extension.js` to (1) add `enableThinking: true` to the wrapper's `modelCapabilities` and (2) add an `else m = {type: "adaptive"}` fallback in the Messages API body builder restores thinking for custom endpoints. This must be reapplied after every VS Code update.

## Related

- Custom endpoint model configuration: `apiType: "messages"` with `thinking: true`
- The `supportsAdaptiveThinking` property IS correctly resolved from user config ŌåÆ `vD()` ŌåÆ `capabilities.supports.adaptive_thinking` ŌåÆ endpoint property. The gap is only that it's never consulted by the wrapper/request builder.

---

## Checklist

- [x] Searched existing issues ŌĆö no matching issue found for custom endpoint + thinking
- [x] Reproduced with all non-Copilot extensions disabled
- [x] Minimal config provided to reproduce
- [x] Filed against `microsoft/vscode` (Copilot extension is bundled in VS Code; `vscode-copilot-release` is archived)

title: Test: Copilot Managed Settings
body:
Refs: #318623

- [x] anyOS @vritant24
- [x] anyOS @dmitrivMS 

Complexity: 4

Authors: @joshspicer


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319025%0A%0A&assignees=joshspicer)

---

### What it does

VS Code now consumes the GitHub Copilot `/copilot_internal/managed_settings` endpoint to apply enterprise policy to Copilot-chat plugin behavior. Settings delivered via this channel flow through the existing `AccountPolicyService` and respect the **Account Policy Gate** (the `ChatApprovedAccountOrganizations` allowlist).

---

### Prerequisites

> [!IMPORTANT]
> You need an **alternate GitHub account that is a member of the `VSCodeTesting` org** and seated for Copilot Business.
>
> **Ping @joshspicer** if you need help

Sign out of your primary GitHub account in VS Code Insiders and sign in with the alt account.

The active policy lives in [`VSCodeTesting/.github-private/.github/copilot/settings.json`](https://github.com/VSCodeTesting/.github-private/blob/main/.github/copilot/settings. **read it to know what's currently configured**, but don't edit it. You shouldn't need to edit it to get basic testing done. 


### Scenarios

#### 1. `chat.plugins.enabledPlugins` (`ChatEnabledPlugins`)

Allowlist of plugins keyed by `<plugin-id>@<marketplace-name>`. When set, only entries mapped to `true` are enabled.

- [ ] Install the plugins listed in `enabledPlugins` via the Copilot CLI.
- [ ] In **Agent Plugins - Installed**, the `true`-mapped plugins are enabled; `false`-mapped (or unlisted) plugins are **disabled** (not hidden).
 Window** logs ` not enabled by ChatEnabledPlugins policy` for each disabled one.blocked 

#### 2. `chat.plugins.extraMarketplaces` (`ChatExtraMarketplaces`)

Additional named plugin marketplaces, unioned with the user's `chat.plugins.marketplaces`.

- [ ] In Settings Editor (`chat.plugins`), `Extra Marketplaces` shows the **"Managed by organization"** badge and renders as **inline read-only key/value rows** matching the policy.
- [ ] **Chat: Manage Plugin Marketplaces** lists every policy entry tagged `(managed by enterprise policy)`.
- [ ] Installing a plugin from a policy-supplied marketplace works normally (with strict mode off).

#### 3. `chat.plugins.strictMarketplaces` (`ChatStrictMarketplaces`)

When `true`, only marketplaces from `extraMarketplaces` are trusted.

- [ ] Install plugins from **both** a marketplace listed in `extraMarketplaces` and one that isn't.
- [ ] Only the trusted-marketplace plugin loads; the other appears **disabled**.
 Window** logs ` marketplace not trusted under strict mode`.blocked 
- [ ] Plugins from a marketplace you added in user settings (`chat.plugins.marketplaces`) are also ** strict mode is enterprise-only trust.disabled** 

---

### How to debug

**Developer: Export Policy Data to ** is the primary diagnostic. It shows the raw managed_settings payload, the gate state, last fetch status, and the applied policy values.File

 Window** (debug-level logs from `PluginMarketplaceService` / `AgentPluginService`), and the **Agent Plugins - Installed** view which shows policy-disabled plugins as disabled rather than hidden.

title: MSSQL Schema Compare: individual row checkboxes unresponsive in Insiders 1.122.0, works in Stable 1.117.0
body:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 
- OS Version: 
## Bug report

**Extension:** ms-mssql.mssql (SQL Server (mssql)) ŌĆö v1.42.2
**VS Code Insiders:** 1.122.0 (commit 6bb5e61adc5714fd0313615ea510be49b5fdd5f1)
**VS Code Stable:** 1.117.0 (works correctly)
**OS:** Windows 10 x64 (build 26300)
**Electron:** 42.2.0 | **Chromium:** 148.0.7778.97 | **Node.js:** 24.15.0

---

### What happened

In VS Code Insiders 1.122.0, individual row checkboxes in the MSSQL Schema Compare results panel are completely unresponsive. Clicking a checkbox to include or exclude a specific object from the comparison does nothing ŌĆö the checkbox state does not change and the selection is not registered.

This is a regression. The same extension version (ms-mssql v1.42.2) works correctly in VS Code Stable 1.117.0 ŌĆö individual row checkboxes respond immediately and correctly.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open VS Code Insiders 1.122.0 with ms-mssql v1.42.2 installed
2. Open the Schema Compare panel (Command Palette ŌåÆ "Schema Compare")
3. Set source and target database connections and run a comparison
4. In the results list, attempt to uncheck an individual row checkbox to exclude that object
5. Observe: checkbox does not respond to clicks

### Expected behavior

Individual row checkboxes should toggle include/exclude state for each object in the comparison results, as they do in VS Code Stable 1.117.0 with the same extension.

### Workaround

Use VS Code Stable 1.117.0 ŌĆö identical extension version, checkboxes work correctly there.

### Notes

VS Code 1.122 includes a significant change to webview streaming (transferable streams via service worker, bypassing postMessage layers). The MSSQL Schema Compare panel is a React webview that communicates with the extension host via postMessage/RPC. It is possible this webview pipeline change is causing checkbox state change events to be dropped before reaching the extension host.
