title: Remote agent sessions started via UI running in the background (detach VSCode)
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

| :sparkles: Feature Request|
|:---------------------------|
| When connected to a remote machine via SSH, I'd love to have the possibility to start an agent session inside VSCode via the UI, but then let it still run when I close my VSCode window on my local machine. |

The [Remote agent sessions](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/concepts/agents#_remote-agent-sessions) describe two possibilities so far:
- *Agent window with remote connection*. Unfortunately, this doesn't work for me as the server I'm working on is behind a VPN, so the agent window won't be able to connect to it. Furthermore, even it that worked, it would be yet another window and not integrated into the existing agent UI (on the sidebar).
- *Copilot CLI remote control*. I can launch copilot in a screen, tmux or zellij session, then detach from it. Additionally, one can use `/remote on` to get the output mirrored to GitHub. This is already amazing! However, so far, I'm not the biggest fan of using agents in the command line as I only see disadvantages with respect to the native UI we have in VSCode. See also the [limitations listed in the docs](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/agents/copilot-cli#_limitations-of-copilot-cli-sessions). For example, the Copilot CLI sessions cannot access all VSCode built-in tools. I can also not as easily attach context to it compared to the VSCode UI, e.g. attach files, my debug context, the current internal browser page, etc.

Given that VSCode already spawns its own server on the remote machine that the local instance connects to, I was wondering if that could be leveraged for the agent sessions such that they are spawned on the remote machine. Thus, they could continue to work in the background even when I'm closing the VSCode window on the local machine.

title: Git "merge/save with conflicts" modal dialogs get blocked, UI becomes unresponsive
body:
Type: Bug When a modal dialog appears, it prevents usage of VScode even when the modal is not visible. Code doesn't seem to care if the dialog is visible. The dialog may even appear on a different desktop. When I use the Git Merge / Conflict resolution and I try to close the window with conflicts still unresolved, Code displays a modal dialog asking if I'm sure I want to quit/save. But if there's another modal dialog already open, the merge dialog doesn't get displayed. Code becomes unresponsive to most GUI inputs for the merge editor. In some cases, I have a separate Code instance on a different desktop. It has displayed a modal telling me it cannot connect to my Remote SSH Host and asking if I want to retry/cancel or something. I haven't looked at that instance in hours or days. Now when I try to close my Merge editor with conflicts, it freezes. After some experimentation, I eventually figured out there was a modal open on another desktop for another instance. When I dismissed that modal, my Merge modal dialog finally appeared, even though it was for the other instance on the other desktop. title: VSCode GitHub Copilot Chat Local Agent Mode crashes when MCP server uses JSON Schema draft-2020-12 features
body:
VSCode GitHub Copilot Chat Local Agent Mode crashes when MCP server uses JSON Schema draft-2020-12 features

Summary

When using GitHub Copilot Chat in Local Agent Mode with an MCP server that returns tools using JSON Schema draft-2020-12, VSCode's internal AJV validator fails to compile the schema. Depending on whether the response uses dynamic/ref features, this results in either a crash or silent degradation.

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Configure an MCP server that uses JSON Schema draft-2020-12 (e.g., Port.io MCP)
  2. Open Copilot Chat in Local Agent Mode
  3. Invoke any tool from that MCP server whose schema uses $dynamicRef, $dynamicAnchor, or unevaluatedProperties
  4. Observe the crash or warning in the GitHub Copilot output log

Expected Behavior

MCP tool schemas using JSON Schema draft-2020-12 are validated correctly, or at minimum degrade gracefully without crashing the IDE.

Actual Behavior

Non-crashing path ŌĆö warning logged, tool silently omitted:

[warning] Error compiling input schema for tool mcp_port-mcp-serv_list_entities:
Error: no schema with key or ref "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema"

Crashing path ŌĆö triggered when response uses dynamic/ref features:

[error] TypeError: wrapper0.validate is not a function
(at tsx element g2 > A5 > ije > fxt > al > <anonymous> > <anonymous> > zkt)
    at validate13 (eval at zIt (...extension.js:192:27372), <anonymous>:3:3017)
    at tDn (...extension.js:1688:9735)
    at Dye.validateToolInput (...extension.js:1688:11325)
    ...

Environment

┬Ā | ┬Ā -- | -- Affected clients | VSCode, VSCode Insiders (likely Cursor too) Mode | Copilot Chat ŌĆö Local Agent Mode only LLM model | Irrelevant ŌĆö issue is in the schema validation layer, not inference OS | Windows # VSCode GitHub Copilot Chat Local Agent Mode crashes when MCP server uses JSON Schema draft-2020-12 features ## Summary When using GitHub Copilot Chat in **Local Agent Mode** with an MCP server that returns tools using JSON Schema draft-2020-12, VSCode's internal AJV validator fails to compile the schema. Depending on whether the response uses dynamic/ref features, this results in either a crash or silent degradation. --- ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Configure an MCP server that uses JSON Schema draft-2020-12 (e.g., [[Port.io MCP](https://docs.port.io/)](https://docs.port.io)) 2. Open Copilot Chat in **Local Agent Mode** 3. Invoke any tool from that MCP server whose schema uses `$dynamicRef`, `$dynamicAnchor`, or `unevaluatedProperties` 4. Observe the crash or warning in the **GitHub Copilot** output log --- ## Expected Behavior MCP tool schemas using JSON Schema draft-2020-12 are validated correctly, or at minimum degrade gracefully **without crashing the IDE**. --- ## Actual Behavior **Non-crashing path** ŌĆö warning logged, tool silently omitted: ``` [warning] Error compiling input schema for tool mcp_port-mcp-serv_list_entities: Error: no schema with key or ref "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema" ``` **Crashing path** ŌĆö triggered when response uses dynamic/ref features: ``` [error] TypeError: wrapper0.validate is not a function (at tsx element g2 > A5 > ije > fxt > al > > > zkt) at validate13 (eval at zIt (...extension.js:192:27372), :3:3017) at tDn (...extension.js:1688:9735) at Dye.validateToolInput (...extension.js:1688:11325) ... ``` --- ## Environment | | | |---|---| | **Affected clients** | VSCode, VSCode Insiders (likely Cursor too) | | **Mode** | Copilot Chat ŌĆö Local Agent Mode only | | **LLM model** | Irrelevant ŌĆö issue is in the schema validation layer, not inference | | **OS** | Windows | --- ## Root Cause Analysis VSCode uses [[AJV](https://ajv.js.org/)](https://ajv.js.org/) for JSON validation. [[AJV does not support draft-2020-12](https://ajv.js.org/json-schema.html#draft-2020-12)](https://ajv.js.org/json-schema.html#draft-2020-12) out of the box. A partial fix was previously merged that allows 2020-12 schemas to pass through schema compilation without immediately failing, but it does **not** register the draft-2020-12 meta-schema with AJV. This means: - Schema uses only basic features ŌåÆ warning logged, execution continues, tool silently omitted - Schema uses 2020-12-specific features (`$dynamicRef`, `unevaluatedProperties`, etc.) ŌåÆ `wrapper0.validate` is `undefined` ŌåÆ runtime crash in `validateToolInput` The current partial fix creates a **false sense of compatibility** while masking a crash path. --- ## Impact This is effectively a regression-level issue for MCP adoption: - **MCP is standardizing on draft-2020-12.** SEP-1613 (`modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol#1613`) ŌĆö *"Establish JSON Schema 2020-12 as Default Dialect for MCP"* ŌĆö has been **accepted**. Clients are now required to support at least JSON Schema 2020-12. - **Major SDKs have already moved.** FastMCP, Zod v4 (via `model_json_schema()`), Port.io, and others now emit 2020-12 schemas by default. - **Vendors are being forced to roll back** or implement VSCode-specific workarounds just to remain usable in Copilot Chat. - This issue has been open in various forms **since 2022** (see [[#155379](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155379)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155379)), but the MCP integration has significantly amplified its severity ŌĆö tools are now simply **unusable**. --- ## Workarounds - Use **Copilot CLI** or **Claude integration** instead of Local Agent Mode - MCP server authors can downgrade schema output to draft-07 ŌĆö not always feasible depending on SDK --- ## Suggested Fix Register the draft-2020-12 meta-schema with AJV using a compatible instance (e.g., `ajv/dist/2020`), or strip/downcast the `$schema` declaration before validation so unrecognized drafts don't leave `validate` as `undefined`. The validator should never reach `validateToolInput` with an unresolved `wrapper.validate`. --- ## Related Issues | Issue | Summary | |---|---| | [[microsoft/vscode#251315](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/251315)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/251315) | **Support JSON schema draft 2020-12** ŌĆö Direct predecessor. Specifically calls out MCP tools being marked unusable due to the same validator. VSCode team acknowledged it's a "major effort" but the partial fix shipped is insufficient and exposes a crash path. | | [[microsoft/vscode#155379](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155379)](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/155379) | **meta-schema features warning since v1.68.0** ŌĆö Open since 2022. Tracks the broader draft-2020-12 support gap in `vscode-json-languageservice`. No fix shipped in 3+ years. The MCP crash is a direct consequence of this unresolved work. | | [[modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol#1613](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1613)](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/issues/1613) | **SEP-1613: Establish JSON Schema 2020-12 as Default Dialect for MCP** ŌĆö Accepted standard. MCP clients are now **required** to support draft-2020-12. This makes VSCode's lack of support a spec-level compliance failure, not just a feature gap. |

title: Agent plugin marketplace mangles SSH `source.url` when user is not `git` (breaks GHE / self-hosted)
body:
**Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?:** Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.122.0 (Stable)
- OS Version: macOS 15 (Darwin arm64)
- Feature: Agent Plugins (Preview), `chat.plugins.marketplaces`

### Summary

If a marketplace plugin's `source.url` is an SSH URL with a user other than `git`, VS Code rewrites it to `https:/HOST/PATH` (single slash) and looks for it as a subdirectory of the marketplace clone. The plugin never installs.

The Copilot CLI clones the same `marketplace.json` fine using `ssh://` or `git+ssh://`.

### Repro

GHE instance whose SSH user is not `git`:

```
$ ssh git@acme.ghe.com
Permission denied (publickey).
$ ssh acme@acme.ghe.com
Hi user! You've successfully authenticated...
```

`.github/plugin/marketplace.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "agent-plugins",
  "owner": { "name": "Example Org" },
  "plugins": [
    {
      "name": "example-plugin",
      "source": {
        "source": "url",
        "url": "acme@acme.ghe.com:team/example-plugin.git"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

`settings.json`:

```json
"chat.plugins.marketplaces": [
  "acme@acme.ghe.com:org/agent-plugins.git"
]
```

Marketplace clones, plugin lists, then **Install** fails with:

```
Plugin source directory 'https:/acme.ghe.com/team/example-plugin.git'
not found in repository 'acme@acme.ghe.com:org/agent-plugins.git'.
```

### Expected

VS Code clones the SSH URL as written, same as the CLI.

### What works in CLI vs VS Code

| `source.url` form | CLI | VS Code |
|---|---|---|
| `acme@host:team/plugin.git` (SCP) | ŌØī "Invalid url" | ŌØī mangled |
| `ssh://acme@host/team/plugin.git` | Ō£ģ | ŌØī mangled |
| `git+ssh://acme@host/team/plugin.git` | Ō£ģ | ŌØī mangled |
| `https://host/team/plugin.git` | Ō£ģ | Ō£ģ (requires HTTPS creds) |
| `git@host:team/plugin.git` | n/a | n/a (auth fails: `git` user invalid) |

Clearing `~/Library/Application Support/Code/agentPlugins/` and toggling the URL format on re-add doesn't change the result.

### Why this hits GHE specifically

The published [marketplace schema](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces#plugin-sources) only lists `https://` and `git@host:path`. VS Code seems to enforce that with `^(https?://|git@)`, and when the URL doesn't match, it falls back to treating the string as a relative path inside the marketplace clone (note the wording "source directory ... not found in repository ...", which matches the RelativePath path; cf. #311725, #308504).

On a GHE where the SSH user is not `git`:

- `git@host:path` authenticates as the wrong user.
- Every other valid SSH URL (`user@host:path`, `ssh://`, `git+ssh://`) is mangled before git ever sees it.

The single slash in `https:/HOST/PATH` looks like a `path.normalize()` somewhere collapsing `//`.

### Fix

1. Accept the following as `url` sources and pass them to `git clone` verbatim:
   - `<user>@<host>:<path>` (SCP, any user)
   - `ssh://<user>@<host>/<path>`
   - `git+ssh://<user>@<host>/<path>`
2. Don't silently fall back to RelativePath when the URL doesn't match. Either reject with an explicit "unsupported URL scheme" or accept it.
3. Reword the error so a misclassified URL doesn't read as a missing subdirectory.

### Related

- #311725 ŌĆö chat: fix installed url-source plugins after restart (RelativePath vs URL classification)
- #308504 ŌĆö URL-source plugins silently uninstall on restart
- #313029 ŌĆö `.git` suffix requirement; non-spec URLs silently mishandled
- #301111 ŌĆö Azure DevOps URL rejection (same family)

---

title: Test: Multiple Visible Sessions
body:
Refs: <!-- Refer to the issue that this test plan item is testing. -->

- [x] anyOS @aiday-mar
- [x] anyOS @dmitrivMS

Complexity: 5


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23318979%0A%0A&assignees=benibenj)

---


The Sessions Part in the Agents window can now host **multiple session views side-by-side**. Exactly one is the **active** session and drives the Changes view (auxiliary bar) and the Terminal (panel).

# Active session

* With multiple session views open, only one should look active.
* The **Changes view** in the secondary sidebar and the **Terminal** should always reflect the active session.
* Click into a different visible session view. The active styling should move, and the Changes view + Terminal should switch to that session.

# New session view

* Only **one** empty new-session view can exist at a time.
* With no new-session view visible, run any `New Session` ŌåÆ a new-session view opens.
* With a new-session view already visible, run `New Session` again ŌåÆ it should just **focus** the existing one.

# Opening sessions from the sessions list

* Click a session that is **already visible** in the grid ŌåÆ it becomes active. The grid does not change.
* Click a session that is **not visible** ŌåÆ it replaces the current active session in its slot.

# Pin a session (toolbar, top-right of each session view)

* Pin action is **not available** on the new-session view. It is available on every created session.
* Pin session A. Open session B from the sidebar (normal click) ŌåÆ B should **not** replace A. It opens in a new slot.
* Unpin A ŌåÆ next click on a new session recycles A's slot.

# Maximize a session

* Maximize action only appears when 2+ session views are visible.
* Click `Maximize` ŌåÆ only that session is visible across the grid; the icon toggles to `Restore`.
* Click `Restore` ŌåÆ the previous side-by-side layout returns.

# Close a session view (without deleting the session)

* Close the **active** slot ŌåÆ a neighbouring slot becomes active; the session itself remains in the sidebar.
* Close a **non-active** slot ŌåÆ the active slot stays active.
* Close a **pinned** slot ŌåÆ the slot is removed.
* If the last slot is closed, the new-session view opens automatically.
* Reopen a closed session from the sidebar ŌåÆ it comes back into the grid (replacing the active non-pinned slot, unless every visible slot is pinned, in which case it opens to the side ŌĆö see below).

# Opening multiple sessions (side-by-side)

Verify each of these opens the session **in a new slot to the side** instead of replacing the active session:

* **Drag & drop from the sessions list** onto the grid.
  * Drop overlay highlights the **left** or **right** half of the target slot depending on cursor position.
  * The session is inserted on the chosen side.
* **Drag a session tab from the chat bar** at the top of one session view onto another ŌåÆ reorders / inserts on the chosen side. Dropping onto itself is a no-op.
* **Alt+click** a session in the sessions list ŌåÆ opens to the side.
* **Right-click in the sessions list ŌåÆ `Open to the Side`** ŌåÆ opens to the side. Works on multi-select too (all selected sessions append in order).
* **Pin every visible session**, then open any other session from the sidebar ŌåÆ because there's no non-pinned slot to recycle, the session opens to the side.

# Not supported yet

* Stacking session views **above each other** is not supported. Only side-by-side. Drag & drop should never produce a top/bottom split.
* Viewing **multiple chats of the same session** at once is not supported. Opening a chat that belongs to a session already visible should activate that session, not open a second view of it.

title: Chinese IME Compatibility Issues in Model Picker Search

### Description #### Issue The model search box in the Model Picker has compatibility failures when working with Chinese Input Method Editors (IME), including abnormal responses to double-click operations and other unexpected misbehaviors. #### Rationale for Supporting Chinese IME in Model Picker Using Chinese IME in the Model Picker is a reasonable and high-frequency usage scenario for Chinese users: When typing pinyin via Chinese IME, users can press the Enter key directly to input the corresponding Latin characters without selecting Chinese candidate words. This avoids frequent switching between Chinese and English input methods when users write Chinese prompts which would otherwise interrupt their workflow and train of thought. #### Environment - Input Method: [Built-in Microsoft Chinese IME for Windows](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/globalization/input/simplified-chinese-ime) #### Additional Information The attached video shows the clear performance difference between input operations under active Chinese IME state and pure English keyboard state. what I want to type was `DeepSeek`. ### Issue Details Item|Value ---|--- Issue Category|Bug Target|Visual Studio Code VS Code Version|Code 1.122.1 (8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e, 2026-05-29T09:25:37+02:00) OS Version|Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200

Steps to Reproduce:

https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/318952 could be related, so this may already be fixed.

title: Custom BYOK model hidden from picker or fails with 502 when model id matches built-in Copilot model
body:
## Summary

Custom BYOK `customendpoint` model configured in `chatLanguageModels.json` cannot be used reliably in Copilot Chat. The model either does not appear in the model picker, or requests fail with `502 Server error`.

## Environment

Version: 1.122.0  
Commit: 6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724  
Date: 2026-05-27T14:03:01-07:00  
Electron: 39.8.8  
ElectronBuildId: 13870025  
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265  
Node.js: 22.22.1  
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0  
OS: Linux x64 6.17.0-29-generic snap  

GitHub Copilot Chat extension version observed in logs: `0.50.0`

## Configuration

`~/.config/Code/User/chatLanguageModels.json`:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Custom Endpoint",
    "vendor": "customendpoint",
    "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.-60b744f3}",
    "apiType": "responses",
    "models": [
      {
        "id": "gpt-5.5",
        "name": "gpt-5.5",
        "url": "https://sub2api....***/v1",
        "toolCalling": false,
        "vision": true,
        "maxInputTokens": 200000,
        "maxOutputTokens": 200000
      }
    ]
  }
]
```

## What happens

When using this model in Copilot Chat, requests fail with:

```text
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: bff44fcc-a972-4532-8ca0-fa817b922031

Reason: Server error: 502: Error: Server error: 502
    at dG._provideLanguageModelResponse (/snap/code/242/usr/share/code/resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:16552)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)
    at async dG.provideLanguageModelResponse (/snap/code/242/usr/share/code/resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:17555)
```

Earlier logs also showed:

```text
Server error: 502 {"error":{"message":"Upstream request failed","type":"upstream_error"}}event: response.failed
```

## Additional observations

The endpoint and API key appear to work outside VS Code:

- `GET /v1/models` returns `200` with the same API key.
- `POST /v1/responses` with model `gpt-5.5` returns `200`.
- Streaming responses also return `200`.
- A simple `tools` request also returns `200`.

However, in VS Code Copilot Chat:

1. With `toolCalling: true`, the model appears usable in Agent mode but requests fail with `502`.
2. With `toolCalling: false`, the model may disappear from the model picker, even in Ask mode.
3. The generated Copilot debug `models.json` contains `gpt-5.5`, but marks it as:

```json
"model_picker_enabled": false
```

This suggests the custom model may be colliding with an internal/official Copilot model named `gpt-5.5`, or the model picker is applying provider policy to the custom endpoint model.

## Expected behavior

A custom endpoint model configured in `chatLanguageModels.json` should:

1. Appear in the model picker when valid for the current mode.
2. Not be hidden because its model id matches or resembles an internal Copilot model id.
3. Use the configured custom endpoint and API key consistently.
4. Surface enough request/debug information to distinguish extension-side request construction issues from upstream endpoint failures.

## Actual behavior

The configured model either:

- is hidden from the model picker, or
- fails with `502 Server error` when selected/used.

## Request

Could you clarify whether custom endpoint model ids can collide with built-in Copilot model ids, and whether `model_picker_enabled: false` is expected for BYOK/customendpoint models? If this is expected behavior, is there a supported way to set a display id/name separately from the actual upstream model name sent to the custom endpoint?

title: BYOK Messages API incompatible with Vertex AI Anthropic endpoints
body:

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->

<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: WSL Ubuntu-22.04

 Problem: When using apiType: "messages" with a Vertex AI Anthropic endpoint (e.g., rawPredict/streamRawPredict), requests fail with 404 because:
 
 1. VS Code sends "model" in the request body ŌĆö Vertex AI rawPredict rejects this (model is in the URL path)
  2. VS Code does NOT send "anthropic_version" in the body ŌĆö required by Vertex AI ("anthropic_version": "vertex-2023-10-16")
 
 Expected: Either a vendor: "vertex-anthropic" provider type, or requestBody overrides (similar to requestHeaders) to allow injecting/removing body fields.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure a BYOK model with apiType: "messages" pointing to any Vertex AI rawPredict Claude endpoint. The request body contains "model": "..." which causes a 404.

 Workaround: A local proxy that removes model and injects anthropic_version into the body before forwarding. But this is very slow.

title: Linux desktop environment not recognised in vscode when trying to configure the keyring
body:
[<!--](

[output.txt]

I'm trying to use vs code on a raspberry pi 8.

It keeps dislaying the window alert "An OS keyring couldn't be identified for storing the encryption related data in your current desktop environment,

Open the troubleshooting to address this or you can use weaker encryption that doesn't use the OS keyring.

I selected the open troubleshooting guide (https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/configure/settings-sync#_troubleshooting-keychain-issues).

I ran the "code --verbose --vmodule="*/components/os_crypt/*=1" command and got the attached output.txt.

On line 5 of the Output.txt file is

[51274:0529/122113.330892:VERBOSE1:components/os_crypt/sync/key_storage_util_linux.cc:46] Password storage detected desktop environment: (unknown)

Which shows that the desktop environment is unknown.

title: VS Code does not terminate active MCP sessions on exit
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.119.0
- OS Version: 1.119.0

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Quit VS Code while MCP Servers is in running state.

### Description

When VS Code exits (either via normal close or force quit), it currently does not send termination signals to active MCP (Model Context Protocol) sessions. These sessions remain alive on the server side even after the client has disconnected, resulting in stale session data that is never cleaned up.

---

### Current Behavior

- User opens VS Code and initiates one or more MCP sessions.
- User closes VS Code.
- MCP sessions on the server remain active indefinitely, holding stale context and resources.

### Expected Behavior

- On VS Code exit (graceful or otherwise), all active MCP sessions should be explicitly terminated.
- The server should receive proper session-close/cleanup signals before the client disconnects.

---

### Impact

- Stale sessions accumulate on the server over time, consuming memory and resources.

---

title: Feature Request: Agent mode should support working across multiple workspace folders simultaneously
body:
## Problem

When using agent mode in a multi-root workspace (or a workspace with multiple folders), the agent is effectively limited to operating within a single folder context. It cannot reason about, navigate, or make changes across multiple repositories/folders in the same session.

This is a significant limitation for developers working on projects that span multiple repositories ŌĆö for example, a frontend app + backend API, a monorepo with multiple packages, or microservices that need coordinated changes.

## Current behavior

- Agent mode (the new dedicated agents window) scopes its understanding and actions to a single workspace folder
- There is no way to instruct the agent to "work across repo A and repo B" in a single session
- **In contrast, the Copilot Chat panel in the normal editor view works fine with multi-root workspaces** ŌĆö it can see and reference files across all folders in the workspace

## Expected behavior

Agent mode should be able to:

1. **Understand code across all folders** in a multi-root workspace, matching the behavior already available in the normal Chat panel
2. **Read and edit files across multiple workspace folders** in a single agent session
3. **Maintain context** about how code in one folder relates to code in another (e.g., API contracts, shared types, service boundaries)

## Use cases

- Coordinating API changes between a backend and frontend repo
- Refactoring shared types/interfaces across multiple packages in a workspace
- Making changes that span service boundaries in a microservices architecture
- Reviewing or understanding cross-repo dependencies

## Current workarounds

- Opening separate VS Code windows (one per repo) with independent agent sessions ŌĆö loses cross-repo context
- Using CLI-based AI tools that can operate across directories

## Environment

- VS Code Version: 1.122.0
- OS: Windows 11

title: Regression: screen sharing dialog appears on every startup on Linux in 1.122.0
body:
### Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?

Yes

### VS Code Version

`1.122.0-1779917575`

### OS Version

Linux, GNOME, Wayland session

### Problem Description

After upgrading to VS Code `1.122.0-1779917575`, a screen sharing dialog appears every time VS Code starts.

The dialog says:

> `org.chromium.Chromium is trying to share your screen`

This happens immediately on startup, even when all extensions are disabled.

I also tested with X11 mode forced:

```bash
code --disable-extensions --ozone-platform=x11
```

and the same dialog still appeared.

Downgrading VS Code to `1.121.0-1779186519` made the issue disappear.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install or upgrade to VS Code `1.122.0-1779917575` from the Microsoft APT repository on Linux.
2. Start VS Code.
3. Observe that a screen sharing dialog appears immediately on startup.

### Expected Behavior

VS Code should start normally without requesting screen sharing unless the user explicitly starts an action that requires it.

### Actual Behavior

A screen sharing dialog appears on every startup:

> `org.chromium.Chromium is trying to share your screen`

### Additional Notes

- Reproduces with `--disable-extensions`
- Reproduces with `--disable-extensions --ozone-platform=x11`
- Downgrading to `1.121.0-1779186519` fixes the problem
- As a local workaround, I pinned `1.122.0-1779917575` to avoid reinstalling that version

This may be unrelated, but I first noticed this after a GNOME Shell hang/restart.

title: BYOK Custom Endpoint with DeepSeek reasoning models fails with HTTP 400 on multi-turn tool calling (`reasoning_content` not echoed back)
body: ## Bug Description When using the **Custom Endpoint** BYOK feature with DeepSeek reasoning models (or similar providers like Moonshot/Minimax that emit `reasoning_content` without an explicit ID), the first LLM turn succeeds, but any subsequent turn (after a tool call) fails with: ``` 400 {"error":{"message":"The `reasoning_content` in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.","type":"invalid_request_error",...}} ``` The Copilot extension already contains logic in `OpenAIEndpoint.createRequestBody()` to echo back `reasoning_content` on assistant messages, but this logic is gated on `data.id` being present. DeepSeek does **not** send an `id` alongside `reasoning_content`, so the echo-back is silently skipped, and DeepSeek rejects the second turn. This makes Agent mode (multi-turn tool calling) unusable with DeepSeek reasoning models via BYOK. ## Version - VS Code: 1.122 (latest main) - GitHub Copilot Chat extension: bundled - OS: all (reproducible on Windows, macOS, Linux) ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Configure a **Custom Endpoint** with DeepSeek: ```json { "name": "DeepSeek", "vendor": "customendpoint", "apiKey": "sk-...", "apiType": "chat-completions", "url": "https://api.deepseek.com/chat/completions", "models": [{ "id": "deepseek-v4-flash", "toolCalling": true, "thinking": true, "maxInputTokens": 128000, "maxOutputTokens": 8192 }] } ``` 2. Open Chat, select the DeepSeek model 3. Send a prompt that triggers tool calling (e.g. "Create a file named `test.txt` with some content") 4. Observe: first turn succeeds, model returns `reasoning_content` + tool call 5. After tool executes, second turn is sent 6. Observe: **HTTP 400** from DeepSeek ## Expected Behavior The `reasoning_content` from the first turn should be echoed back in the assistant message of the second turn, so DeepSeek accepts the request. ## Actual Behavior The `reasoning_content` is silently dropped because it has no `id`, and the second turn fails with HTTP 400. ## Logs ``` 2026-05-29 07:10:17.012 [info] message 0 returned. finish reason: [tool_calls] 2026-05-29 07:10:17.019 [info] ccreq:d3c749f5.copilotmd | success | deepseek-v4-flash | 1336ms 2026-05-29 07:10:17.392 [info] Request ID for failed request: 1d4d4316-... 2026-05-29 07:10:17.396 [error] Server error: 400 {"error":{"message":"The `reasoning_content` in the thinking mode must be passed back to the API.","type":"invalid_request_error","param":null,"code":"invalid_request_error"}} 2026-05-29 07:10:17.399 [info] ccreq:0a452385.copilotmd | failed | deepseek-v4-flash | 225ms ``` ## Affected Providers - **DeepSeek** (V4, V3 reasoning mode) Any OpenAI-compatible provider that sends `reasoning_content` without an accompanying `id` field will hit this issue.

title: Github Copilot Agent Plugins - Support PLUGIN_ROOT in .mcp.json.
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Please add support for a PLUGIN_ROOT variable in `.mcp.json`.

Claude agent plugins support using [CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference) in `.mcp.json`. The [open-plugin-spec](https://github.com/vercel-labs/open-plugin-spec) uses PLUGIN_ROOT. Github Copilot does not seem to have an equivalent - [link to vscode code](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/9fd54367ba0143ff690170c7a25fe78da7c79bef/src/vs/platform/agentPlugins/common/pluginParsers.ts#L129).

See also https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/197251

**Example Use Case**
When launching pwsh I need a way to know where the plugin lives to run a script (since the pwsh working directory becomes the user home).

```json
{
    "mcpServers": {
        "my-mcp-server": {
            "command": "pwsh",
            "args": [
                "-NonInteractive",
                "-File",
                "${PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/my-mcp-server-bootstrapper.ps1"
            ]
        }
    }
}
```

title: Remote SSH: Connection times out in VS Code while terminal SSH works ŌĆö macOS Tahoe, Insiders 1.122.0
body:
## Environment

- **VS Code**: Insiders 1.122.0-insider
- **Commit**: fb72b9be76b694b456c5b15903e1f63457848ec8
- **Date**: 2026-05-28T16:51:00Z
- **Electron**: 42.2.0
- **ElectronBuildId**: 14159160
- **Chromium**: 148.0.7778.97
- **Node.js**: 24.15.0
- **V8**: 14.8.178.14-electron.0
- **OS**: Darwin arm64 25.5.0
- **Remote-SSH**: 0.123.0
- **SSH**: OpenSSH_10.2p1, LibreSSL 3.3.6

## Problem

SSH connects successfully from terminal (`ssh root@<host>`) ŌĆö **this works fine**. But VS Code Remote SSH consistently times out with:

```
ssh: connect to host <host> port 22: Operation timed out
```

The same server, same SSH key, same network ŌĆö terminal SSH works, VS Code Remote SSH does not.

VS Code immediately retries a second connection attempt via exec server, which also times out.

## Additional observations

1. **PATH env corruption** ŌĆö The PATH variable in the VS Code local server environment contains npm error output injected inline:
   ```
   Unknown command: bin
   
   To see a list of supported npm commands, run:
     npm help:
   ```
   This appears between path entries and may affect process spawning or environment inheritance.

2. **Node deprecation warning** ŌĆö Local server emits:
   ```
   (node:66919) [DEP0169] DeprecationWarning: `url.parse()` behavior is not standardized and prone to errors that have security implications. Use the WHATWG URL API instead.
   ```

3. **Double connection attempt** ŌĆö After the first timeout and `OfflineError`, VS Code spawns a second local server (serverId:2) which also times out.

4. **Dynamic port forwarding** ŌĆö VS Code adds `-D 62219` (SOCKS proxy) to the SSH command, which is not used when connecting manually from terminal. This could be a factor if the server or network restricts dynamic port forwarding.

## SSH command comparison

**Terminal (works):**
```
ssh root@<host>
```

**VS Code Remote SSH (times out):**
```
ssh -v -T -D 62219 -o ConnectTimeout=15 <host>
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Configure SSH host in `~/.ssh/config` with `User root`
2. Verify `ssh root@<host>` connects successfully from terminal
3. Connect via Remote-SSH extension in VS Code Insiders
4. Connection times out after 15 seconds

title: Terminal: Selection background appears and input fails when pressing Backspace/Space/Esc on Windows 10
body:
Type: Bug ### Environment - VS Code Version: Latest Stable (1.114.x) - OS: Windows 10 (PowerShell as default terminal) - Terminal Type: Integrated (Windows PowerShell) - Extensions: Disabled (tried in clean mode) ### Description When using the integrated terminal in VS Code, the following issues occur after typing characters: 1. Pressing and holding the `Backspace` key causes a selection background to appear in the terminal. The delete function stops working, and the cursor does not move back. 2. Pressing the `Space` key triggers a similar selection background. No space character is inserted, and the key press has no effect. 3. Pressing the `Esc` key also shows a selection highlight, and the terminal becomes unresponsive to further input until I click elsewhere or press Enter. This issue appears to be a rendering or input handling bug, not related to extensions (tested with all extensions disabled). The problem persists even after: - Disabling GPU acceleration (`"terminal.integrated.gpuAcceleration": "off"`) - Changing cursor style to line - Disabling shell integration - Resetting VS Code settings ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open VS Code on Windows 10. 2. Open the integrated terminal (PowerShell). 3. Type any characters (e.g., `test`). 4. Press and hold `Backspace`, or press `Space` / `Esc`. ### Expected Behavior - `Backspace` should delete characters normally. - `Space` should insert a space character. - `Esc` should work as expected (e.g., exit current mode). - No selection/highlight background should appear during these operations. ### Actual Behavior - A solid selection background appears in the terminal. - The corresponding key function fails (delete/space does not work). - The terminal becomes temporarily unresponsive to these inputs. title: Agent Picker not working with Agent Hosts
body:
* Open VS Code
* Select `Copilot CLI Agent Host`
* Select a custom agent
* Send a promtp
* Open another session
* Come back to the above (first) session
* Agent picker is now empty

Whats happening is
* The correct agent information is being sent back from agent host to client
* Race condition in `chatModes.ts`, as a result of which the agent isn't valid
* The agent is reset to empty,
* This empty selection goes back to the agent host

I.e. just opening a session clears the agent selection from the agent host!

title: High CPU from VS Code `rg` when Java workspace detection scans pnpm `node_modules` in mixed frontend and backend workspace
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.122.0 (Universal)
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.5.0



**Steps To Reproduce**

```text
1. Create or open a VS Code multi-root workspace that contains both:
   - a Java backend project, for example: `/Users/me/projects/example-backend`
   - a large frontend project using pnpm, for example: `/Users/me/projects/example-frontend`

2. Make sure the Java backend contains typical Java project files, such as:
   - `pom.xml`
   - `build.gradle`
   - `.classpath`

3. Make sure the frontend project has a large pnpm dependency tree, for example:
   - `example-frontend/node_modules/.pnpm`
   - package-level `node_modules` symlinks if using a monorepo layout

4. Enable Java-related VS Code extensions, such as:
   - Extension Pack for Java
   - Language Support for Java by Red Hat
   - Maven for Java
   - Java Test Runner / Debugger for Java

5. Reload the VS Code window.

6. Open Activity Monitor / Task Manager / `ps` and observe VS Code-bundled `rg` processes consuming high CPU for an extended period.

7. Inspect the `rg` processes. In my case, their command lines included Java-related file discovery patterns such as:
   - `pom.xml`
   - `.classpath`
   - `*.gradle`
   - `*.java`

   They also included options similar to:
   - `--no-ignore`
   - `--follow`

8. Optional: run `lsof -p <rg_pid>` and observe that the `rg` process is traversing the frontend dependency tree, such as:
   - `/example-project/example-frontend/node_modules/.pnpm/...`
   - `/example-project/example-frontend/packages/*/node_modules/...`

9. Remove the Java backend folder from the mixed workspace, or open the Java backend in a separate VS Code window.

10. Reload the original frontend workspace and observe that the high-CPU `rg` processes no longer appear.
```



 

In my case, the VS Code window was opened with a mixed frontend/backend multi-root workspace. One folder was a Java backend project, and another folder was a large frontend project with a big pnpm/node_modules tree.

The symptom was that several VS Code-bundled `rg` processes kept using very high CPU for a long time. Their parent process was the VS Code Extension Host. The command lines looked like file discovery scans for Java-related activation patterns, for example:

- `pom.xml`
- `.classpath`
- `*.gradle`
- `*.gradle.kts`
- `*.java`

The important detail is that these `rg` processes were started with options like:

```text
--no-ignore
--follow
```

To investigate further, I checked the running `rg` processes with `ps` and then inspected their open file descriptors with `lsof`. The `cwd` of those `rg` processes was the workspace folder, and `lsof` showed that they were actively traversing frontend dependency directories such as:

```text
/example-project/example-frontend/node_modules/.pnpm/...
/example-project/example-frontend/packages/*/node_modules
```

This made the behavior much easier to understand:

- `--no-ignore` means normal ignore rules such as `.gitignore` are not respected, so `node_modules` is not skipped.
- `--follow` means symlinks are followed.
- pnpm creates a symlink-heavy `node_modules` structure.
- In a large frontend monorepo, traversing `node_modules/.pnpm` can be extremely expensive.

So my theory is that Java-related workspace detection, probably through `workspaceContains` activation checks, was triggered by the Java backend folder, but because the VS Code window was a mixed multi-root workspace, the resulting file discovery also traversed the large frontend project. Instead of being effectively limited to the Java backend, it ended up scanning the frontend dependency tree as well.

As a workaround, I removed the Java backend folder from that mixed workspace and reloaded the VS Code window. The high-CPU `rg` processes disappeared immediately.

So one possible workaround is:

- Avoid putting large frontend projects and Java backend projects in the same VS Code multi-root workspace.
- Open the Java backend in a separate VS Code window.
- Or disable Java-related extensions for the mixed workspace if Java support is not needed there.
- It may also help to try settings such as `"search.followSymlinks": false`, `"search.ripgrep.maxThreads": 2`, or `"search.experimental.useIgnoreFilesInFindFiles": true`, but separating the Java project was the workaround that clearly fixed it for me.

This may not be the only cause of high CPU usage from `rg`, since there are many reports of similar symptoms with different root causes. But in this case, the evidence points to Java extension workspace detection running across a large mixed workspace and traversing pnpm/node_modules directories because of `--no-ignore` and `--follow`.

title: Add "Debug: On Errors"
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

### Problem / Motivation

As a Python developer who relies on multiple type checkers (e.g., Pylance, Pyrefly), I currently have to manually create `.vscode/launch.json` and `.vscode/tasks.json` for every new project just to wire up pre-launch type checking via `preLaunchTask`.

This approach has two major pain points:

1. **Repeated boilerplate** ŌĆö every small project needs the same configuration overhead.
2. **CLI-less checkers are excluded** ŌĆö extensions like Pylance have no CLI interface, so they cannot participate in `preLaunchTask` at all, regardless of how the project is configured.

---

### Proposed Solution

Introduce a new VSCode setting, e.g. `debug.onErrors`, that controls debug launch behavior when the PROBLEMS panel contains error-level diagnostics:

| Value                | Behavior                                           |
|----------------------|----------------------------------------------------|
|debugAnyway (default) | Ignore errors and start debugging.                 |
|showErrors            | Show the Problems view and do not start debugging. |
|prompt                | Ask the user whether to continue.                  |
|abort                 | Cancel debugging.                                  |

The default `debugAnyway` ensures zero behavioral change for existing users who are unaware of this setting.

---

### Expected Benefit

With this change, developers working on small projects can install type checker extensions (Pylance, Pyrefly, etc.) and immediately benefit from error-aware debugging ŌĆö **without creating any `.vscode` directory or configuration files**. This enables a true zero-configuration, out-of-the-box type checking experience. Moreover, users can configure the inspector, and the settings can be synchronized between different devices along with the Profile, instead of having to manually configure it at least once for each project.

---

### Alternatives Considered

- Using `preLaunchTask` with CLI-based checkers (e.g., mypy, pyright CLI) ŌĆö works, but requires per-project setup and excludes CLI-less extensions.
- Project templates / snippets ŌĆö reduces repetition but does not solve the Pylance exclusion problem.

   ```json
   {
     "servers": {
       "poc-server": {
         "type": "stdio",
         "command": "node",
         "args": ["-e", "setInterval(() => {}, 1000)"],
         "env": {
           "NODE_OPTIONS": "--inspect=127.0.0.1:9229"
         }
       }
     }
   }
   ```

2. Open the workspace and trust it - the MCP server starts.

3. Check if port 9229 is open:
   ```
   ss -tlnp | grep 9229
   ```

4. Compare with launching the same command manually:
   ```
   NODE_OPTIONS=--inspect=127.0.0.1:9229 node -e "setInterval(() => {}, 1000)"
   ```
   Note that port 9229 is opened by the child process.

## Expected Behavior

`NODE_OPTIONS`, `LD_PRELOAD`, and `DEBUG` should be stripped from the subprocess environment before spawning the MCP server process, matching the behavior of every other subprocess spawn path in VS Code (`localProcessExtensionHost.ts:239`, `extensionHostConnection.ts:259`, `utilityProcess.ts:294`, `ipc.cp.ts:204`).

## Actual Behavior

The MCP spawn path in `extHostMcpNode.ts:100-105` passes the full unsanitized environment (including `process.env` + envFile vars + `launch.env`) directly to `child_process.spawn()`. The existing `removeDangerousEnvVariables()` function at `src/vs/base/common/processes.ts:128-153` which strips `NODE_OPTIONS`, `LD_PRELOAD`, and `DEBUG` is never called in the MCP code path.

This allows injection of arbitrary Node.js flags via `NODE_OPTIONS` (e.g., `--inspect`, `--require`) and shared library injection via `LD_PRELOAD`, capabilities that every other spawn path in VS Code explicitly prevents.

## Relevant Code

- **Missing call:** `src/vs/workbench/api/node/extHostMcpNode.ts` - `startNodeMcp()` assembles the `env` object at lines 64ŌĆō82 but never calls `removeDangerousEnvVariables(env)` before passing it to `spawn()` at lines 100ŌĆō105.

- **Existing guard:** `src/vs/base/common/processes.ts:128-153` -`removeDangerousEnvVariables()` already exists and is used by extension hosts, utility processes, and IPC children. MCP is the sole omission.

title: `Branch Changes` is empty when creating a new Copilot Agent Host Session with chagnes
body:
* Start Agent Window
* Start a new session (Copilot CLI Agent Host) with the prompt `update the readme.md file with some random changes`
* Wait for turn to complete
* You can see the diff counter in the session list (left hand side)
* However when you look under the `Branch Changes` in `Changes`, there's nothing
* If you select `Uncommitted Changes` you can see the changes
* If you go back to `Branch Changes` still nothing
* Go to another session and come back, now things work

title: Insiders Download Link on VS Code Website Points to Stable Version Instead
body:

title: Copilot Chat debug file logging omits per-turn events for empty-window chat sessions
body:
## Summary

Copilot Chat debug file logging only writes full per-turn events when Chat is run in a real workspace-backed VS Code window. With the documented/visible file logging setting enabled, empty-window `code chat --reuse-window` sessions persist chat transcript files globally but do not create workspace debug logs containing `user_message`, `agent_response`, `llm_request`, or token counts. This makes it look like debug file logging is enabled but not capturing turns.

## Environment

- VS Code: 1.122.0
- Bundled GitHub Copilot Chat extension: 0.50.0
- macOS
- User setting enabled: `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.fileLogging.enabled = true`

## Repro

1. In user settings, enable `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.fileLogging.enabled`.
2. Restart VS Code.
3. Run an empty-window chat turn from the CLI, for example:

   ```bash
   code chat --mode ask --reuse-window 'Reply with DEBUG-LOG-TEST only.'
   ```

4. Inspect VS Code workspace storage for Copilot Chat debug logs:

   ```bash
   find "$HOME/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage" -type f -name 'main.jsonl' -print0 | xargs -0 ls -lt | head -n 5
   jq -r '.type' "/path/to/main.jsonl" | sort | uniq -c
   ```

5. Open a real folder in VS Code, then run the same `code chat --reuse-window` command again.
6. Inspect the workspace-backed debug log under that folder's `workspaceStorage/.../GitHub.copilot-chat/debug-logs/.../main.jsonl`.

## Expected

If `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.fileLogging.enabled` is true and Chat is accepting turns, disk logs should either:

- include the per-turn events and token counts for the chat session, or
- clearly indicate that per-turn file logging requires a workspace-backed window and is unavailable in empty-window chat sessions.

## Actual

Empty-window CLI chat sessions do not produce the expected workspace debug log events. The only prior debug session files I found contained `session_start` metadata only. The chat content itself was written separately under global empty-window chat session storage, not under `workspaceStorage/.../GitHub.copilot-chat/debug-logs`.

When the same chat command is run after opening an actual folder/workspace window, file logging works and `main.jsonl` includes the expected events:

```text
agent_response: 1
child_session_ref: 1
discovery: 10
llm_request: 1
session_start: 1
turn_end: 1
turn_start: 1
user_message: 1
```

The `llm_request` entry included token fields such as:

```text
model: gpt-5-mini
inputTokens: 642
outputTokens: 1810
```

## Investigation notes

Local source inspection of the bundled extension suggests that the file logger is gated by `github.copilot.chat.agentDebugLog.fileLogging.enabled` and writes via `extensionContext.storageUri`. That matches the observed behavior: when Chat is associated with a real workspace storage URI, per-turn debug logging appears; in the empty-window path, it does not.

## Request

Please either make Copilot Chat debug file logging capture per-turn events for empty-window chat sessions too, or document/surface that file debug logging only works when Chat is running in a workspace-backed window. It would also help if logs included an explicit event explaining why detailed debug logging is skipped when `extensionContext.storageUri` is unavailable.

title: getCwd() on macOS invokes lsof on every terminal command, generating many syscalls per invocation
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: **Yes**

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
Version: 3.5.38
VSCode Version: 1.105.1
Commit: 009bb5a3600dd98fe1c1f25798f767f686e14750
Date: 2026-05-26T21:32:06.537Z
Layout: editor
Build Type: Stable
Release Track: Default
Electron: 39.8.1
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Darwin arm64 25.4.0


**Expected**: No lsof invocation. Shell integration already delivered the CWD via OSC 633 (P;Cwd=...) before getCwd() was called.
**Actual**: lsof -OPln -p <pid> | grep cwd is executed on every command completion regardless of whether shell integration has already reported the CWD.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Run `sudo fs_usage -w -f exec 2>/dev/null | grep --line-buffered lsof` in a terminal
2. Run `echo hello` in vscode integrated terminal
  a. you should see `execve    /usr/sbin/lsof ...` in your terminal from (1), clearly lsof is being invoked

It's a little annoying to count number of syscalls, but this script does the trick
```
#!/bin/bash
LOG=/tmp/fs_sample.log
echo "Sampling 10s ŌĆö run a command in VS Code's integrated terminal now..."
sudo fs_usage -f filesys 2>/dev/null > "$LOG" &
FSPID=$!
sleep 10
sudo kill $FSPID 2>/dev/null
wait $FSPID 2>/dev/null
TOTAL=$(grep -c 'lsof' "$LOG" 2>/dev/null)
CLOSES=$(grep 'lsof' "$LOG" | grep -c ' close ')
echo ""
echo "lsof syscall total:  $TOTAL"
echo "  of which close():  $CLOSES"
echo ""
echo "lsof executions:     $(grep -c 'execve.*lsof\|lsof.*execve' "$LOG" 2>/dev/null)"
```

Example output...
```
$ sudo bash counter.sh 
Sampling 10s ŌĆö run a command in VS Code's integrated terminal now...

lsof syscall total:  245921
  of which close():  245771

lsof executions:     1
```

Oof, a quarter million syscalls because I ran 'echo' in the terminal.


---

I think this is the root cause: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/f0fd9f0dfb9aa1fddcfbb5e39c484c407dd0e22c/src/vs/platform/terminal/node/terminalProcess.ts#L617

Root cause: terminalProcess.ts:606. The lsof approach was added as a workaround for a Big Sur Electron spawn-blocking bug (#105446). The comment in the code acknowledges it was fixed ("this is fixed in VS Code's internal Electron") but the workaround was never removed. On macOS, lsof enumerates every open file descriptor system-wide before filtering to the target PID, even with -p.

---

Admittedly chat-recommended proposal...

```
async getCwd(): Promise<string> {
    if (isMacintosh) {
        // Shell integration reports CWD via OSC 633 P;Cwd=... on every prompt.
        // Use it if available instead of spawning lsof.
        if (this._properties.cwd) {
            return this._properties.cwd;
        }
        // Fallback: lsof (expensive ŌĆö see #105446 comment)
        // Long-term: replace with proc_pidinfo(PROC_PIDVNODEPATHINFO)
        ...
    }
}
```

This is particularly acute for users with endpoint security software (SentinelOne, CrowdStrike) which intercepts each syscall and makes the system run very slow when there's a lot of syscalls.

title: GitHub Copilot in v1.122 combines Markdown paragraphs in chat display
body:
1. Issue a prompt in the chat consisting of a small paragraph, followed by a single sentence. e.g.

```markdown
I am puzzled by your earlier response. Blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. In addition blah blah blah blah blah and blah blah blah. Furthermore blah blah blah blah blah blah. 

Nevertheless, what do you think of Foo Bar and the Widgets?
```

**Expected:** The two paragraph should be separated, as they always have, and as is the rules when parsing and rendering Markdown.

> I am puzzled by your earlier response. Blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. In addition blah blah blah blah blah and blah blah blah. Furthermore blah blah blah blah blah blah. 
> 
> Nevertheless, what do you think of Foo Bar and the Widgets?

**Actual:** The two paragraphs have been merged into one.

> I am puzzled by your earlier response. Blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah blah. In addition blah blah blah blah blah and blah blah blah. Furthermore blah blah blah blah blah blah. Nevertheless, what do you think of Foo Bar and the Widgets?

If I edit the submitted prompt in the conversation history, the edited "source" shows the two paragraphs as separate. This suggests that the problem has something to do with some new parsing or rendering engine, not the editing or data entry/persistence.

title: Agents: Selected model shown incorrectly for existing sessions
body:
## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Agent Window
2. Start an Agent Host session with **Opus 4.7**, wait for completion
3. Start another session with **Opus 5.5**, wait for completion
4. Open both sessions ŌĆö both will show either 4.7 or 5.5 (the same model for both)
5. Exit and reload ŌĆö the issue persists

## Expected

Each session should display the model that was actually selected when the session was created.

## Actual

Both sessions show the same model (whichever was last selected), even after reload. The selected model is not correctly persisted/restored per session.

title: REGRESSION: new default dark theme is absolute crap for syntax highlight
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes - VS Code Version: 1.122 - OS Version: Manjaro Linux I never changed the theme or touched any theme-related settings, and the syntax highlight of PHP code used to look more or less like this: I think the theme that was selected (again, I didn't choose it and I didn't change it) was called "Default Dark Modern". Now, after some update, the selected theme has changed to "**Dark 2026**" and **the syntax highlight (for PHP at least) is absolute shit**: This is _not_ a matter of personal preference: the choice of colors is **objectively bad**: two things that stand out are: - a lot of things are **red**, which is distracting and **makes errors not stand out** - things like variables are just light grey, which make them look like plain text and make you wonder whether there's some syntax error that's preventing the code from being highlighted properly. There's no "Default Modern Dark" anymore among the available themes, but there's a "Modern Dark" which seems at least very close if not identical to what I was used to, and it's what I've used to take the first screenshot, as I couldn't find an old screenshot from before the change. Having reasonable, decent syntax highlight by default out of the box is important. Additionally, changing the theme upon update to an objectively hugely inferior one is particularly unacceptable. I understand that a default can change (as opposed to a setting that the user has set manually) but it has to be an improvement; this is not only not an improvement, this is the shittiest syntax highlight I've seen in a long time.

title: Remote sessions appear in session list when agent host is disabled
body:
### Bug Description
When `"chat.agentHost.enabled"` is set to `false`, remote sessions still show up in the session list. This can be confusing as disabling the agent host should logically prevent remote agent hosts/sessions from appearing.

#### Steps to Reproduce
1. Set `"chat.agentHost.enabled": false` in your settings.
2. Observe that remote sessions are still visible in the sessions list, despite the agent host being disabled.

#### Expected Behavior
Remote sessions should not appear in the session list when the agent host is disabled.

#### Actual Behavior
Remote sessions continue to show up in the available sessions list.

<!-- Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No -->
<!-- VS Code Version: -->
<!-- OS Version: -->

Slack source context (for reference):
> have agent host disabled but remote sessions still show up in the sessions list
> ```
> "chat.agentHost.enabled": false,
> "chat.remoteAgentHosts": []
> ```

title: MCP HTTP Client Regression
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
VS Code 1.122ŌĆÖs built-in HTTP MCP transport is throwing `TypeError: fetch failed` before it opens a socket. Evidence:

- curl, terminal node, browser fetch, and VS CodeŌĆÖs bundled Electron/Node runtime can all POST to https://mcp.example.com/mcp.
- VS CodeŌĆÖs built-in HTTP MCP client fails against localhost, 127.0.0.1, and even a tiny probe server on 127.0.0.1:8999.
- That probe server logged no connection from VS Code, so the request never reached https://mcp.example.com/mcp or the network layer.
- Switching to a stdio bridge via mcp-remote works immediately. VS Code discovered 3 tools, and my MCP server logged the MCP initialize and tools/list traffic.

Works:
```json
{
  "servers": {
    "my-mcp-server": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": [
        "-y",
        "mcp-remote",
        "https://mcp.example.com/mcp"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

Does not work as of 1.122.0:
```json
{
  "servers": {
    "my-mcp-server": {
      "url": "https://mcp.example.com/mcp",
      "type": "http"
    }
  }
}
```

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.122
- OS Version: MacOS Tahoe 26.5

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Add HTTP MCP server to mcp.json, see examples above.
2. Attempt to start it.
3. It will not start. MCP logs show:
```text
2026-05-28 13:34:03.696 [info] Connection state: Starting
2026-05-28 13:34:03.709 [info] Starting server from LocalProcess extension host
2026-05-28 13:34:03.711 [info] Connection state: Running
2026-05-28 13:34:03.724 [info] Connection state: Error Error sending message to https://mcp.example.com/mcp: TypeError: fetch failed
```

title: Preserve Original Prompt Structure When Constructing CAPI Responses API Input Messages
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

In CAPI Responses API, when constructing input messages of the next request, we should preserve the original structure and ordering of the model output as much as possible. This would improve cache hit rates by keeping repeated prompt prefixes stable and efficient, and it would also make the prompt format closer to what the model saw during training, which should lead to more accurate behavior.

Currently, we observe two post-processing in `vscode-copilot-chat` extension:
- Within the same turn, previous rounds can be transformed in a way that changes the original ordering of commentary and analysis. That is, the original order could be `commentary-then-analysis`, but the post-processing will convert them to `analysis-then-commentary`
  - This is the primary issue that affects model quality
- Historical turns drop the modelŌĆÖs analysis / reasoning content
  - This is fine. We can discuss whether it should be kept or not

Ideally, input construction should avoid such reshaping of prior assistant output. Historical and previous-round content should remain as close as possible to the original model response, including preserving reasoning/analysis metadata when available and maintaining the original relative order between commentary, analysis, final text, and tool calls.

## Notes
- Each turn represents a user request. Each turn could contain multiple rounds (model requests).
- We do not hit such commentary-analysis reordering issue when using AOAI BYOK, since AOAI BYOK Response API uses **stateful** response API calls while CAPI uses **stateless** calls.

title: Copilot OTel: gen_ai.request.model and gen_ai.response.model use inconsistent naming formats
body:
## Summary

On `chat` and `invoke_agent` spans emitted by `copilot-chat`, the `gen_ai.request.model` and `gen_ai.response.model` attributes use different naming formats for the same logical model, causing deduplication/filtering issues for telemetry consumers.

## Observed data

| `gen_ai.request.model` | `gen_ai.response.model` | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-opus-4.6` | `claude-opus-4-6` | Dot ŌåÆ hyphen version separator |
| `gpt-5.4-mini` | `gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17` | Alias ŌåÆ dated snapshot (expected) |
| `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` | `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` | Ō£ģ Consistent |

## Impact

- `GROUP BY` / `DISTINCT` on model produce duplicate rows for Claude
- Filtering by one format misses spans recorded with the other
- The `gpt-5.4-mini` ŌåÆ `gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17` case is arguably correct (alias ŌåÆ resolved), but `claude-opus-4.6` ŌåÆ `claude-opus-4-6` is purely a formatting inconsistency

## Expected

Per [OTel GenAI semantic conventions](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/), `request.model` is the model requested and `response.model` is the model that responded. When the same model handles the request, only the level of specificity should differ ŌĆö not the format (`.` vs `-`).

Either normalize `request.model` to the API identifier format, or have `response.model` echo back the alias.

## Repro

Collect OTLP from Copilot Chat (via `github.copilot.advanced.debug.collectOTel`) with model set to Claude Opus 4.6. Compare `gen_ai.request.model` vs `gen_ai.response.model` on any `chat` span.

title: [1.122.0 regression] xdg-desktop-portal-kde Screen Sharing dialog appears on launch / Markdown preview on KDE Plasma 6 Wayland (not present in 1.121.0)
body:
### Type of issue

Bug

### Version

1.122.0

### OS Version

Debian GNU/Linux 13.5 (trixie), KDE Plasma 6 on Wayland

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch VS Code 1.122.0 on a KDE Plasma 6 Wayland session (Debian 13.5, Plasma 6, `XDG_SESSION_TYPE=wayland`).
2. Open any file (or open the Markdown preview of a `.md` file).

### Actual behavior

The `xdg-desktop-portal-kde` **Screen Sharing** dialog appears, prompting the user to "Choose which screen to share with:" (Full Workspace / Rectangular Region / individual monitors). This happens shortly after VS Code starts and again when opening a Markdown preview.

Closing the dialog cancels the screencast and VS Code continues normally, but the dialog reappears on subsequent launches and previews.

Journal evidence (`journalctl --user`):

```
xdg-desktop-portal-kde[...]: xdp-kde-screencast: Aborting screencast
code[5088]: [...:ERROR:third_party/webrtc/modules/desktop_capture/linux/wayland/screencast_portal.cc:369] Failed to start the screen cast session.
code[5088]: [...:ERROR:third_party/webrtc/modules/desktop_capture/linux/wayland/base_capturer_pipewire.cc:93] ScreenCastPortal failed: 2
```

The PipeWire screen-capture portal is being invoked by VS Code's Chromium subsystem at startup / WebView creation, without any user action that should require screen capture (no Live Share, no recording extension, no `getDisplayMedia` call from any open page).

### Expected behavior

No screen-sharing prompt should appear when simply launching VS Code or opening a Markdown preview. Screen capture should only be requested when an extension or feature explicitly needs it.

### Regression

This **does not occur on 1.121.0**, identical environment, identical user profile and extensions. Issue started immediately after upgrading from `1.121.0-1779186519` to `1.122.0-1779917575` (`.deb` package size jumped from ~158 MB to ~232 MB, suggesting a major Electron / Chromium bump).

Downgrading to 1.121.0 (`apt install code_1.121.0-1779186519_amd64.deb && apt-mark hold code`) fully resolves the issue.

### Workarounds attempted (none fully effective on 1.122.0)

1. `--disable-features=WebRTCPipeWireCapturer` Chromium flag ŌåÆ Electron warns *"'disable-features' is not in the list of known options, but still passed to Electron/Chromium"* and screencast portal is still invoked.
2. `--ozone-platform=x11` ŌåÆ flag is applied (confirmed in `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`), but the PipeWire portal is still called.
3. `ELECTRON_OZONE_PLATFORM_HINT=x11` env var (Plasma session-wide) ŌåÆ same outcome.
4. Full env masking (unset `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`, set `XDG_SESSION_TYPE=x11`, `GDK_BACKEND=x11`, `QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb`, plus all flags above) ŌĆö only this aggressive combination might suppress it, but it should not be necessary.

Only **downgrading to 1.121.0** cleanly resolves the prompt.

### Extensions

No screen-capture, screen-share, or recording extensions installed. No Live Share. Verified via `ls ~/.vscode/extensions/ | grep -iE 'live.?share|screen|record|cast|pair'` ŌåÆ empty.

### Additional environment

- Electron: 39.8.8 (from Crashpad annotations)
- `xdg-desktop-portal-kde` 6.x (Plasma 6)
- PipeWire / WirePlumber active
- No Flatpak (native `.deb` from `packages.microsoft.com`)
- Other Electron apps in the same session (e.g., Claude Desktop, launched explicitly with `--ozone-platform=x11`) do **not** exhibit this behavior.

### Reproducibility

100% ŌĆö every launch and every Markdown preview open on 1.122.0 in this environment.

title: No way to start a new project in Agents Window when using Simple File Dialog
body:
### Steps to Reproduce:

1. Enable `"files.simpleDialog.enable": true`
2. Try to select a folder using the file picker

### Bug
You'll find that there is no way to make a new folder. 

Instead, you have to use a terminal to `mkdir` the folder, then select it.

On local OS's, native file pickers often have "New Folder" buttons, which makes this a regression in convenience, especially for the Agent Host or when the simple file picker is used.

title: Extension host crash loop (exit code 5) on startup when Copilot Chat is enabled in a large multi-root workspace
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: `github.copilot-chat@0.48.1`
- VS Code Version: 1.120.0 (commit 0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, arm64)
- OS Version: macOS 26.2 (Darwin arm64)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): N/A, the host crashes at startup before any chat session is opened
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): N/A, see above
- Logs: see below

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a multi-root `.code-workspace` containing ~50 folders (mix of git repos, several large; same workspace as microsoft/vscode#318279).
2. Have `github.copilot-chat@0.48.1` enabled. Do not interact with the window.
3. Wait ~25 to 30 seconds after the Extension Host starts.
4. Renderer reports `Extension host (LocalProcess pid: N) terminated unexpectedly`; main log shows `exit code: 5, signal: unknown`. Auto-restart starts a new host, which dies on the same cycle.

## Bisect

Reproduced with `code --disable-extension <id>` per launch, watching `~/Library/Application Support/Code/logs/<ts>/main.log` for ~75s after each Extension Host start:

| Launch | What was disabled (active 3rd-party set) | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--disable-extensions` | all 3rd-party | survive >80s |
| Round 1 | second half of activated extensions, alphabetical | crash ~26s |
| Round 2 | + claude-code, copilot-chat, postgres, prettier, biome | survive >80s |
| Round 3 | + only prettier, biome | crash ~21s |
| Round 4 | + postgres also | crash ~21s |
| Round 5 | + copilot-chat also | survive >80s |
| Re-enable all | (none, copilot-chat back on) | crash ~28s |

Disabling `github.copilot-chat` alone with every other extension enabled is sufficient. Re-enabling it brings the loop back.

## Logs (one cycle)

`main.log`:

```
2026-05-28 10:04:46.787 [info] Extension host with pid 40087 exited with code: 5, signal: unknown.
2026-05-28 10:04:46.787 [error] [UtilityProcess id: 2, type: extensionHost, pid: 40087]: crashed with code 5 and reason 'crashed'
```

`renderer.log`:

```
2026-05-28 10:04:19.398 [info] Started local extension host with pid 40087.
2026-05-28 10:04:46.790 [error] Extension host (LocalProcess pid: 40087) terminated unexpectedly. The following extensions were running: ..., GitHub.copilot-chat, ...
```

`exthost.log` (next cycle, right before next kill, only chat-related error):

```
2026-05-28 10:04:54.643 [error] Error calling provideChatSessionProviderOptions for handle 0: Canceled
```

Earlier the same day, a same-coalition Homebrew `node` child (thread named `chrome-devtools-mcp`) crashed with the classic V8 OOM stack (`node::OOMErrorHandler` to `v8::internal::V8::FatalProcessOutOfMemory`). Different process, but consistent memory-pressure signal in this VS Code instance, suggesting the exthost is also V8-OOMing.

## Possibly related

microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#14139 is also `exit code 5` in a Copilot Chat workflow on macOS, but that one is the *renderer* crashing during sustained Remote-SSH chat usage; this one is the *local extension host* crashing on startup. Likely a shared memory-pressure root cause.

## Workaround

Disable `github.copilot-chat`, per launch with `code --disable-extension github.copilot-chat`, or persistently via the Extensions sidebar.
