title: Wayland: Portal screen share dialog appears on every startup (desktopCapturer.getSources in warmUpScreenSources)
body:
## Environment

| | |
|---|---|
| **VS Code** | 1.122.0 (`visual-studio-code-bin`) |
| **Electron** | 39.8.8 |
| **Chrome** | 142.0.7444.265 |
| **OS** | Arch Linux, kernel 6.18.33 |
| **Compositor** | niri (Wayland) |
| **PipeWire** | 1.6.6 |
| **xdg-desktop-portal** | 1.20.4 |
| **xdg-desktop-portal-gnome** | 50.0 |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch VS Code on any Wayland compositor (niri, Sway, Hyprland, etc.)
2. An xdg-desktop-portal screen share dialog appears immediately:

```
org.chromium.Chromium wants to share your screen.
Choose what you'd like to share.
```

3. Dialog appears on every cold start, regardless of extensions or settings.

## Root Cause

`src/vs/code/electron-main/app.ts` lines 234-256:

```typescript
const warmUpScreenSources = () => {
    desktopCapturer.getSources({         // <-- triggers portal dialog on Wayland
        types: ['screen'],
        thumbnailSize: { width: 0, height: 0 },
    }).then(...)
};

if (!isMacintosh || systemPreferences.getMediaAccessStatus('screen') === 'granted') {
    warmUpScreenSources();               // <-- always true on Linux (!isMacintosh)
}
```

On macOS, `getMediaAccessStatus('screen')` guards this call ŌĆö if screen recording permission is denied, the warm-up is skipped. On Linux, `!isMacintosh` is unconditionally `true`, so `desktopCapturer.getSources()` always runs at startup.

On Wayland, `desktopCapturer.getSources()` goes through the xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast portal, which requires user consent and pops up the screen share permission dialog. The same code on X11 works fine because screen enumeration does not require portal mediation.

## Confirmed Not the Cause

- Extensions (`--disable-extensions` has no effect)
- `issueReporter.wizard.enabled` setting
- `--disable-features=DesktopCapture,WebRTCPipeWireCapturer` Chromium flags (dialog still appears)
- Google Chrome 148 on the same system does **not** exhibit this behavior (only Electron 39 `desktopCapturer.getSources()` triggers it)

## Suggested Fix

Either:

**a)** Skip `warmUpScreenSources()` on Linux (same as macOS when permission is denied):

```typescript
if (isMacintosh && systemPreferences.getMediaAccessStatus('screen') === 'granted') {
    warmUpScreenSources();
}
```

**b)** Or defer the warm-up to only run when the issue reporter actually needs screen sources, rather than proactively in the constructor.

This is a regression ŌĆö older versions of VS Code (<1.121) on the same system did not show this dialog.

title: Notifications: Allow richer content formatting beyond plain text
body:
**Problem:**

  VS Code notifications currently only support plain text (with links/buttons). This makes it hard to surface important information clearly.

**Proposal:**

  Allow optional richer formatting in notifications, like:
  - **Progress flows** ŌĆö step indicators with dots or icons
  - **Icons & color accents** ŌĆö to signal severity or state at a glance
  - **Text styles** ŌĆö bold/muted to create visual hierarchy

  This would make it easier for users to instantly recognize when something needs their attention, without relying on plain text alone.

title: Undocumented removal of Github backed Copilot memory
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: latest built-in version
- VS Code Version: 1.122.0
- OS Version: OSX 25.5.0
- Feature: Copilot memory in agent mode
- Selected model: All models

**Steps to Reproduce**

1. Configure Github stored Copilot memory following the online instructions provided by Microsoft:

- Enable Github Copilot Memory for your repository/organisation/user/enterprise: https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/copilot-memory

2. Enable Github stored Copilot memory in VS Code
- Enable Agent Memory as documented: https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_109#_copilot-memory-preview
  - Add vscode setting: 'github.copilot.chat.copilotMemory.enabled:true'
 
- In VS Code disable the local memory tool and enable GitHub Memory: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/agents/memory
  - Disable local memory: 'github.copilot.chat.tools.memory.enabled:false
  - Enable remote Github memory: 'github.copilot.chat.copilotMemory.enabled:true'

3. In a VS Code Copilot Chat session ask the agent to store a memory and to retreve all memories.

**Expected Outcome**

* Memories are stored to Github backed memory by Copilot.
* Memories can be retrieved by Copilot in VS Code

**Actual Outcome**

* Copilot cannot find memory tools and falls back to attempting local file based memory.

**Likely Cause**

#315794 Disabled Copilot Memory
#315813 Removed Copilot Memory 

Looks like it was fully removed in VS Code 1.120, with no documentation or information included in release notes https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_120 and no updates to any of the published documentation instructing developers how to use the related Github Copilot features.

**Impact**

Not possible to have memory interop between Github Cloud Agents, which store memory Github backed, and local Copilot sessions in VS Code which cannot access Github backed memory. So the self-improving local/remote Agent based workflows that Microsoft is trying to enable on Github is broken as per the current officially documented setup. Every organisation and team is instead required to create their own memory solution which will interop between Cloud and local agents, instead of using the built-in Microsoft documented way.

- VS Code Version: 1.122.0
- OS Version: Ubuntu 22.04
- Copilot extension version: 1.388.0 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a simple extension, based on the hello-world example extension, that uses the `vscode.lm.selectChatModels` API. The `extension.ts` file can look like this:

```
import * as vscode from 'vscode';

export function activate(context: vscode.ExtensionContext) {

	const disposable = vscode.commands.registerCommand('extension.helloWorld', async () => {
		await vscode.lm.selectChatModels({ vendor: 'copilot' });
	});

	context.subscriptions.push(disposable);
}
```
2. Set the extension kind for `github.copilot-chat` to "ui"
3. Connect through Remote-SSH to a machine and install the dummy extension on that machine 
4. Run the command that triggers the selectChatModels call

Result:

The `Extension Host (Remote)` console is flooded with warnings like these:
```
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gpt-4.1' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/oswe-vscode-prime' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gpt-5.2' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/auto' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/copilot-utility-small' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/copilot-utility' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gemini-3.1-pro-preview' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gemini-3.5-flash' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
```
As you can see, these are fired non-stop (thousands each second), and the resource usage of the local machine spikes.

If I remove "github.copilot-chat": "ui" from the value of the remote.extensionKind setting, these warnings are no longer triggered.

title: Custom endpoint language models display id instead of name in model picker
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes `customendpoint` language models display `id` instead of `name`, causing duplicate-looking models in picker and Manage Language Models I tested the BYOK language model changes introduced in VS Code v1.122, especially around local/custom models. I configured my `chatLanguageModels.json` according to the documentation here: https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/customization/language-models#_add-a-custom-endpoint-model For models configured with: ```json "vendor": "customendpoint" ``` the displayed model name appears to be taken from the model `id` instead of the `name` field. **This also occurs other vendors.** As a result, I cannot add the same underlying model twice with the same `id` but different `name` values and different configuration parameters, because both entries are shown with the same visible name. This causes the models to look duplicated/colliding in both the model picker and the **Chat: Manage Language Models** UI. ## Repro Steps 1. Open VS Code v1.122.0. 2. Configure `chatLanguageModels.json` with a `customendpoint` vendor. 3. Add two models with the same `id` but different `name` values. 4. Open the model picker or run **Chat: Manage Language Models**. 5. Observe the displayed model names. Example configuration: ```json [ { "name": "example", "vendor": "customendpoint", "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.example}", "apiType": "chat-completions", "models": [ { "id": "Qwen3.6-27B", "name": "Qwen3.6-27B", "url": "https://example.com/v1", "toolCalling": true, "vision": true, "maxInputTokens": 229376, "maxOutputTokens": 32768, "thinking": false, "streaming": true, "zeroDataRetentionEnabled": false, "reasoningEffortFormat": "chat-completions" }, { "id": "Qwen3.6-27B", "name": "Qwen3.6-27B-thinking", "url": "https://example.com/v1", "toolCalling": true, "vision": true, "maxInputTokens": 180224, "maxOutputTokens": 81920, "thinking": true, "streaming": true, "zeroDataRetentionEnabled": false, "reasoningEffortFormat": "chat-completions" } ] } ] ``` ## Actual Behavior Both `customendpoint` models are displayed using the `id` value: ```text Qwen3.6-27B Qwen3.6-27B ``` This happens in: - Model picker - **Chat: Manage Language Models** Because of this, two entries with the same `id` and different `name` values are indistinguishable in the UI. ## Expected Behavior The visible model name should use the `name` field, as it does for other vendors. For the example above, the UI should show: ```text Qwen3.6-27B Qwen3.6-27B-thinking ``` This would allow users to register the same underlying model more than once with different parameters, such as different context size, output token limits, thinking mode, or other endpoint-specific options. ## Additional Notes This seems all vendor behave same like `vendor: "customendpoint"`. Other vendor configurations don't appear to respect the `name` field for the visible model name. ## VS Code Version ```text Version: 1.122.0 (system setup) Commit: 6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724 Date: 2026-05-27T14:03:01-07:00 Electron: 39.8.8 ElectronBuildId: 13870025 Chromium: 142.0.7444.265 Node.js: 22.22.1 V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0 OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.22631 ```

title: The "Loaded Scripts" debug view is confusingly named for compiled languages
body:
The debug view named "Loaded Scripts" is misleading outside scripting-language debug environments.

In compiled-language debugging (e.g. C or C++), the view commonly shows source files discovered via debug information rather than runtime ŌĆ£scriptsŌĆØ. The current name feels specific to scripting languages, and is confusing to some users who are new to the interface.

The name is also inconsistent with the DAP terminology, which uses "Loaded Sources" e.g. https://microsoft.github.io/debug-adapter-protocol//specification.html#Requests_LoadedSources

Personally I think a transition to just rename it "Loaded Sources" would be easy and accurate, but more complex (dynamic) solutions would also be possible.

title: Performance Issues Large Find-Replace
body:

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Currently not possible to test

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
````
Version: 1.121.0 (user setup)
Commit: f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282
Date: 2026-05-19T11:51:53+02:00
Electron: 39.8.8
ElectronBuildId: 13870025
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200Steps to Reproduce:
````

1. Take a large C++ project with clangd as LSP, running on a remote linux machine. (Probably also possible in other setups)
2. Have only a single source file open in the editor
3. Do a Find-Replace of a commonly used string, in my case +/- 4000 files to be touched
4. Do a replace all

The result:
1. The VS Code UI becomes very unresponsive
2. VS Code Sends the +/- 4000 find-replaces to the remote machine to adjust
3. On the remote machine, the 'server/node --dns-result-order... --type=extensionHost ... process takes 100-200% for a very long time
4. Once all +/- 4000 find-replaces are applied, VS Code starts sending format (on save?) requests to the remote LSP
5. Clangd now has +/- 1000 extra threads in its process
6. The format requests result in many requests, having clangd process them, raising to +/- 7500 threads and +/- 4000% CPU
7. The remote server/node process is still at 100-200%
8. Once all formats are processed by clangd, it falls back to 0% CPU
9. The remote server/node process is still at 100-200%
10. Messages are being sent to remote like `workspace/semanticTokens/refresh`, `textDocument/publishDiagnostics`, `textDocument/inactiveRegions` ... for the files that received the find-replace
11. The remote server/node process is still at 100-200%
12. Wait a long time
13. VS Code sends 'textDocument/didClose' for all the files it tries to update
11. The remote server/node process is now at 80-100%
14. Clangd gradually reduces its threads back to the original number of threads, while again using a lot CPU
15. SSH Host in VS Code tries to reconnect to the remote machine

Arguments can be made that clangd is doing some strange things here with its thread management, though from what I can see, it is perfectly able to handle the incoming messages and sending back responses. The bottleneck seems to be in the VS Code processes.

Looking at the flow, I believe the primary problem starts with how VS Code handles it's find-replace, namely: It first opens all files and replaces the content, than it formats all files and finally is closes all files. Which is perfectly fine when updating 50 files, though it's problematic when it's working with thousands of files. For such numbers, it would be much more logical if VS Code would be limiting the number of 'open files' for it's find-replace, trying to do 'open' > 'update' > 'format' > 'save' > 'close' as fast as possible for every file, such that it can follow the stream of messages while keeping the number of open files in the LSP process low.

title: Regression in VS Code 1.122.0: Remote Extension Host WebSocket repeatedly disconnects in WSL, breaking all AI extensions (Copilot, Kilo, etc.)
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
### Description

After updating to VS Code 1.122.0 (commit `6a49527b96`), the Remote Extension Host over WSL keeps disconnecting every ~20 seconds due to socket timeouts. This causes the GitHub Copilot Chat extension (and any other extension running in the remote extension host) to fail initializing ŌĆö it gets stuck at `Waiting for copilot token or BYOK model to activate conversation feature` indefinitely.

Downgrading to VS Code 1.121.0 resolves the issue immediately.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code 1.122.0 (Windows, user setup)
2. Install WSL extension (`ms-vscode-remote.remote-wsl`)
3. Connect to any WSL distribution (tested on Arch Linux via WSL2)
4. Open any folder (even an empty `/tmp/test` folder)
5. Open GitHub Copilot Chat ŌåÆ observe "Chat took too long to get ready" error
6. Check `Output ŌåÆ GitHub Copilot Chat` log: stuck at `ConversationFeature: Waiting for copilot token or BYOK model to activate conversation feature`

### Expected Behavior

Copilot should initialize and acquire a token within ~60 seconds (as it does in 1.121.0), and the remote extension host should maintain a stable WebSocket connection.

### Actual Behavior

**Developer Tools Console** shows the remote extension host repeatedly timing out and reconnecting:

```
INFO Extension host (Remote) is unresponsive.
INFO [remote-connection][ExtensionHost][ŌĆ”][reconnect] received socket timeout event
     (reason: unacknowledgedMessage, unacknowledgedMsgCount: 432,
      timeSinceOldestUnacknowledgedMsg: 20009)
INFO [remote-connection][ExtensionHost][ŌĆ”][reconnect] starting reconnecting loop.
INFO [remote-connection][ExtensionHost][ŌĆ”][reconnect] reconnected!
// ... 20 seconds later, same cycle repeats indefinitely
```

The `unacknowledgedMsgCount` keeps accumulating (432 ŌåÆ 470 ŌåÆ 517 ŌåÆ 519 ŌåÆ 556 ŌåÆ 566 ŌåÆ 567), indicating messages queued up on the extension host side are never delivered to the renderer.

**Copilot Chat output log** shows only:
```
[info] ConversationFeature: Waiting for copilot token or BYOK model to activate conversation feature
[info] Can't use the Electron fetcher in this environment.
[info] Using the Node fetch fetcher.
```
No `Got Copilot token` ever appears ŌĆö the authentication flow is stuck waiting for the extension host to stabilize.

Developer Tools also shows this error from the built-in Copilot extension:
```
ERR navigator is now a global in nodejs, please see https://aka.ms/vscode-extensions/navigator
```

### Scope

This is **not limited to Copilot** ŌĆö any extension that requires a stable remote extension host connection is affected. For example, the Kilo AI extension also hangs at "Connecting to server...".

### Evidence that this is a VS Code 1.122.0 regression

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| VS Code 1.122.0 ŌåÆ WSL extension ŌåÆ local WSL | **FAILS** |
| VS Code 1.121.0 ŌåÆ WSL extension ŌåÆ local WSL | **WORKS** (immediately after downgrade) |
| VS Code 1.122.0 ŌåÆ Remote SSH ŌåÆ another machine's WSL | **WORKS** |

The fact that Remote SSH to another machine works proves the VS Code server binary and the WSL environment themselves are functional. The bug is specifically in the WSL extension's WebSocket communication layer introduced in 1.122.0.

### What was already ruled out

- ŌØī Network connectivity ŌĆö curl and Node.js `fetch()` can reach `api.github.com`, `api.business.githubcopilot.com`, and `copilot-proxy.githubusercontent.com` with HTTP 200
- ŌØī Proxy environment variables ŌĆö tested with and without `http_proxy`; no difference
- ŌØī WSL Mirrored networking mode ŌĆö already enabled; disabling/enabling has no effect
- ŌØī Corrupted `~/.vscode-server` ŌĆö full removal and clean reinstall does not help
- ŌØī DNS resolution ŌĆö all GitHub/Copilot domains resolve correctly
- ŌØī Node.js version ŌĆö Node.js v26.2.0 on both local and remote WSL
- ŌØī Copilot extension version ŌĆö Copilot is built-in (bundled with VS Code core)
- ŌØī BYOK models ŌĆö also stuck, confirming the failure is at the extension host level, not the token acquisition path
- ŌØī Extension conflict ŌĆö tested with all other extensions disabled

### Environment

```
VS Code version: Code 1.122.0 (6a49527b96, 2026-05-27)
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
Remote: WSL: Arch Linux (WSL2)
WSL extension version: ms-vscode-remote.remote-wsl@0.104.3
Remote OS: Linux x86_64
```

### Workaround

Downgrade to VS Code 1.121.0 and disable automatic updates:

```json
"update.mode": "none",
"update.enableWindowsBackgroundUpdates": false
```

title: File view still thinks it is on unknown although a folder is selected for the session
Terminal local file links are mis-tokenized around CJK punctuation
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.121.0 (All Versions)
- OS Version: Windows 11 (All Platforms)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a folder in VS Code and create a file:

   ```sh
   mkdir -p src
   touch src/main.ts
   touch src/utils.ts
   touch src/view.ts
   touch src/a.ts
   touch src/b.ts
   touch 'ŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docx'
   ```

2. In the integrated terminal, print localized output that includes paths next to CJK sentence/separator punctuation:

   ```sh
   printf '%s\n' \
     'ASCII baseline: file: src/main.ts' \
     'CJK fullwidth colon before path: Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ćµ¢ćõ╗Č’╝Üsrc/main.tsŃĆé' \
     'CJK comma/full stop: ÕĢÅķĪīÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝īÕ╗║ĶŁ░µ¬óµ¤źŃĆé' \
     'CJK list separator: ķ¢óķĆŻŃāĢŃéĪŃéżŃā½’╝Üsrc/a.tsŃĆüsrc/b.tsŃĆé' \
     'CJK exclamation/question: Ķ»ĘµŻĆµ¤ź’╝Üsrc/view.ts’╝üµś»ÕÉ”ÕŁśÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝¤' \
     'CJK punctuation in filename: Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ć’╝ÜŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docxŃĆé'
   ```

3. Hold Ctrl/Cmd and hover or click the file path portions in each line.

Actual:

- The ASCII baseline, `file: src/main.ts`, is detected as a local file link.
- Paths next to CJK sentence/separator punctuation appear to be mis-tokenized as candidates that include surrounding text or punctuation, causing local file validation to fail:
  - `src/main.ts` is effectively tokenized as `Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ćµ¢ćõ╗Č’╝Üsrc/main.tsŃĆé`
  - `src/utils.ts` is effectively tokenized as `ÕĢÅķĪīÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝īÕ╗║ĶŁ░µ¬óµ¤źŃĆé`
  - `src/a.ts` and `src/b.ts` should be two separate links, but the text is effectively tokenized as `ķ¢óķĆŻŃāĢŃéĪŃéżŃā½’╝Üsrc/a.tsŃĆüsrc/b.tsŃĆé`
  - `src/view.ts` and `src/utils.ts` should be two separate links, but the text is effectively tokenized as `Ķ»ĘµŻĆµ¤ź’╝Üsrc/view.ts’╝üµś»ÕÉ”ÕŁśÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝¤`
  - `ŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docx` is effectively tokenized as `Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ć’╝ÜŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docxŃĆé`

Expected:

- The local file link text should be the path itself, without surrounding natural-language text or CJK sentence/separator punctuation:
  - `Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ćµ¢ćõ╗Č’╝Üsrc/main.tsŃĆé` should link `src/main.ts`
  - `ÕĢÅķĪīÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝īÕ╗║ĶŁ░µ¬óµ¤źŃĆé` should link `src/utils.ts`
  - `ķ¢óķĆŻŃāĢŃéĪŃéżŃā½’╝Üsrc/a.tsŃĆüsrc/b.tsŃĆé` should produce two links: `src/a.ts` and `src/b.ts`
  - `Ķ»ĘµŻĆµ¤ź’╝Üsrc/view.ts’╝üµś»ÕÉ”ÕŁśÕ£©’╝Üsrc/utils.ts’╝¤` should produce two links: `src/view.ts` and `src/utils.ts`
- Bracket-like CJK punctuation such as `ŃĆŖŃĆŗ` is more likely to be part of an actual file name, so it should be preserved when it is inside the path. This is different from CJK sentence/separator punctuation, which appears around or between paths in these examples.
  - `Ķ»ĘµēōÕ╝Ć’╝ÜŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docxŃĆé` should link `ŃĆŖĶ«║µ¢ćŃĆŗ.docx`

Local file links should still work when paths are surrounded by common CJK sentence/separator punctuation.

These are common shapes in localized CJK terminal output where punctuation is used as a label separator, trailing sentence punctuation, or list separator. This is also common **in natural language output from tools and AI coding assistants**.

Notes:

- I could not find a setting that works around this. `terminal.integrated.wordSeparators` is documented and wired up for double-click word selection and fallback word/search link detection, while terminal local file links are parsed separately by the local file link parser.
- This is specific to terminal local file link parsing. URL link detection does not have the same issue with these CJK punctuation boundaries, and already has explicit handling for several CJK punctuation characters as link terminators. Local file link parsing appears to have a smaller hardcoded delimiter set.
- Terminal local file link detection is already heuristic and optimizes for likely clickable paths rather than all syntactically valid file names. ASCII punctuation such as `:` and `;` is already treated as separators even though some of these characters may be valid in file names on some platforms.
- A reasonable scope would be CJK sentence/separator punctuation such as `’╝ī` / `ŃĆé` / `’╝Ü` / `’╝ø` / `’╝ü` / `’╝¤` / `ŃĆü`, which commonly appears around or between paths in localized and natural-language terminal output. Bracket-like CJK punctuation such as `ŃĆÉŃĆæ`, `ŃĆŖŃĆŗ`, `ŃĆīŃĆŹ`, and `’╝ł’╝ē` is more likely to be part of actual file or directory names, so it should be preserved when it appears inside the path rather than treated as the same kind of delimiter.

**­¤Öŗ I can work on a PR for this if the proposed scope sounds reasonable.**

title: Allow GitHub Copilot in VS Code to configure third-party / custom model providers
body:
### Summary

Please add first-class support in GitHub Copilot for VS Code for configuring third-party and custom model providers, including OpenAI-compatible endpoints, local models, and enterprise/private model gateways.

GitHub Copilot in VS Code already supports an increasingly agentic workflow and has started integrating third-party agents such as Claude Agent and OpenAI Codex. However, users still cannot freely configure their own model providers or custom API endpoints in the Copilot experience.

This feature would make Copilot for VS Code more flexible, enterprise-friendly, and competitive with tools such as Cline, Continue, Kilo Code, OpenCode, Cursor, and other AI coding assistants that support bring-your-own-model workflows.

---

### Motivation

Many developers and organizations now use multiple LLM providers depending on cost, latency, compliance, region, privacy, and task type.

Common use cases include:

- Using a company-approved internal LLM gateway.
- Using an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Using local models through Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or similar tools.
- Using enterprise model platforms such as Azure AI, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or private deployments.
- Using different models for different tasks:
  - lightweight models for quick edits;
  - stronger reasoning models for agent mode;
  - local/private models for sensitive code;
  - cheaper models for high-volume usage.

VS Code already documents support for third-party agents such as Claude Agent and OpenAI Codex in Copilot-powered workflows, which shows that the product is moving toward a more open and agent-based ecosystem.

Reference: [Third-party agents in Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/agents/third-party-agents){target="_blank" class="gpt-web-url"}

GitHub Copilot also advertises support for Claude and Codex on GitHub and VS Code.

Reference: [GitHub Copilot ┬Ę Your AI pair programmer](https://github.com/features/copilot){target="_blank" class="gpt-web-url"}

At the same time, the broader VS Code AI ecosystem already has strong demand for bring-your-own-model workflows. For example, Cline is often highlighted because users can bring their own models directly inside standard VS Code.

Reference: [GitHub Copilot ŌĆö tip 1 ŌĆö the agents have arrived! - Medium](https://medium.com/@svdoever/github-copilot-tip-1-the-agents-have-arrived-0394fbf6c132){target="_blank" class="gpt-web-url"}

There are also recent discussions and articles showing that VS Code is moving toward supporting local AI/LLM workflows without relying entirely on third-party software.

Reference: [Using local AI/LLM in VS Code without third party software, on the ...](https://peterfalkingham.com/2025/10/27/using-local-ai-llm-in-vs-code-without-third-party-software/){target="_blank" class="gpt-web-url"}

---

### Proposed Solution

Add a new "Custom Model Providers" section in GitHub Copilot for VS Code settings.

Example settings:

```json
{
  "github.copilot.customModels.enabled": true,
  "github.copilot.customModels.providers": [
    {
      "name": "Local Ollama",
      "type": "openai-compatible",
      "baseUrl": "http://localhost:11434/v1",
      "apiKey": "",
      "models": ["qwen2.5-coder:32b", "deepseek-coder-v2"]
    },
    {
      "name": "Company LLM Gateway",
      "type": "openai-compatible",
      "baseUrl": "https://llm-gateway.company.com/v1",
      "apiKey": "${env:COMPANY_LLM_API_KEY}",
      "models": ["company-coder-large", "company-reasoning-large"]
    },
    {
      "name": "OpenRouter",
      "type": "openai-compatible",
      "baseUrl": "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
      "apiKey": "${env:OPENROUTER_API_KEY}",
      "models": ["anthropic/claude-sonnet", "openai/gpt-4.1", "google/gemini-pro"]
    }
  ],
  "github.copilot.customModels.defaultChatModel": "company-coder-large",
  "github.copilot.customModels.defaultAgentModel": "company-reasoning-large",
  "github.copilot.customModels.defaultInlineCompletionModel": "qwen2.5-coder:32b"
}

title: chat.instructionsFilesLocations: applyTo frontmatter changes not reflected until VS Code restart (stale cache)
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
Confirmed nothing matching

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.50.0
- VS Code Version: 1.122.0 (Universal)
- OS Version: Mac OS Tahoe 26.0.1 (25A362)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6 - High
- Logs: See chat session below

# Steps to Reproduce:

1. Add to settings.json
```json
    "chat.instructionsFilesLocations": {
        "~/.config/ai/instructions": true
    },
    "chat.promptFilesLocations": {
        "~/.config/ai/commands-prompts": true
    },
```

2. Create ~/.config/ai/instructions/company.instructions.md with this frontmatter:
```yaml
---
name: myco
description: Helps agents understand the company Auction Edge which users work for. 
applyTo: "**"
---
```

3. Create file ~/.config/ai/commands-prompts/context.prompt.md with this content:
What instructions files or custom context are currently applied to this conversation? List the file paths.

4. Start a new session and type `/context`. Expect something like this response:

Based on the current conversation context, the following instruction files and custom context are applied:

Instruction Files:

[company.instructions.md] ŌĆö applied via applyTo: "**" (all files)


5. Edit ~/.config/ai/instructions/company.instructions.md and change applyTo to this:

applyTo: "**/*.{sql,ipynb,sh,bash}"

6. Start a new session and type `/context`

# Expected Result

Instruction Files:

[company.instructions.md] ŌĆö applyTo: "**/*.{sql,ipynb,sh,bash}" (SQL, notebooks, shell, and bash files only)

# Actual Result

Instruction Files:

[company.instructions.md] ŌĆö applied via applyTo: "**" (all files)

# Workaround
Restart VS Code. The correct applyTo then displays

## GitHub Tags
#A11yTCS;#Visual Studio CodeŌĆ»Client; #E2E_VisualStudioCodeClient_Win32_Dec2025;#DesktopApp;#FTP;#Win32;#A11ySev3; #A11yMAS; #WCAG2.4.3;ŌĆ»#Keyboard;

## Issue Subcategory 
Focus is incorrect or lost after dynamic interaction 

## User Experience 
Keyboard users lose their place after closing a popup, deleting a list item, or completing an inline action. 

## Repro Steps 
Launch Visual Studio Code > Extension > Search for Microsoft sentinel extension >Auto Update checkbox > Observe issue: Focus is lost after interacting with "Auto Update" checkbox using keyboard.


## Actual Result 
After activating the Auto Update checkbox, focus moves away from the control and becomes not visible. Users lose track of current focus location. Pressing Tab moves focus to the next control.

## Expected Result 
Focus should remain on the Auto Update checkbox after it is checked/unchecked, and the focus indicator should remain clearly visible.

## How to Fix 
Manage focus programmatically: return it to the trigger element after dialog close, or to the next sibling item after deletion. Never let focus reset to <body> or to some illogical element after dynamic content changes.

title: Feature request: Fall back to a Node-side verifier when bundled `vsce-sign` can't launch
body:
`vsce-sign` (the bundled .NET binary used to verify extension signatures) fails to start on a growing list of environments, each with a different proximate cause but the same user-visible outcome:

- **Linux with restricted virtual memory**: GC heap init mmap fails. See #249427.
- **macOS with `amfi_get_out_of_my_way=1` in boot-args**: `CorHost2::Start()` returns `0x8007000C` after `EEStartupHelper` succeeds. Traced with lldb on a 1.119.0 install. `mmap(MAP_JIT)` itself works on this system, so it isn't the JIT entitlement ŌĆö something else in EE startup silently depends on a `cs_flags` bit AMFI normally grants. See #270269.
- (Probably others: Alpine without libicu, sandboxed containers, etc.)

In all cases, install + auto-update returns `UnknownError`, internal code 137, and prompt "Install Anyway (Don't Verify Signature)" on every operation. Verification is effectively off, but with a prompt each time instead of a clean opt-out.

`extensionSignatureVerificationService.ts` and `extensionDownloader.ts` currently treat "ran and reported bad signature" the same as "couldn't run at all" ŌĆö both surface as `UnknownError`. Those are different things, and the second category can be handled in-process without `vsce-sign`.

There are two ways that I think this could be addressed, if by some miracle this request gets due consideration:

1. **Built-in Node fallback verifier.** When `child_process.execFile` returns a non-numeric `code` (i.e. the binary didn't actually run), verify the detached CMS signature with Node's built-in `crypto` against the marketplace cert chain instead of returning `UnknownError`. `@vscode/vsce-sign` stays the source of truth on healthy systems; verification works on the rest instead of being permanently broken.
2. **`extensions.signatureVerifierPath` setting.** Lets a user point at an alternate verifier conforming to the same exit-code contract.

I'm happy to put a PR up myself for either of these, if the team decides to move forward with one of them, or something else

I could just disable verification entirely, but I don't want to put myself at greater risk than is strictly necessary for my setup

title: Remove agent distractions in zen mode
body: In Zen mode, the focus is all on the text, and meant to provide minimal distractions. However, the agent chat indicators are still available in chat mode, providing notifications and changing color as agents execute. Request: hide agent-related features in Zen mode, seen in screenshot.

title: Agents window with Remote SSH fails after session materialization: Copilot CLI session is not authenticated
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No, requires Remote SSH, GitHub Copilot Chat, etc - VS Code Version: 1.122.0 - OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 (and the remote is Amazon Linux 2023) ## Summary When I use the Agents window with a Remote SSH host, the SSH connection and remote Agent Host appear to start successfully, and a provisional Copilot CLI session is created and materialized. However, the first message fails with a Copilot authentication error from the Agents Host output: ``` Session error: authentication - Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider ``` Regular GitHub Copilot Chat works in VS Code, and Copilot CLI also works from the integrated terminal on the same remote host. The failure appears specific to the Agents window + Remote SSH + Copilot CLI Agent Host path. ## Steps to Reproduce: 1. Open the Agents window. 2. Select a Remote SSH host from the SSH config. 3. Select a folder on the remote host. 4. Start a new Copilot CLI session. 5. Send an arbitrary message, for example `Hello?`. ### Expected Result The message should be sent to the remote Copilot CLI session, and the Agents window should show the assistant response. ### Actual Result The session fails immediately after materialization. In VS Code 1.121.0, the authentication error was shown as a response message in the Agents window. In VS Code 1.122.0, the error is no longer shown in the session UI; instead, the session panel returns to the new-session panel immediately. The underlying error can be seen in the Agents Host output channel. The screenshot shows that the Agents window is connected to a Remote SSH workspace, Copilot CLI is selected, the remote file tree is visible, and the Agent Host terminal starts successfully on the remote host. ## Relevant Agents Host Output I redacted the remote host name and repository path. ``` [2026-05-28T05:01:02.195Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Listing sessions... Please authenticate first. at handleResponse (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:565:48) at handleMessage (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:345:13) at processMessageQueue (/home//.vscode-server/cli/servers/Stable-/server/node_modules/vscode-jsonrpc/lib/common/connection.js:362:17) at Immediate. [2026-05-28T05:01:54.965Z] [INFO ] [Copilot] Session materialized: copilotcli:/5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047 [2026-05-28T05:01:54.969Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] sendMessage: cachedEntry=true, hasActiveClient=true, activeClientId=(set) [2026-05-28T05:01:54.970Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] sendMessage called: "Hello?" (0 attachments) [2026-05-28T05:01:54.982Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] rpc.mode.set succeeded: mode=interactive [2026-05-28T05:01:54.991Z] [INFO ] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] session.send() returned [2026-05-28T05:01:54.991Z] [ERROR] [Copilot:5525e056-a87d-4598-8b64-be76dffd7047] Session error: authentication - Execution failed: Error: Session was not created with authentication info or custom provider ``` ## Additional Notes - GitHub Copilot Chat works normally in the regular VS Code window. - Copilot CLI also works when I run it manually in the integrated terminal on the same remote host. - The remote Agent Host terminal is created successfully, so the Remote SSH connection itself appears to be working. - The log suggests that VS Code receives or updates a Copilot auth token, starts `@github/copilot/index.js` with token-related context, and materializes the provisional session, but the Copilot CLI/SDK backend still treats the session as unauthenticated. - This looks different from #316223, where selecting the SSH host did not proceed. In this case, the connection proceeds further and fails after session materialization. - It may be related to #317297 because both involve Agents window + Remote SSH + Copilot CLI routing, but in this case the request appears to reach the remote Agent Host and fails as an unauthenticated Copilot session rather than being silently dropped before reaching the remote CLI.

title: [Regression 1.122.0] Bundled ripgrep binary path changed, breaking extensions that resolve rg via vscode.env.appRoot (Todo-Tree, Roo Code/Zoo Code)
body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.122.0 (6a49527b96e326fe62fbdb56f60e16877c9aa724, x64, user setup)
- OS Version: Windows 11 x64

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Update VS Code to 1.122.0
2. Open any extension that resolves VSCode's bundled ripgrep via `vscode.env.appRoot`
   (e.g. Todo-Tree, Roo Code / Zoo Code codebase indexing)
3. Extension fails with: `Failed to find vscode-ripgrep` / `Could not find ripgrep binary`

**Root cause:** VSCode 1.122.0 changed the internal directory structure ŌĆö the bundled `rg.exe`
is now located at:
`resources/app/<commitHash>/resources/app/node_modules/@vscode/ripgrep-universal/bin/win32-x64/rg.exe`

Previous versions placed it at:
`resources/app/node_modules/@vscode/ripgrep/bin/win32-x64/rg.exe`

Extensions resolving ripgrep via `vscode.env.appRoot` + standard relative paths no longer
find the binary. System-installed ripgrep (`rg` in PATH) is also not used as fallback.

**Confirmed affected extensions:** Todo-Tree, Zoo Code (Roo Code fork) codebase indexing
**Workaround:** None fully working. Installing ripgrep system-wide via Chocolatey
(`choco install ripgrep`) does not fix extensions that rely on the bundled binary path.

title: Add support for local-only tracked changes in Source Control
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

### **Problem**

Users often maintain local modifications in tracked files that should never be committed, such as:
- local environment configuration
- temporary debug changes
- developer-specific feature flags
- machine-specific adjustments

Current Git solutions like skip-worktree and assume-unchanged are difficult to discover and manage, especially from the VS Code Source Control UI.

Additionally, using .gitignore is not appropriate for tracked files and may introduce repository-wide side effects.

### **Proposed Solution**

Introduce a new SCM resource group named _Local Only_.

Files added to this group would:
- remain modified locally
- be excluded from commits/staging
- avoid repository changes
- avoid modifying .gitignore
- persist only locally for the current workspace/user

This would provide a safer and more discoverable workflow for managing local-only tracked changes directly from the Source Control UI.

### **Example Use Cases**
- local API endpoints
- debug flags
- temporary instrumentation
- developer-specific workspace adjustments
- locally customized settings files
- Related Discussions

### **Related to:**

#313007
#121635

This proposal approaches the problem from a Source Control workflow and UX perspective rather than exposing raw Git internals directly.

### **Additional Context**

I already implemented a working prototype locally, including:
- a dedicated SCM group
- staging prevention
- local persistence behavior
- integration into the Source Control view

title: Show active model immediately after submitting a prompt
body:
### Problem Statement
Currently, when asking GitHub Copilot a question in VS Code, the specific model name and token billing info are only displayed at the very end of the response *after* it finishes generating. 

Occasionally, the model dropdown selection gets reset or switched unexpectedly. When the AI is thinking/generating a long response, I am unable to verify which model is currently running. If it happens to be a model I didn't intend to use, I have to waste time waiting for it to finish just to check.

### Proposed Solution
Please display the active model information (e.g., "Using GPT-5.5...") **immediately** at the beginning or top of the response block as soon as the prompt is submitted, rather than waiting for the entire generation to complete.

title: PR #311844 broke service-worker version check in extension webview
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- ­¤Ģ« Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- ­¤öÄ Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- ­¤¦¬ Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- ­¤ÆĪ Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- ­¤ö¦ Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- ­¤¬ō If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- ­¤ōŻ Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.121
- OS Version: Ubuntu 24.04 ARM64

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Install vscode 1.121, run `code --serve-web`
2. Extension like `lean 4` cannot load its resource files properly

PR #311844 bumped `_expectedServiceWorkerVersion` from 4 to 5 in src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/webviewElement.ts.

However, the relevant change in src/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/pre/service-worker.js (which contains the updated version 5 service worker) is NOT reflected in product.json: [code](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/0ee1ce21073e02290d3b9b6ef333a9279bfec0b2/product.json#L37). product.json still points to an old commit from Aug 10, 2022: [ef65ac1ba57f57f2a3961bfe94aa20481caca4c6](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/commit/ef65ac1ba57f57f2a3961bfe94aa20481caca4c6).

When using code server, extensions are still loading service worker built from that commit, so loaded service worker is still version 4, but we already expect version 5. So the service worker cannot be loaded successfully, and extensions cannot load their resource files properly.

@mjbvz Could you take a look at this? I think the service worker js needs to be rebuilt and pushed to CDN, then product.json needs to be updated to point to the updated build. Thanks!

title: Add maximize button for the code browser window and the terminal window, too
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Current we can see there is a "maximize" button on the copilot chat window, that is great, because developers are working on that window with more and more long time. That button is very convenient for me to switch between the chat window and vs code IDE other parts. However, it is still not good enough, you guys need to add such a "maximize" button (or some fast key) for both the  code browser window (they are tab pages) and the terminal window   (it also tabbed with other windows like output/debug) as well. The max advantage of VS code IDE + copliot chat for me, is that it allows me to switch from AI session to my code/console in one window very smoothly. And adding such buttons definitely will improve my experience when using the tools together.

title: Chronicle search: cloud queries timeout with documented 90-day default window
body:
## Problem

The Chronicle skill's Search workflow documents a **90-day default window** for `turns` table queries on the cloud (DuckDB) backend. In practice, even a 30-day window with simple `ILIKE` patterns causes `context deadline exceeded` errors consistently.

## Observed Failures

### 1. CTE with UNION ALL across multiple tables (90-day window) ŌåÆ timeout

```sql
WITH hits AS (
  SELECT session_id, ... FROM turns
  WHERE timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL '90 days'
    AND (user_message ILIKE '%keyword%' OR ...)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT id AS session_id, ... FROM sessions WHERE ...
  UNION ALL
  SELECT session_id, ... FROM checkpoints WHERE ...
)
SELECT ... FROM hits JOIN sessions ...
```
**Result**: `context deadline exceeded`

### 2. Simplified query (30-day window, fewer tables) ŌåÆ still timeout

Dropping checkpoints and reducing to 30 days with a single ILIKE still exceeds the query budget.

### 3. What works: 7-day window on `turns` alone

```sql
SELECT session_id, substr(user_message, 1, 160) AS snippet
FROM turns
WHERE timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL '7 days'
  AND user_message ILIKE '%keyword%'
LIMIT 30
```
Then enriching with a second query:
```sql
SELECT id, repository, branch, summary, updated_at
FROM sessions WHERE id IN ('...', '...')
```

## Recommendations

1. **Change the documented default window** from 90 days to **7 days** for `turns` searches. Widen progressively (14d ŌåÆ 30d ŌåÆ 90d) only if no results are returned.
2. **Recommend the two-step pattern** as the primary approach: collect `session_id` list first (narrow window), then enrich from `sessions` using `WHERE id IN (...)`.
3. **Avoid UNION ALL across heavy tables** in a single CTE ŌĆö the combined scan cost exceeds the deadline even at 30 days.
4. **`sessions` table search is always fast** (small table, no full-text scan needed) but often yields no results because `summary` is frequently NULL.

## Additional Notes

- The `sessions` table PK is `id` (not `session_id`) ŌĆö the skill documents this correctly but the natural pattern of writing `SELECT session_id` in a UNION with child tables leads to errors. The skill could add a code example showing the `id AS session_id` alias pattern.
- Multiple OR'd ILIKE conditions compound the cost ŌĆö prefer a single broad ILIKE over multiple narrow ones when possible.

title: UBB Usage Telemetry
body: I am signed in with a CB account with no ULB set but if I look at my telemetry output for `chatinstallentitelment` `"measurements":{"entitlement":8,"quotaChat":100,"quotaChatUnlimited":1,"quotaChatHasQuota":0,"quotaChatEntitlement":0,"quotaPremiumChat":100,"quotaPremiumChatUnlimited":1,"quotaPremiumChatHasQuota":1,"quotaPremiumChatEntitlement":0,"quotaCompletions":100,"quotaCompletionsUnlimited":1,"quotaCompletionsHasQuota":0,"quotaCompletionsEntitlement":0,"usageBasedBilling":1,"additionalUsageEnabled":1,"additionalUsageCount":0,"canUpgradePlan":0,"common.msftInternal":1,"common.timesincesessionstart":9739,"common.sequence":1117,"common.cli":0}}` Here is the explanation Copilot gave for each of the measures: - entitlement: Represents the user's entitlement level, which determines their access to specific features or plans (e.g., individual, business, etc.). - quotaChat: The total number of standard chat messages the user is allowed to send within the current period. - quotaChatUnlimited: Indicates whether the user has unlimited standard chat messages (1 = unlimited, 0 = limited). - quotaChatHasQuota: Indicates whether the user currently has any remaining standard chat quota (1 = yes, 0 = no). - quotaChatEntitlement: Represents the entitlement level specifically for standard chat features (often 0 if not differentiated). - quotaPremiumChat: The total number of premium chat messages the user is allowed to send within the current period. - quotaPremiumChatUnlimited: Indicates whether the user has unlimited premium chat messages (1 = unlimited, 0 = limited). - quotaPremiumChatHasQuota: Indicates whether the user currently has any remaining premium chat quota (1 = yes, 0 = no). - quotaPremiumChatEntitlement: Represents the entitlement level specifically for premium chat features (often 0 if not differentiated). - quotaCompletions: The total number of code completions the user is allowed to use within the current period. - quotaCompletionsUnlimited: Indicates whether the user has unlimited code completions (1 = unlimited, 0 = limited). - quotaCompletionsHasQuota: Indicates whether the user currently has any remaining code completions quota (1 = yes, 0 = no). - quotaCompletionsEntitlement: Represents the entitlement level specifically for code completions (often 0 if not differentiated). - usageBasedBilling: Indicates whether the user is on a usage-based billing plan (1 = enabled, 0 = disabled). - additionalUsageEnabled: Indicates whether the user can purchase or use additional quota beyond their current plan (1 = enabled, 0 = disabled). - additionalUsageCount: Tracks the number of additional usage units (e.g., extra messages or completions) the user has consumed. - canUpgradePlan: Indicates whether the user is eligible to upgrade their current plan (1 = eligible, 0 = not eligible). - common.msftInternal: Indicates whether the user is recognized as a Microsoft internal user (1 = yes, 0 = no). Some things I noticed: - It is marking this account as msft internal which it is not connected to the GH/MSFT org. - quotaChat is 100 but then it also says I have unlimited? - Is quotaCompletionsEntitlement needed? Maybe we can cut down on the data sent here as well?

title: Remote-SSH renderer repeatedly crashes (code 5) when workspace root is a large home directory
body:
# Remote-SSH renderer repeatedly crashes (code 5) when workspace root is a large home directory

## Environment

- VSCode: 1.121.0 (Stable, commit `f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282`)
- Electron: 39.8.8
- Remote-SSH extension: 0.123.0
- macOS 26.5
- Remote: Linux x86_64 in a privileged container, kernel 5.x, glibc-based; 503 GB RAM
- Workspace root on remote: the user's `$HOME` (Ōēł5.5 TB on disk, ~277k inodes at depth 6, with one subtree containing ~15 M files)
- Notable extensions enabled in the affected window: `ms-python.python` (with Pylance 2026.2.1 / pyright 1.1.408), `Anthropic.claude-code`, `GitHub.copilot-chat`, `openai.chatgpt`, `GitHub.vscode-pull-request-github`, plus the usual Git / JSON / Python-envs

## Symptom

The Remote-SSH window's renderer process dies repeatedly with:

```
[error] CodeWindow: renderer process gone (reason: crashed, code: 5)
[error] [uncaught exception in main]: Error: Render frame was disposed before WebFrameMain could be accessed
```

Typical lifetime of a freshly opened window: **2 ŌĆō 10 minutes** before the first crash.

Frequency (counted from `~/Library/Application Support/Code/Crashpad/completed/`): **79 minidumps in 15 days**, all attributable to this window.

## What the dumps say

`strings` over the minidumps consistently shows:

```
--type=renderer
v8-oom-location
v8_isolate_address
v8_ro_space_firstpage_address
ThreadPool.UnnecessaryWakeup.Renderer.Foreground
```

This looks like a V8 heap exhaustion in the renderer process, not a system-level kill (no entries in `~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports`, and macOS memory pressure is moderate at crash time).

## What we measured (5-second sampling on both sides)

After killing all `vscode-server` processes and reconnecting:

```
t=0      vscode-server starts on remote
t=~30s   Pylance RSS:        0 ŌåÆ   1.0 GB
t=~35s   Pylance RSS:      1.0 ŌåÆ   4.5 GB   (single 5-second sample jump)
t=~90s   extensionHost RSS:        ~700 MB ŌåÆ 2.5 GB
t=~120s  fileWatcher RSS:           plateaus ~5.9 GB
t=~150s  extensionHost RSS:        5.0 GB
t=~150s  Mac renderer RSS:         starts being observed, ~285 MB
t=~210s  Mac renderer RSS:         1.4 ŌåÆ 3.5 GB
t=~270s  Mac renderer RSS:         5.4 ŌåÆ 6.4 GB
t=~275s  Mac renderer disappears  ŌåÆ entry in main.log: "renderer process gone, code 5"
```

## What we tried that did *not* fix it

### 1. `python.analysis.exclude` for the giant subtree

Set in Machine-scope `settings.json`:

```json
"python.analysis.exclude": ["/home/<user>/<large_dataset_dir>"]
```

The Pylance log (`Python Ķ»ŁĶ©Ćµ£ŹÕŖĪÕÖ©.log`) shows entries like the following **shortly after** the setting was added:

```
[info] FG(<user>): Skipping recursive symlink "file:///home/<user>/<large_dataset_dir>/.../sheep/..."
   -> "file:///home/<user>/<large_dataset_dir>/.../sheep/..."
[info] Found 8491 source files
[error] Enumeration of workspace source files is taking longer than 10 seconds.
```

The "Skipping recursive symlink" lines from inside the excluded directory suggest enumeration **enters** the excluded path before exclusion is applied. The setting either does not gate enumeration, or it gates it only after entering and noticing the symlink.

`pyrightconfig.json` was not attempted in this round.

### 2. Globally disabling Pylance

Set `"python.languageServer": "None"` in Machine-scope settings. Verified at runtime: no `vscode-pylance` process spawns. Reproduced the scenario.

Result:

- Pylance process: gone Ō£ģ
- `extensionHost` RSS grew to **7.8 GB** (higher than with Pylance enabled)
- `fileWatcher` RSS still plateaued at ~5.9 GB
- Mac renderer still died at ~5 GB RSS, ~5 minutes after window open

So disabling Pylance shifts memory pressure but does not stop the renderer crash.

### 3. `files.watcherExclude`

Set in Machine-scope settings:

```json
"files.watcherExclude": {
  "/home/<user>/<large_dataset_dir>/**": true
}
```

`fileWatcher` (parcel) RSS dropped on a fresh start (from ~6 GB to ~400 MB observed immediately after reload). It rebuilt back to ~5.9 GB over time in subsequent runs. The exclude appears to filter event delivery rather than prevent initial crawl/metadata retention.

### 4. inotify limit

The server-side `remoteagent.log` contains:

```
[error] [File Watcher ('parcel')] Inotify limit reached (ENOSPC) (path: /home/<user>)
```

At the moment of this error, an actual count of inotify watch descriptors across the user's processes shows fewer than 800 / 65536. This appears to be a **transient peak during initial enumeration** rather than a sustained breach. Raising `fs.inotify.max_user_watches` was not attempted on the host (per-user-namespace nuances).

## Reproduction (minimal)

1. Use Remote-SSH to a host where the user's `$HOME` contains:
   - several million files in one subtree (e.g., a data-science dataset directory)
   - a few hundred thousand files spread across other subtrees
2. Open `$HOME` directly as the VSCode workspace root.
3. Enable: `ms-python.python` (default Pylance), plus 2ŌĆō3 chat / LLM extensions, plus `GitHub.vscode-pull-request-github`, plus Git.
4. Use the window normally for 2ŌĆō10 minutes.

Expected: window stays alive.
Actual: renderer dies with code 5; main.log records "renderer process gone (reason: crashed, code: 5)" and the subsequent "Render frame was disposed" exception.

## Questions to maintainers

1. The crashpad annotations look like V8 renderer OOM. Is there a recommended way to raise the renderer's V8 heap limit per-window or per-host, beyond global `argv.json` `--max-old-space-size=N`?

2. Is `python.analysis.exclude` expected to prevent enumeration of the listed paths, or only to drop them from later analysis? The log we captured suggests the latter.

3. Is opening a many-million-inode `$HOME` as workspace root considered an unsupported configuration today? If so, is there a way for the workbench to detect it and warn the user explicitly (currently Pylance prints an error to its output channel but the workbench keeps going)?

4. Why does the `fileWatcher` retain GB-range RSS even when the dominant subtree is in `files.watcherExclude`? Is the parcel/watcher metadata cache supposed to honor that setting before the initial walk?

5. The transient `Inotify limit reached (ENOSPC)` despite low steady-state watch counts ŌĆö is there a way to back-pressure the initial enumeration instead of erroring on the spike?

I am happy to attach:
- a representative `.dmp` from `Crashpad/completed/`
- the 5-second sampled RSS time series for both sides
- the full Pylance and remoteagent log files (sanitized)

Just say what shape would be most useful for triage.

title: Allow configuring thinking/reasoning effort for Claude Opus 4.7 in Copilot
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
Please allow users to choose the thinking/reasoning effort for Claude Opus 4.7 in VS Code Copilot Chat / Agent Mode, instead of it being fixed to `medium`.

## Motivation

Copilot is moving to token / AI Credits based billing in June. Under token-based billing, users should be able to choose the reasoning-depth / latency / cost trade-off for expensive frontier models.

Today, Claude Opus 4.7 appears to be fixed at `medium` effort in Copilot. For many tasks I would prefer a lower/fast/no-extra-thinking setting to reduce latency and token cost; for harder tasks I might explicitly opt into more reasoning if the model/backend supports it.

## Requested behavior

- Expose a per-session or per-model setting in Copilot Chat UI/settings for Claude Opus 4.7 thinking/reasoning effort.
- If Opus 4.7 currently only supports `medium` through Copilot, please make that capability/limitation clear in the model picker or docs.
- Ideally provide a lower-cost/fast option for Opus 4.7 so users can control spend once billing is token-based.

## Related issues

- #313546 asks for reasoning effort in custom `.agent.md` files.
- #303790 covers reasoning effort configurability for the Codex integration.

This request is specifically for user-selectable thinking/reasoning effort for Claude Opus 4.7 in VS Code Copilot Chat / Agent Mode, motivated by token-based billing.

8

title: CI: Electron SIGSEGV crash in "Linux / Remote" smoke tests (Data Loss + Multiroot)
body:
## Summary

The **Linux / Remote** smoke test job is failing with an Electron SIGSEGV crash and a follow-on timeout.

Observed in run [#26519275250](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/26519275250/job/78105290056) on PR #318571, but the failures are unrelated to the PR changes ŌĆö they appear to be infrastructure/flakiness issues.

## Failures

### 1. Data Loss ŌĆö `verifies editors can save and restore`

```
electron.launch: WebSocket error: socket hang up
[pid=10974][err] DevTools listening on ws://127.0.0.1:39813/devtools/browser/...
<ws error> socket hang up
<ws connect error> socket hang up
[pid=10974] <process did exit: exitCode=null, signal=SIGSEGV>
```

Electron crashes with **SIGSEGV** before the DevTools WebSocket can be established, so the smoke driver never connects.

### 2. Multiroot ŌĆö `"before all" hook for "shows results from all folders"`

```
Error: Timeout of 120000ms exceeded.
```

This times out in the `before all` hook ŌĆö likely a cascading failure caused by the Electron crash in the preceding test leaving the environment in a broken state.

## Reproduction

- Job: [Linux / Remote #78105290056](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/actions/runs/26519275250/job/78105290056)
- Platform: `linux-x64`, Electron smoke tests (remote mode)
- Relevant log snippet:
  ```
  [pid=10974][err] [0527/150628.039401:ERROR:crashpad/util/file/file_io_posix.cc:145]
      open /sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu0/cpufreq/scaling_cur_freq: No such file or directory
  [pid=10974] <process did exit: exitCode=null, signal=SIGSEGV>
  ```

The `cpufreq` errors suggest the runner lacks `/sys/devices/system/cpu/cpu0/cpufreq/`, which is a known missing sysfs path in some containerized CI environments and can affect Electron's crashpad initialisation.

## Impact

Unrelated PRs are blocked from auto-merging because this flaky job poisons the required checks.

title: Agents window: only show browser "share with agent" when the current session has access to browser tools
Feature Request: Idle Timeout for MCP Servers
body:
## Problem

MCP servers remain running indefinitely after being started, consuming memory even when not actively used. During heavy Copilot agent sessions that trigger multiple MCP servers (Atlassian, GitHub, custom servers), the cumulative memory pressure can cause VS Code renderer crashes, especially on memory-constrained systems.

**Current behavior:**
- MCP servers start when their tools are first invoked (with `chat.mcp.gallery.autoStart: false`)
- Servers keep running until manually stopped via `MCP: List Servers` ŌåÆ Stop
- No automatic cleanup mechanism exists

**Observed impact:**
- Renderer process crashes (exit code 5) during concurrent MCP operations
- Memory spikes when multiple servers initialize simultaneously  
- Users must manually track and stop unused servers

## Proposed Solution

Add an `idleTimeout` configuration option for MCP servers:

```json
{
  "servers": {
    "atlassian": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://mcp.atlassian.com/",
      "idleTimeout": 300000  // Stop after 5 minutes of inactivity
    }
  }
}
```

**Alternative:** A global setting like `chat.mcp.idleTimeout` that applies to all servers.

## Benefits

1. **Automatic memory recovery** ŌĆö Servers shut down after period of no tool invocations
2. **Reduced crash risk** ŌĆö Fewer concurrent processes during memory pressure
3. **Transparent to users** ŌĆö Servers restart automatically when needed again

## Environment

- VS Code Version: 1.121.0
- OS: macOS
- Copilot Chat Version: 0.49.0

title: Improve Azure OpenAI URL Handling in the BYOK Provider
body:
## Issue

Related context: BYOK can now be used in signed-out / air-gapped scenarios after microsoft/vscode#317428, which makes Azure BYOK setup more important for enterprise and sovereign-cloud users.

Azure BYOK URL inference currently recognizes commercial Azure OpenAI base URLs like:

```text
https://<resource>.openai.azure.com
```

and expands them to the legacy deployment-based Chat Completions route:

```text
/openai/deployments/<deployment-id>/chat/completions?api-version=2025-01-01-preview
```

However, Azure OpenAI and Foundry endpoints now appear across multiple Azure host families, including sovereign cloud and Foundry resource hosts such as:

```text
https://<resource>.openai.azure.us
https://<resource>.cognitiveservices.azure.us
https://<resource>.cognitiveservices.azure.com
https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com
```

## Why This Matters

Azure customers now encounter several valid endpoint families:

- Legacy Azure OpenAI deployment routes:
	- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/openai/deployments/<deployment>/chat/completions?api-version=...`
	- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.us/openai/deployments/<deployment>/chat/completions?api-version=...`
- Azure OpenAI Responses API routes:
	- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.com/openai/responses?api-version=...`
	- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.us/openai/responses?api-version=...`
	- `https://<resource>.cognitiveservices.azure.us/openai/responses?api-version=...`
- Foundry endpoint hosts documented for Azure resources:
	- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.com`
	- `https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com`
	- `https://<resource>.cognitiveservices.azure.com`

Users should not need different BYOK behavior for equivalent Azure OpenAI resource hostnames across Azure clouds. Similarly, explicit Foundry/Azure endpoint URLs should use Azure's `api-key` header rather than being treated as generic OpenAI-compatible bearer-token endpoints.

## Recommended Fix

Update Azure BYOK URL handling to recognize Azure OpenAI endpoint host families more consistently.

Recommended behavior:

- Treat Azure OpenAI resource hosts across Azure clouds as recognized Azure hosts:
	- `*.openai.azure.*`
- Treat Foundry/Azure AI endpoint hosts as Azure API-key endpoints:
	- `*.cognitiveservices.azure.*`
	- `*.services.ai.azure.*`
- Preserve explicit API paths unchanged, including:
	- `/openai/responses`
	- `/openai/deployments/.../chat/completions`
- For legacy Azure OpenAI base URLs, continue expanding to the deployment-based Chat Completions route for compatibility.

The API-key header logic should treat Azure OpenAI and Foundry endpoint host families as Azure API-key endpoints. Today URLs containing `openai.azure` get the `api-key` header, but Foundry hosts such as `cognitiveservices.azure.*` and `services.ai.azure.*` may otherwise be treated as generic OpenAI-compatible endpoints and receive `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

Also add tests for:

- `https://<resource>.openai.azure.us` expands to the deployment Chat Completions URL.
- Explicit Gov `/openai/responses?...` URLs pass through unchanged.
- Explicit Gov `/openai/deployments/.../chat/completions?...` URLs pass through unchanged.
- API-key requests use the `api-key` header for `*.openai.azure.*`, `*.cognitiveservices.azure.*`, and `*.services.ai.azure.*` endpoint hosts.

title: Backgrounded VSCode window throttles integrated terminal and Remote-SSH processes on Windows
body:
Type: Bug ’╗┐## Problem Description After updating VSCode to a newer version, terminal processes become extremely slow whenever the VSCode window loses focus or is minimized on Windows. This issue affects both: * local integrated terminal processes * Remote-SSH sessions running on remote Linux servers The slowdown is severe enough that remote CPU utilization drops dramatically when the local VSCode window is placed in the background. Once VSCode returns to the foreground, execution speed and remote CPU utilization immediately recover. This behavior does NOT occur on macOS. ’╗┐ ## Key Observation Programs with continuous terminal output or actively updating progress bars (such as tqdm in deep learning training scripts) are mostly unaffected. However, programs with minimal terminal output become extremely slow when VSCode is unfocused. This strongly suggests the issue may be related to: * terminal/PTTY update handling * stdout/stderr buffering * Electron background throttling * xterm.js refresh scheduling * Remote-SSH terminal event handling ’╗┐ ## Steps to Reproduce ### Local Terminal 1. Open VSCode on Windows 2. Start a long-running CPU-intensive process in the integrated terminal 3. Switch focus to another application or minimize VSCode 4. Observe significant slowdown 5. Bring VSCode back to foreground 6. Execution speed immediately recovers ### Remote-SSH 1. Connect to a remote Linux server using Remote-SSH 2. Start a CPU-intensive task remotely 3. Monitor remote CPU utilization using htop 4. Minimize VSCode or switch focus away 5. Observe remote CPU utilization dropping significantly 6. Return VSCode to foreground 7. Remote CPU utilization immediately returns to normal ’╗┐ ## Expected Behavior Terminal processes should continue executing at normal speed regardless of whether the VSCode window is focused. Remote server computation should not depend on local VSCode window focus state. ’╗┐ ## Actual Behavior ### When VSCode is unfocused or minimized on Windows: * integrated terminal processes slow down dramatically * Remote-SSH tasks also slow down * remote CPU utilization drops significantly * execution speed becomes extremely slow ### When VSCode regains focus: * execution speed immediately recovers * remote CPU utilization returns to normal ’╗┐ ## Additional Notes * The issue started after updating VSCode * macOS does not reproduce the problem * Continuous terminal output (e.g. tqdm progress bars) significantly reduces or avoids the issue * The problem appears strongly correlated with terminal output activity VS Code version: Code 1.120.0 (0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, 2026-05-12T20:17:22Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes: Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.17.0-29-generic Remote OS version: Linux x64 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2

title: VS Code Installer Option Add "Open with Code" action to Windows Explorer directory context menu failed
body: Type: Bug In the VS Code installer, we have **Add "Open with Code" action to Windows Explorer directory context menu** option, it is attached to the **addcontextmenufolders** Inno Setup Task. But this option doesn't work recently. I looked into it. In the older version of VS Code installer (I have VSCodeUserSetup-x64-1.87.2.exe file), the installer will process this option. Like > Root: HKCU; Subkey: "Software\Classes\directory\shell\VSCode"; ValueType: ExpandSZ; ValueData: "{cm:OpenWithCodeContextMenu,Code}"; Tasks: addcontextmenufolders; Check: "not (IsWindows11OrLater and QualityIsInsiders)"; Flags: uninsdeletekey But in the latest version of VS Code installer (I got VSCodeUserSetup-x64-1.120.0.exe file), the installer ignored this option, and created a new condition called **ShouldInstallLegacyFolderContextMenu** > Root: HKCU; Subkey: "Software\Classes\directory\shell\VSCode"; ValueType: ExpandSZ; ValueData: "{cm:OpenWithCodeContextMenu,Code}"; Check: "ShouldInstallLegacyFolderContextMenu"; Flags: uninsdeletekey I am not sure whether I understood this correctly or not and I also don't know what is the **ShouldInstallLegacyFolderContextMenu** flag. But now, it doesn't work on my Windows 11 laptop. If I checked **Add "Open with Code" action to Windows Explorer directory context menu** but not check **Add "Open with Code" action to Windows Explorer file context menu** in the installer, it will not create the **directory context menu**. Please check the installer script. VS Code version: Code 1.120.0 (0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, 2026-05-12T20:17:22Z) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Right-click in an unfocused window opens the context menu over the focused window
body:
When VS Code has multiple windows open (main plus one or more auxiliary windows), right-clicking inside the unfocused window opens the context menu over the focused window instead of inside the window where the click actually happened.

This reproduces in both directions: clicking from main into an unfocused auxiliary window, or clicking from an auxiliary window into an unfocused main window. This affects multiple UI areas (webviews, activity bar header items, etc.) and both context menu styles, the custom  menu and the native OS context menu.

## Video

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c0af7f8-d45c-44ce-8aad-938efc7d9468

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open VS Code and a Markdown file. Run `Markdown: Open Preview` (or any command that opens a webview).
2. Run `View: Move Editor into New Window` to put the preview into an auxiliary window.
3. Click into the main window to focus it.
4. Right-click inside the Markdown preview content in the auxiliary window ŌĆö without clicking to focus it first.

## Expected

Context menu opens in the auxiliary window, anchored at the click.

## Actual

Context menu opens in the main (focused) window.

## Version
VS Code version: Code 1.120.0 (Universal) (0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, 2026-05-12T20:17:22Z)
OS version: macOS 26.5 (Darwin arm64 25.5.0)

title: Agent window: when starting a new session via worktree+branch, the remote branch is used, not the local branch
body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.48.0
- VS Code Version: 1.120.0
- OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26100
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Agent window
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Claude Sonnet 4.6


Steps to Reproduce:

1. Make sure branch (`main`) is up to date with remote and open VSCode to this folder.
2. Open Agents window.
3. Start a session with "worktree" mode targeting `main` with "Copilot CLI".
4. Finish work in the session, commit and merge.
5. Confirm changes are locally in `main` in the VSCode window from 1, but not pushed to remote.
6. Start a new session with "worktree" mode targeting `main` with "Copilot CLI".
7. Type something so the worktree gets set up
8. Open terminal and run `git log`.

Actual: the last commit is the code from 1
Expected:  the last commit is the code from 5

After I've merged the code from a session back to my branch, I expect that starting a new session from that branch will then use the _local_ version of that branch, not the _remote_.  If I push my local to remote, then I get the code from 5 when I do steps 6-8.  I could understand that maybe there are scenarios where I would want to use the remote version (and would have to if I chose "Cloud" as the location), but when I'm otherwise working completely locally, it seems wrong to use `origin/main` instead of `main` as the start of the worktree, especially without some option to choose between local vs. remote where I pick the branch.

title: [Agents App] Changes view categories are confusing ŌĆö "Branch Changes" vs "All Changes" vs "Uncommitted Changes"
body:
<!-- ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ŌÜĀ’ĖÅŌÜĀ’ĖÅ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
**Problem**

The three change categories in the changes view are redundant and confusing to users. Participants couldn't distinguish between them or understand when to use each.

**Suggestion**

Simplify the change categories. Consider a single default view with optional filters, rather than three separate categories with unclear distinctions.

**Evidence**

- **P0423A:** *"One of them has to go. It's confusing... Whether I say all changes or branch changesŌĆ” since we're on the branch, then it should be branch changes. The 'all' was making me feel like the two branches."*
- **P0423B** wanted a unified view across worktrees: *"For the folderŌĆ” the main branchŌĆ” It would be nice to see the changes for the entire thingŌĆ” so I can review everything."*

> This issue is based on evidence from the [Agents Worktree Usability Study](https://github.com/coreai-microsoft/caidr/issues/1038).
